text: The free version is terrible. Bro you can only do 6 skips per hour and then it gives you a whole bunch of recommendations into the playlists you're listening to! like bro when I make a playlist I like it how it is I don't need some robot to recommend things for it! like seriously! I pay for premium now, because it's worth it, but barely. The paid version is excellent but very expensive.
author: Catherine Hempel

text: free version sucks. Windows media player is better by light years (and WMP isn't an amazing platform, either). premium version works well enough to play music from, but is very confusing in how song ratings work. audiobooks are severely limited (15 hours with premium, and 15 hours more on a separate subscription - per month). if you want audiobooks or free music, I highly recommend a different platform.
author: Nevada ZG

text: I love music, it's the only thing that keeps me focused on things like drawing, love Spotify, I COULD listen outside of the app and for the last few months of what I expected was just a bug that now constantly happens whenever I leave the app to go to anything else, it closes and turns my music off, back when I'd have said five stars, maybe lower the price instead of increasing an extra dollar every year for premium, and FIVE ads? Thats unnecessary, two or three is fine, but five hits my anger.
author: Angel Mead (Willow)

text: I already pay for premium but that only allows for 15 hours of audiobooks and now they want me to pay for an add on that's the same price as the monthly fee for only 15 hours more. For an extra 11.99 a month audiobook add on it should be unlimited. not to mention the constant price hikes and still having ads in my podcast when I pay for no ads. This will just be the next streaming service I cancelle
author: Brandy R

text: Edit 2/27/26: Something seems to have changed in the last few weeks. I listen a lot, probably 4-5 hours a day while I do renovation work and every time I open the app it shows a "you are back online" message and it takes 2-3 minutes to load up all the recommendations and personalization stuff. Most of the time I am connected to high quality Wi-Fi or 5G networks. it's definitely not a connection issue since it happens everywhere I go. It's a music app so it's not critical, but still.
author: Ryan Yokel

text: Personally, I don't understand why you have to double down on the free version of the app with a bunch of ads AND limited song choices. That's overkill and it makes this app hardly even usable for longer than 20 minutes before it devolves into music that's vaguely related to the stuff you chose to listen to. It's great when you spend money, but that's by design. Y'all make enough money to give us more than 6 skips... I mean, c'mon.
author: Deagan Bailey

text: Shuffle does not actually shuffle. 'Shuffle' is just Spotify's version of a playlist. If I add a song to the queue, it'll play the song but will not change the shuffle, so that song could be played right away again if it was in that order of the 'shuffle'. Super annoying, especially considering the monthly price keeps going up, but the issue doesn't get resolved! Also, my app keeps skipping! Makes me feel like I'm back in the 90's and I need to take the CD out and wipe it off! it's not my settin
author: Alissa Troidl

text: I love this app for the most part. I have premium and have ever since switching from Pandora, so I don't even fully remember the distinction between the free and paid subscriptions at this point, however it is frustrating when I am listening to a podcast with video and all of a sudden, it just shows the profile of the podcaster rather than the video. I remember having a method to fix this in the settings, but it's been too long since then to even remember what it was. Please fix this!!
author: Arcadia Lamming

text: Free version is almost unusable. Good grief! 4 ads in a row then it tells me I can enjoy 30 minutes ad free. 2 songs later here are 4 more ads. At first I figured maybe a weird glitch. Nope. It's happens several times over the last few days. And on top of the that the ads are horrible. They're louder than your music so it's very jarring especially if you're listening to something chill, and they're repetitive. I can't listen to specific songs, everything is behind a pay wall now.
author: jolene

text: Spotify used to be incredible! Now, it's total trash! The connectivity is asteonomcally garbage - I could have 5G UC service, be playing downloaded songs, but Spotify will be constantly interrupted. I'm currently experiencing a complete blackout. I loved the way the queue was set-up, where you could see what was in the queue, & what was playing from the list - it was a large, legible set of tracks; now, it's a "user-friendly" mess, where it's impossible to see the queue, versus the list.
author: AL

text: there are multiple things wrong with it, though it does have a lot of music it's hard to get to that music you want because of how it's set up. When I try to get a song it plays a different song from "things we added" which is really just a way to get money so you get so annoyed that you pay for premium. It plays other songs before it plays your song and it only lets you skip five times, it also adds things to your own playlist when you try to create one. I won't be using this app anymore.
author: Courtney Richey

text: The app is driving me crazy. For one, there's an oversaturation of ads -- roughly 2-3 minutes of ads per 3-5 minutes of music. And the ads where it's like "Watch this ad to get 30 minutes of ad-free listening" don't work at all; I just get more normal ads within 5-10 minutes. I'm not buying a membership, Spotify, and your bugs, intentional or not, aren't gonna force me to. Edit: Looked at community page. Solution was to keep the app in focus while the video plays. Problem: I ALREADY HAVE BEEN!
author: Zac Broderick

text: I have the free version right now because I can not afford premium. I will get the ad saying "listen for 30 minutes of ad free music" then it literally plays 1 3-4 minute song before I get another 2 minute set of ads. I habe time stamped screen shots to prove this. At this point, I feel as though im listening to more ads than I am music and highly considering switching streaming services. We're getting to a point where I almost can't listen to music unless I pay. Fix it!
author: Tabetha Baranski

text: Great app! The only thing I hate about this app is that it literally does NOT give us the bare minimum!? For example, being able to loop a song. I get the ad part, I don't mind it that much actually– but why not give free users the loop button? We already deal with 6 skips an hour, 2-4 ads after 2 songs, and not being able to play a playlist in order. Plus, the individual premium is 12 dollars a month, probably not bad for others, but I wouldn't buy it for a music app. Updated: Not much changed.
author: Inez Tela (Nezz)

text: This company has just the worst software. It is slow to load and not very responsive. I click to play a different song and it takes 5 seconds to switch songs, if it does it at all. Constantly disconnects from connected devices. I move a song in the playlist and it bounces around, sometimes returning back to original position. The AI DJ just plays the same songs over and over and the voice is obnoxious. Some of these bugs have been in the software for nearly 10 years and still not fixed.
author: Matthew Koenn

text: I love Spotify and have for years, use it almost every single day. So when they added the option to listen to audio books with the premium subscription I was very excited, however, who ever decided to put a limit on how many chapters you can listen to and then decided you can buy more chapters for the same price as I pay for my premium subscription deserves to get fired. That is the stupidest thing I've ever seen. Do better Spotify.
author: Ron Middleton

text: I usually love Spotify, I even have premium, but recently it has been SO testy when I try to open it. Sometimes it will work fine, but most of the time it just opens to a black screen and no matter if I reset the app, or even my phone, nothing really helps it. When it does this, if it eventually opens, everything is incredibly slow to load. I'm very disappointed.
author: Sam Hatcher

text: I love having the music. I use spotify every day. Three stars because the app itself is absolute trash. It is constantly crashing, freezing, and randomly stopping. EDIT 03/22: don't know what they did but they made it worse than ever. Spotify is trash, yall. Edit 08/24: Dear Spotify. Pay artists more. Also do something about the various versions of songs as it relates to the favorites list. Maybe like suggest the other versions when you add the song to a playlist or something.
author: Kacey Mayes

text: I had to edit my review, it used to be 5 stars but I'm lowering it because all of the sudden, every now and then, it'll switch what Im listening to. I've been listening to a podcast's library and it switched me to a curated list. If you want to make me a recommended list, fine but I don't HAVE to listen to that other stuff and this app is making it so I have to. Super inconvenient, especially while driving. I've used this app for years and never had any issues until recently.
author: HeatherLee S

text: I LOVE Spotify, and as a user since circa 2013, I like to think I'm think I'm an early adopter and have a lot of experience with the app. That said, the last 6 months it's gone drastically downhill. The app constantly closes with no crash report, it just stops playing and it closes. Sometimes it's playing and the app is not even showing as open, which is fun. Sometimes, it's choppy and that may be a service issue but it's only been happening the last few months. Not sure what's going on!
author: Bridget Gunden

text: Love the app! Good music variety. Lyrics are pretty in sync with every song. This also has a wide variety of podcasts. Edited: This kinda sucks. I was using it for a month, and I was able to skip any song, pick what song I wanted to listen to, and listen offline. I wasn't even paying for premium. Now I wake up, and it all changes. I can't skip as much as I could, and I can't pick out a specific song. AND THERE ARE SO MANY ADDS. GOOD GOD. The fact that I can't skip as much is ridiculous.👎
author: Kameron Fields

text: App has become horribly unstable. The app crashes on me every other song, sometimes I get two songs in-between. I have reinstalled the app, cleared the cache, undownloaded and redownloaded all saved playlist. But the app still crashes constantly. Not worth the membership cost anymore, given that I can't enjoy my music. I hope it gets fixed with the next update, but considering the issue wasn't fixed after the last time I brought this issue up, I'm not hopeful.
author: Taylor Dixson

text: TOOK AWAY THE LAST GOOD THING ABOUT SPOTIFY, the queue!! Used to be able to click queue and select random songs from library in the queue to play next, now you can't, have to individually drag each song to the top. Absolute trash. EVERY single update Spotify has done for the last 5 years has made the app worse and worse. Used to be the best, can't get my 7000 songs off the damn app without starting completely over on something else, no transfer apps work. So effing mad.
author: keith saylor

text: When using the Free version of this app, searching and playing a song is broken. For example, if I search and play "Billie Jean" by MJ, it will play "Beat it" or "Thriller". Earlier versions of spotify did not do this and it makes this app one of the most unreliable apps for Free music playing - along with your procured playlists on permenant shuffle. You'd be better off with competitor products. WOULD NOT RECOMMEND - unless you have over 100 dollars a year to blow on premium.
author: Pyroshock

text: I am so tired of this app. It gets more and more expensive. I had premium and it advertises audiobooks with your subscription, but only allows so many hours before it wants more money. cancelled premium bc I only listen to podcasts that have ads built in, forgot my alarm was set to my spotify Playlist. instead of a song, I woke up to 6 consecutive ads I had to listen to to get back to my podcast, to listen to more ads. Total garbage.
author: Angela Bottorff

text: Unless you're going to pay for premium, don't bother. You used to get 30 minutes add free, but no longer. Oh, they still say to enjoy the next 30 minutes add free. After ONE song, you're stuck listening to several ads that run longer than the song you just heard. I thought it might be a bug, but it happens consistently now.
author: Donovin Harvey

text: At this point, just make Spotify a paid app cause instead of two ads, it's three, sometimes even FOUR. says 30 mins without ads, but it's like 5 mins, adds random songs in my Playlist, can only skip 6 songs each hour, and can't see what song is up next. a little update!! I love listening to music!! but I got 5 ads in a row. And I am not paying for Spotify premium.
author: Rhaleesi M

text: Spotify has become top tier grabage these days. Phone and desktop doesn't sync reliably, can't remember what device it was just streaming to a second ago, doesn't play songs when they're clicked on, plays song when you don't click them but scroll past, their "help" is just full of regurgitated nonsense and they put zero effort into improvement. They corned the market and then went cut corners to make more money. what a surprise.
author: Anthony

text: I've been using Spotify for almost 10 years. I've noticed that the app no longer shuffles my playlits, so the same songs keep playing over and over again. I'm on the premium plan, yet I'm still getting ads on podcasts and I don't have access to audio books. There are some podcasts that I used to listen to almost daily that have now become locked. What's the point of paying for a premium subscription if I don't gain access to everything the app offers?
author: Effie

text: I really think Spot is the best music app there is. However, paying for a premium for AD FREE LISTENING and getting ADS during a podcast is pretty stupid. Because you get like 5 ads that are like 2 or more minutes long. At that point, it's better to just watch YouTube so you can skip the ads for free instead of being forced to watch ads while actively PAYING for NO ADS. That is so annoying. I considered 1 or 2 stars if Spotify wasn't otherwise genuinely good. At least as far as music goes.
author: Kura Yami

text: Great navigation, love the system and format, especially the playlist making system. It's just SO MANY ads, and that's not something I usually complain about but seriously? After every other song? The thirty minute ad breaks don't even work, it's totally immersion breaking, especially getting the same ads over and over and the singing ones are just so annoying. Quit money grabbing, not everyone has money to throw away. Sometimes it just skips songs on its own and it's janky on Aux.
author: Analeigh Galindo

text: Good interface, but... It'd be way better if Spotify on mobile devices was like Spotify on computers. Just look at how much more freedom you get on a computer when you listen to music in comparison with how limited the mobile version is! I don't know why it is the way it is (most likely marketing, but what isn't nowadays?)... Anyway, not really that good of an experience if you have Spotify Free but I still use it for some reason :/
author: Teacup Appleblood

text: Always loved this app. I pay for premium but you still add commercials even though I pay to not have commercials. Granted they can be skipped, but I don't EVEN WANT THEM THATS THE POINT OF PAYING NOT TO HAVE THEM, having the ability to skip is pointless when I have to pick up my phone a million times during a podcast to skip the stupid BOSE headphones commercials. Per Spotify they told me to contact customer support, which I have, and they've never responded or fixed it.
author: Courtney

text: Plays 1 song at a time. No longer able to listen to playlists or stations. It stops after 1 song. I've cleared cache. Restarted my phone. Uninstalled and reinstalled. Even cleared storage and Uninstalled other apps. Nothing works. I stopped my subscription due to similar issues and now this free version is no longer worth the work or time. I'm done.
author: Avalia Leyva

text: I very much like this app and have loved organizing all my songs and playlists. I'm a musician, so I have dozens of playlists. Overall great app, but please bring back the hearts! I don't know why it ever needed to get changed, but I way much more prefered the heart symbol to like a song over the little plus sign. It's now much more complicated than it needed to be.
author: Michaela Henderson

text: Tbh I'm constantly having issues with the app. It never remembers what podcast episode I last listened to so I have to scroll forever everytime I listen to something else. It freezes a lot or takes forever to start playing. Tons of issues in Android auto with it. Freezing and just not playing, or playing but no sound is coming out. The widget almost never seems to work and I used to use it all the time. Samsung 24 Ultra. Also I'd like to be able to message my friends songs right in the app plz
author: Burn The Blue Skies

text: The apps performance really has degraded recently. Crashing every thirty or so minutes, randomly pausing songs, or just straight up dropping the playlist after it crashes and starts playing "trending" songs. I can't listen to custom mixes anymore because it crashes and kills the playlist so I have to restart it. The advertisement vs song ratio has become has become a joke, I can listen to FM radio stations and get less ads.
author: Joseph

text: Honestly, when i first started using this, it was an awesome app! It's got all my favorite artists in one place, but, after a while, on my phone, it just doesn't work(?) It will not play the next song, and it will get stuck on an ad, which i can't skip since I have to close the app. But overall, it's a great app, aside from the fact that you only have limited lyrics per month and six skins per hour, I love it! It's great for people who have money to spend on premium. (Aside from me, I dont)
author: Kimber Mcgahan

text: I've used this platform since 2011, and it seems to get worse every year. I open another app(non-media related) and my queue disappears and stops playing the current song. It's as if Iuninstalled the app and reinstall it every time that happens. But if I close the app and reopen it, the music resumes. And when I open the app after a while, it lags. The team that works on this app need to do better. I'm really close to canceling premium and switching to an app that actually works properly.
author: Eldon

text: Unreliable app. I have my music downloaded so I can use when there is no service, but Spotify app freezes SO often. This is a recent issue, so hopefully I can get that fixed. There's also poor syncing across devices, making me have to delete EVERYTHING and redownload to see all of my liked songs again. pst, why is there a single button at the top of the library that can instantly delete all downloaded songs with no window asking if I meant to hit it?🙃
author: Daniela Schaal

text: Used to have a 2 star rating for the app in 2023, and major issues have been updated, but i still continue to have some issues with songs randomly pausing and not playing. The ads are also insane. 3 to 4 30 second ads in a row after only playing a few songs is very irritating to say the least. I don't have the money to be spending $12/mo for premium so it is what it is, but it gets annoying. Otherwise, app is pretty good for listening to music whenever, driving, walking, etc.
author: Christina Porter

text: I absolutely love Spotify and have since 2008. I always pay for premium because I can't stand commercials. Reason for the 4 star rating is due to a reoccurring issue causing me to reinstall the app to fix it. Eventually i can only play 1 genre of music from my library. doesn't matter if I make a shuffle all playlist or select a specific song from different genre. it only affects my downloaded/liked songs. It could be 4 months or a year, it always seems to happen. It's very annoying.
author: Chris Kealiher

text: I am not a fan of the results that kick out anytime I go to a song's "Go to song radio". When I want to go to the radio of a particular song, I expect similar songs that align with the song tempo/genre. However, this option just shuffles the latest music that has been released. I'm not sure if that's what is intended with this option, however my interpretation is that it should be playing similar music styles. If I wanted to listen to new releases, I would just go to my release radar.
author: kristina249

text: Great streaming app. 2 things I wish they would add, one, a "dislike" button to compliment the heart. Hiding songs isn't the same. Makes playlists shorter by just not playing a song, and doesn't solve the problem of a not liked song showing up on playlists. It just gets grayed out and skipped. Let me dislike the song and just don't waste a spot on my Playlist with it. Secondly, the my DJ thing would be so much better if it didn't consistently cut off 30 seconds to a minute of most songs
author: Matthew Poworoznek

text: I used to use spotify alot and I thought it was the best music app, but that was only because I was paying for premium, without premium you can't even skip or pick any of the music, you can only use shuffle or play it the playlist regularly but with my playlist I have a bunch of the same people over and over and that gets annoying, plus the app usually closes when I try to use it in the background then it gives me three 30 second ads after it just told me I get 30 minutes of uninterrupted music.
author: Aiden “River Carver” Smith

text: I've had Spotify premium for years. It's great to be able to select specific songs and listen without ads and they do have a really wide selection. I used to love their recs but the algorithms suck now. Harder to find new music bc they just keep pushing the same old. I also wish they would bring back the curated running playlist mix (your likes, mixed, w specific bpm).
author: Alexandra Howland-Lopez

text: after a while, you have to buy a premium subscription so you can go back to a song, and you also can't replay the song after a while it's just really annoying so I really hope this can be fixed but if it doesn't then I will most likely delete the app. Even though I have worked on it very hard... But also I'll be playing a game like Dandys World or smth, and i will get silence from the app and i will be listening to a song, and then it will just stop randomly playing the song! So please fix this.
author: Faith Webb

text: Super interesting that Spotify said in-app that they were asking for "bluetooth" permission when what was REALLY requested was permission to locate/connect with nearby devices. (I was listening to music using bluetooth while this was happening, so it wasn't because my BT was off.) Why do you want that, Spotify? Couldn't be anything good if you have to trick people into granting you access. I'm switching to Tidal. So tired of this bull.
author: Katie House

text: Generally I love Spotify over most other streaming apps. I just started using it for audio books as well since some are included with premium. It's absolutely wild that you "run out of hours" to listen to a book, especially when those limited hours are less than it takes to get through a book. Why offer audiobooks with premium at all if you're going to charge more to keep listening to a book that's "included" with premium?
author: Cyndi Hagan

text: "Enjoy the next 30 minutes commercial free." 1 song plays, then 3 commercials, literally 4 minutes after the message. Lies. Another thing? I thumb down an ad, because I'm married 20 years and don't use dating apps, or no kids and every third ad is for Capri Sun or school supplies. Your targeted ads suck. The feedback form doesn't fix it; thumb down/does not apply/etc: it doesn't work. 1/3 off my ads are for Kamala, and every one pushes me farther to the right.
author: Daniel Pierce

text: Doesn't play well with Google Pixel 8. Stops connecting to my cars Bluetooth until I restart my phone (won't play any audio). Music stutters while playing. "Upstream request" timeouts when I try to access their website. Player won't work on desktop. Wrapped won't play. I restart the app. I install updates. I check settings. I try different browsers. Terrible user experience with Spotify this year. [Edit] I appreciate the response. I have tried reinstalling. I can't reach your site, but will try
author: Karolina

text: I used to really like Spotify because it was extremely simple and had a large library. It hasn't changed too much, but the problem is that over the years this app has started to work less and less. In the modern day Spotify feels barely functional: Constantly crashing, extremely slow, skipping large portions of playlists, a broken shuffle feature, and buggy bluetooth drivers. I just need an app that plays music and actually works.
author: ryan colon (ryodude101)

text: I've been loyal to Spotify for over a decade. But it is not the same platform it used to be. It is impossible to discover small artists. My recommendations are clogged with popular sponsored artists, even ones I've BLOCKED. All the cool features and staff curated playlists are soulless AI garbage now that Spotify has laid off hundreds of employees. Adding songs to a playlist (my favorite feature) frequently crashes my phone. Premium rates rise. If I find a good alternative, I will switch.
author: A Google user

text: The shuffle doesn't really shuffle your music, it just plays the songs you've been listening to recently. The discover weekly sucks, its not at all tailored to my taste, it NEVER has the genres I listen to and ive only found a handfull of songs I actually like from it. The offline option is very iffy, sometimes it'll load but a lot of times it won't and tell me I'm offline. Well duh, thats why I'm in offline mode!! I like this app but would love it if things were improved.
author: Jade Kathleen

text: Updated review after TRYING to listen to audio books. you get 15 hours even with premium and have to pay a ridiculous amount of 13$ per 10hours. I took that 13$ to audible and finished the book I was listening to and got even better deals to continue the series. I spent 6 dollars for the 2nd book in the series that was over 20hrs long. I would have paid 4 times that to listen to it on spotify. I suggest either update the business model or get rid of it completely before you lose business.
author: gary johnson

text: App isn't great. Bad UI, glitchy additions of songs to playlists, discrepancies between data of the web client and mobile, and often recommends music I dislike in service to some unknowable algorithm. The ceo investing in the military industrial complex's use of ai makes me plan on dropping it entirely. Spotify's main draw is its collection, which I'm sure will migrate elsewhere at some point
author: Conlan Parkman

text: Edit: must be nice to make millions every month off an app that don't work. Wth is happening to this app I PAY for?! Every single time I open it, it just goes dumb and my data stops working for up to an entire minute. It's extremely laggy skipping through songs and does this weird thing where it goes back a previous song and then forward to your next song. Everything is updated. These problems shouldn't even exist today. So quit being lazy and fix your mess. Canceling subscription until then.
author: Brion Burroughs

text: Would love to give more stars. But, I found that when you're broke for a month & can't afford premium, the quality of the app decreases heavily. For example: I got my music ready for a shower and listened to an ad that told me to enjoy 30 minutes of AD FREE music. Guess what happened after one song while I was in the shower..? THREE MORE ADS LMAO. I lowkey understand the hate Spotify gets now. These ads straight up lie if you aren't paying Spotify. Do better. Sincerely, a 10+ year Spotify user
author: bridget burton

text: One problem: I have a shared playlist with a friend. It's very long, over 8 hours. Recently, some songs will just disappear from the playlist without either my friend or I touching them. I'll see songs I've added to the playlist a while ago in the recommended songs at the bottom of the playlist. I'll add it back. Its happened one or twice where the same song will disappear after being added back. We both have premium if that makes a difference.
author: Miranda McKinley

text: I don't have premium but I get alot of ads, I get 2-4 adds after one song. I get the same ads over and over. But it's still a great app for listening to music on, there's plenty of podcasts to listen to, you can even make your own playlists and add any song to it! Still an amazing app! I changed my mind a bit, recently I've been getting 5 ads after one song I've uninstalled and reinstalled the app and it's not helping I've been trying to listen to a certain song and can't due to skips.
author: Ella Marie Burnham

text: I've been using this app for several years now (paying for ad-free, too), however it has become more of a hassle than anything recently. It plays a song or two, then pauses altogether rather than moving on to the next song in the queue. I contacted customer support for assistance, and all they could advise is that I uninstall and reinstall it, which works for a period of time before the error comes back. The only thing keeping me on Spotify has been my playlists, but that may not be enough now.
author: Katherine Noury

text: VERY annoying, in my opinion. You can't do a lot of stuff without premium, which is the main thing. You have a limit on skips, lyric views, and even personal song picks. Ads tend to pop up very frequently, and as if that wasn't annoying enough, you have to listen to 5 FULL ads straight, just when you think the app can't get worse it somehow does it some different way.
author: lily

text: Free podcasts are cool, that's one of the only upsides though. Theres a new issue every month. The episodes are constantly glitching and restarting, after I've finished an episode,it will restart, and when a playlist I've downloaded ends, why would you try to play music I don't have downloaded? I just end up listening to nothing for the rest of my shift. The subscription is always going up while there are issues that just keep ruining the app for me. Not worth it at all.
author: Brandie Earl

text: Spotify is honestly one of those apps that just becomes part of your daily routine without you even noticing. It’s super easy to use, and it somehow always knows what you want to hear—like the playlists and recommendations feel really personal, not random. What I like most is that it doesn’t feel limited. You can switch from music to podcasts to something new you’ve never heard before, and it all just flows naturally. It’s simple, but it doesn’t feel basic.
author: wilber vasquez

text: Actually a great app with relatively few adds and lots of great music for being the 'free' version. The more I use it, the more I enjoy it. Its my go-to for my music when I'm trying to get things done and although there are adds, i can listen right through them without having to acknowledge that "I'm still listening!" That, and you can actually hit the power button so you're not accidentally calling Singapore! And, that's with the free-version!
author: Jim Mastin

text: Premium payer. It's my favorite music/podcast app. I've only dabbled in the audio books but its nice to have those available. Some tech issues from time to time but usually fixed within a few hours. (edited) Thanks for the response developer. I did restart my phone and uninstall from scratch, still didn't work this morning. Did nothing else except walk away from the phone for a few hours and now it works as it should without changing anything.
author: Dennis Pinsonneault

text: Been using Spotify for more than a decade now I think, it is a good app, great way to listen to music. I have been a premium member for most of the time I've had it. You can manage with the free version, but having whatever song you want on demand at all times is really handy, additionally being able to download songs for offline use while going camping or through mountains/flying is nice as well. It would be 5 star but I took a star after the post pandemic layoffs, left a bad taste in my mouth.
author: Reyn Matter

text: I love Spotify until 06/20/26. I did an update, and now my app doesn't open!!! I use Spotify on a daily basis for work, and I will not pay for something that does not work!!!( Yes, uninstalled,reinstalled your app) that's just a temporary fix!!!! I've spent years building my music library and playlist. please fix this issue, I do not want to have to find a new music streaming service.😞 (Thank you for the quick response, the clean reinstall seems to have worked.)🙂
author: Darrel Lawler

text: Stupid buggy, with nowhere to report bugs. Offline mode doesn't work MOST of the time. So if I have no signal I better hope I'm ALREADY listening to something, because I won't be able to access any of the other playlists, books, or podcasts that I have downloaded SPECIFICALLY FOR offline usage. The new (and now only) widget doesn't work half the time. The media playback "notification" also doesn't work half the time, and I have to specifically go into the app to pause or skip a song.
author: McKendra Garrison

text: Better than Pandora, but getting popups for things I'm not interested in when I pay for premium... is getting old VERY quickly. I also don't care about audio books... I came here for music why hit me with a pop-up about books? The algorithm has really been missing the mark as well lately. Rarely playing new music, repeating the same songs, or when I finally find a playlist I like... it changes. Really if the popups continue I'll stop paying and just listen to my own music on another app.
author: J E

text: Sort of works, 4/5 days it seems fine. Other days it wants to recommend the same 2 podcasts that I have no interest in and it won't let me remove them from recommended, or my taste profile (that I've searched for and can't find). The only thing I can do is leave a bad review to a podcast that is fine, just not for me. The rest of the day it does this play/pause thing that I have to remove the app just to play anything again, I've never shared my login. Has the music my kid wants to listen to.
author: Calvin Ikenouye

text: I wish I could leave negative stars. I've used Spotify for 13 years, and I have seen the platform completely disintegrate over that time. All complaints that have been left here by others are the same ones I have, but as a bonus, as of today I can only listen to one single song between ad rolls. Just one. Even a song under three minutes long. Spotify free is completely unusable. It is just a continuous slew of ads with a single song thrown in every once in awhile. It's time to move on.
author: A.B.

text: First, FIRST. Shuffle doesn't work which is like a whole thing that I don't even know how to go about. But second, the ads. As someone who uses the free version, the ratio between song and ad is 4:4 which is equal, yea expect when you realize how long the ads are. Third, you only get 6 skips a day. As someone who listens to Spotify everywhere at every time, I promise some of my songs are not appropriate in some settings. Yes I can buy the monthly thing but still it doesn't make sense.
author: Ally

text: My app keeps closing and saying there is a bug 🐛 in the app that you are fixing. WHEN are you fixing it? I can't log out of my account without losing it! you've made it impossible to change the email address associated with my original account. I don't receive emails due to storage issues. I'm not paying to get an email from you. PLEASE ADVISE. I am on a premium Spotify account. PAID TO LISTEN. FIX IT, Please.
author: Vannie Frederici

text: great at first ... until you realize how much AI generated music there is. I loved the audiobook feature until you realize you aren't given enough time in one month to finish a book more then 10 hrs long. they offer "top ups" which cost more then the premium membership. ridiculous. the podcasts are great. but you can listen to those on multiple platforms. so the totality of service is more frustrating then enjoyable.
author: Opal

text: lots of bugs, turns off multiple times ! Please fix it during night, not in the mornings .I couldn't listen to anything on my drive to work it was a bummer .I never had issues with it before but now turns off all the time. And won't load or would be stuck on loading and freezes my whole phone. It was perfect before (if it's not broken don't fix it it ) Spotify you should live by that .Everyone would be happy even you .:)
author: Peony Princess

text: the free version is mid but Spotify does have a very wide range selection of songs which is amazing. though you can't do much with the free version. six skips per hour, no downloading songs(which is kinda valid tbh), and ads which isn't too surprising. premium is worth it depending on how much music you listen to. other than that, Spotify is pretty decent.
author: Lilyanna Rodriguez

text: I've been a Spotify premium member for years and honestly thinking about just canceling my subscription. The shuffle plays the same songs in the same order over and over. I've tried going into settings to fix it and it just won't stop. My playlist has almost 1.5k songs on it, there's absolutely no reason it should be playing the same stuff over and over again. Do better for charging people $15/month and not properly paying the artists that make the music.
author: Deacon Potter

text: I bought a large storage phone so I could download all my liked songs for offline listening. the app will still refuse to work, when online, when offline. it will just freeze. been a subscriber since 2014 but I have no idea why the more I seem to use the app, the worse it works. It will be the only app running on my phone and still not be able to play a song. very very disappointing. I have lots of music I love on Spotify and love the access it gives, but this is horrible
author: Lucas Meier

text: I find it incredibly stupid that theres only 6 song skips an hour and you need to pay for ads to go away. Spotify is big enough to not need all the ads it has. Why do you even need to limit it to 6 skips? theres no reason. Seems pretty hungry for money if you ask me. Not to mention the "30 minutes of ad free listening" which isn't true. I've read other reviews and spotify keeps saying they'll fix it but they arent. I get 2-3 minutes of ad free listening before another ad pops up.
author: Brooklyn

text: I'm officially exhausted with Spotify. What kind of music app doesn't let you play an album without including random songs then plays tracklist on shuffled?! Not to mention, how many ads there are. There's a limit to specify songs I can pick, I can't download songs, and you have to pay just for a trial beforehand if you are paying with Google Play. Not everyone can afford a 144 dollar subscription btw. Also I'm disgusted by how AI features have been forced on people. Bring back free music!!!!
author: Lyddie D

text: What's up with the songs that suddenly sound different? Add song to a playlist and after a couple weeks the song I've listened to a dozen times is suddenly a completely different sound. Same or similar lyrics, same melody, but a different tempo and composition, and suddenly the voice has a weird Transylvanian (?) accent? What is this? Please fix.
author: tertyree

text: Overall I like the app now that I am on premium. If the price raises any more I'll go back to radio though. They have most of the songs I like, but there is a growing library of of music I cannot listen to anymore. The new widget is also very limited. the old widget would allow me to activate an auto-generated Playlist then mark the songs I liked. the new widget still plays the Playlist but I have to open the app to mark the songs I like.
author: Gregory Dayley

text: I have been using Spotify for several years now and I have come to the realization that it is another victim of shitification. It was a beloved music player once, but now all it does is lag. It is incredibly buggy and doesn't work half the time. I have to restart the app over and over and over again just to get my liked songs, the songs that are downloaded to my phone, to load. I don't understand why I have to wait for downloaded songs to load if they are on my device. Pathetic
author: Sherry W

text: I has a terrible experience with this app. The free version is just horrendous and unbearable. you can only get 6 skips per hours then you the playback bar is unusable. That's not even the worse part. You can't use the back button as its a premium feature and the icing on the cake is that at some point like after clicking a few songs, they dont allow you to choose a song again until the next day which is so stupid. The app has just become money hungry and im transferring because it's ridiculous.
author: Erwinsmissingarm

text: I've used Spotify for over 10 years, and it just keeps getting better. The seamless experience across all my devices, with any device able to control playback, is unmatched. I also love that they've optimized the app for foldable phones; something so few developers do well. Whether I'm offline on a long flight or sharing a Jam with friends, Spotify is always my go-to for music.
author: Jeffrey Rothstein

text: too many ads, but there are a lot of songs to choose from, and you can have thousands of songs on one playlist. I do wish that when you create a new account, you can try 1-3 weeks of premium without it going to the purchase area, but other than that, it is a really good app with tons of different music styles, genres, types, artists, etc.
author: Caitlyn Chapin

text: Podcast videos no longer work when connected to car! It's so frustrating when my passenger wants to see the video but also has it through the cars Bluetooth! I understand it's probably a safety feature! But I don't need a parent! as an adult, if my passenger wants to watch the video podcast on the phone while also having it through the Bluetooth speakers, then it should be able to happen especially for long road trips! Why are you regulating us! It's despicable! It's freaking communist!
author: Tanner Michels

text: money hungry. constant ads after 2-3 songs. if I'm lucky then 4-5. half of those ads are to advertise Spotify premium, how desperate are you? when I'm using the app on my tv I get 5 ads at once. I would like to use another music app but this one has so many songs and playlists, it would be terrible to create those playlists and like songs all over again. I like how you can get 30 minutes without ads. and I'm glad that not everything is restricted for free users. but still that's the bare minimum
author: Zyazan Schmal

text: Every update seems to make the app crash. I end up having to uninstall and reinstall it just to get it working again. It's frustrating that this keeps happening. For how often Spotify pushes ads encouraging users to upgrade to Premium, you'd think the app would be more reliable. Please focus on fixing stability instead of constantly pushing subscriptions.
author: Amanda K

text: Why are there videos on this platform if it can't support them? Especially if you're going to charge more per month after forcing them into the app. It freezes my phone up every time I turn on a podcast that has video. I never want to watch them, video just comes up automatically and I have to spend the next 5 minutes trying to fix my phone. Good job.
author: Elyse

text: As of 6/17/2026, we are now forced to use the big widget instead of the smaller one. Changing my rating to a 1 star. Too many ads now, tells you here's a ½ hour of ad free play and plays an ad after the 1st or 2nd song. 80% of the time, when I skip a track, an ad comes on. Starting to be like all the other apps. Still not fixed as of 6/24/2025 & now we are forced to warch ads. can't search or anything. pauses as soon as you back out to search. Come on guys! This is getting more & more ridiculous
author: Timothy Eisenhart

text: I've used this app and paid for premium for years, it's the only subscription service I pay for except my phone bill. But in the last several days it has been crashing for doing basic functions like opening the search tab or the queue. Now it crashes almost as soon as I open it. I've cleared the cache, closed the app, and tried force stopping it but nothing works. It's currently unusable.
author: Alex Thomas

text: problem 1 after every single song 3 to 6 ads play and they are 30 to 60 seconds long and you can't skip them in app I understand that they have to make money somehow but with how many people buy premium I feel 6 30 second ads is just greedy. problem 2 a lot of features are locked or hard to access I know why this is it's to encourage people to buy premium but having a random song that is "similar to your Playlist" is not even close to what I want to listen to. otherwise a decent app for music.
author: That Train Guy

text: Used to be great. Something changed at some point. I'm paying for premium, have 3000+ songs saved, and their shuffle algorithm absolutely sucks. Plays the same few hundred songs(being generous) and in the same order. It's really not shuffle. Reached out a few times about it and got nowhere. I'm probably canceling soon.
author: Matt F

text: I'll be blunt. the new music widget absolutely sucks. the old one worked great. but this "new and improved" one... won't even open 80% of the time and when it does it opens to the complete wrong song or playlist that you were playing. it's quite disappointing. I used to use the widget daily. now I almost never use it. in fact it has decreased my Spotify usage.... better luck next time spotify
author: Mitchial Stones

text: Gets more expensive, but rarely better The bad captions are a huge problem, & I've heard the artists have no option to turn them off OR correct them This is an accessibility issue first, but also creators should have the option to correct them if the auto gen is wrong As for the audio book rip off? save your money, get a library card, download Libby. You get access to audio books for free, & any exclusives won't be for long. Remember the podcasts?
author: abby v.r.

text: I used to love spotify. I've had it for over 10 years. lately it's been telling me I'm offline all of the time when I have either Wi-Fi or cellular data connection. I'm having trouble playing it on an external speaker. whether that be a Bluetooth speaker or on my TV app... not very happy with the service as of right now.... I've uninstalled and re-installed the app on both my phone and my tv. I am getting automatic updates. I'm at a loss here. I will update review if the service gets better..
author: Aurora

text: I've used Spotify for over 10 years. i remember when you got 1 minute of adds every 30 minutes on the free version. ive paid the subscription for most of those years, but i can't afford it anymore. went back to free, now its 1 to 2 minutes of adds every 2 songs. so every 6 minutes there are 2 minutes of adds. its disgusting. its predatory. this billion dollar business has no reason to put adds this frequently. i feel like listening to music and i think "nvm the adds are too much". bad company.
author: Abigail Laird

text: Stop suggesting me podcasts in other feeds. If I'm already looking at a podcast I do not want to see episodes from other shows, let alone the ones that are being suggested. If I want to find other podcasts I'll look for them. If I accidentally click on one of them I keep seeing them and other unhelpful suggestions. Fix this or were canceling our premium subscription.
author: John Kanzlemar

text: Spotify, Great app and selection. There's only one thing. Please allow the ability to delete your podcast and show list. There are numerous complaints about this. Why won't you do this? 6/17/26- Hey Spotify, me again. Writing to leave a review of the app. I really enjoy it and finding new music and podcasts. However, I still would love for you to give us the ability to delete podcasts we no longer listen to. Why won't you do this?
author: Jeff Tarantino

text: Spotify is a great app for listening to music. If you have a group of friends or family that all want premium, it is very affordable to split costs and definitely worth it. there is a plethora of songs and podcasts to keep you entertained, and it has some cool features like creating a playlist based off of a prompt you gave, or a cool blend option to make a playlist with your friends to mix your tastes into 1 playlist and see how similar your tastes are. Awesome app!
author: Obadiah Theis

text: Please fix your app. I'm so frustrated at the constant crashing. After it crashes I get pop-up "Something went wrong with Spotify. Spotify closed because this app has a bug. Try updating this app after the developer provides a fix for this error." I also got an alert from my phones Device Care.. "Spotify is crashing frequently. Put it in deep sleep to keep your phone running smoothly." I've done "clean reinstall", did not help. Multiple hand devices, same problem. This started after last update.
author: Can F

text: app has been crashing like crazy! please fix it! Music is great!!! but the audio books are NOT worth it. most books are somewhere around 20 or so hours long and they only give you 10 listening hours a month when you pay for the audio book add on. you should be able to listen to at least 1 whole book if you're paying for the add on
author: Meowlory Carnez

text: i've been paying for premium for a few years now, and i've had some minor and mildly annoying issues here and there. this most recent one is easily the most frustrating, though. whenever i play a song, it randomly pauses every 31 seconds. i hit play to resume the song and same thing, paused after 31 seconds exactly. continuous problems like this makes me think it might just be worth it to start fresh on a new app. $13/month for dumb problems like this.
author: omierufus

text: Preferred these days over amazon music and others due to more consistent title retention. My issues are with the shuffle selection which just replays the same small selection in the same order. It's more of a recent snapshot then a true shuffle. Also, as of lately the widget has been replaced it seems with a much larger version. The old slim design has been disabled it appears forcing me to use the larger. Except I'm stubborn so I'm just not going to have one at all.
author: DZ TRIPP

text: ​I am rating this 3 stars because I signed up for the 3-month free trial, but I was immediately charged $13 instead. The app explicitly advertised a free trial with no ads, yet my account was billed right away. While the premium features themselves work great and the ad-free listening is exactly what I wanted, the unexpected charge was really frustrating and misleading. I hope customer support can resolve this billing issue, as the promotion should have been honored as advertised.
author: Isaiah Carter

text: 5 minutes of songs with 2 minutes of ads. can only listen to so many books with a subscription. I'll get the subscription again, if you can do a little better. otherwise I can just be a pirate instead. Why don't you know how shuffle works? it's supposed to shuffle my whole playlist, not just a bunch of songs you want me to hear on loop.
author: Keegan Branscum

text: For reference, i share a duo plan with my best friend. The app is simple to navigate, you can find songs you are looking for simply and quickly. Streaming quality is good. The only issue I run into is the window, when I'm doing other things on my device or if I have my device off, closing itself abruptly.
author: Shadow Rosetta

text: I really don't appreciate forcing a new widget on me. always nice to have less options for more money. ever hear the phrase "if it isn't broken, don't fix it"? The last few times they raised the cost, the app seemed to get worse. Tracks start over when I get a notification, app would freeze then not remember what I was listening to. If they raised the cost of a family plan again any time soon, I'll go the free route. I used to have 5 stars on here.
author: Gawd Furbid

text: (Originally 2 stars.) Fine app, way too many ads. Every time I get an ad it's already 3 ads or 6. Disappointed (New response, still 2, if not 1.5 stars.) The song selection is fine I guess, the amount of ads are annoying especially for mobile. The good quality of the Songs gets out weighed bu the tons and tons of ads. (Updated) Now I get more songs but 4-5 or even 6 ads. (Overal) Fine if you have money to waste on this app...
author: Braxton Nortz

text: App has not stopped crashing since the last update. Now I can't even get songs to play for 2 seconds before the app completely crashes and I get a notification saying the app has a bug and to update when a fix is provided. I pay for premium and I can't access my playlists on mobile without the app crashing, which means I can't even access the playlists I've downloaded for offline listening. I've already cleared the cache, as instructed, but it made it worse. So frustrating.
author: Emily Meyer

text: the free version is horrible I am unable to pay for premium features and I have to deal with the free version you only get 6 skips an hour you can't change the queue you can't go back to a previous song! ...at least give us the ability to change the queue + go back to the song that just played at least the ads are bearable for the most part tho edit: WHAT THE HELL I FOUND OUT THAT YOU CAN'T EVEN CHANGE THE ORDER OF THINGS YOU PUT IN THE QUEUE AT LEAST LET US CHANGE THE ORDER OF WHAT WE PUT IN 🙏
author: Arianna Donaldson

text: Paying for the premium at one point was worth it. I continue to have issues with the player. The app will stop playing music and close. This started 2 or 3 months ago. I have reached out to customer support and no response. I have done everything on my end. Uninstall then re-install and this has done nothing. Deleted cache, nothing. Today it closed 5-10 times on a 30 mins drive. This is not only unsafe as I know have to focus on my phone and not the road. Started looking for another service.
author: Joe-Alan Grant

text: I pay for Spotify. When it gets updated I loose liked songs. It's song types like "rock" or country or something like that is completely random for what it classifies as a style. I gave a very critical review of this app before but no surprise the review disappeared. I'm actually going to stop paying for this app, they don't pay the artists for the songs, increase how much I have to pay and I loose my likes and play lists so seriously why would I pay for something that doesn't deliver?
author: Karl Calkins

text: Your new 5×2 widget sucks. It is presented well, but I have had nothing but problems when actually interacting with it. I just opt to open the app to start, pause, and change what I'm listening to because it's more reliable than hoping the widget will work. The 4×1 works mostly fine. Spotify premium has also felt less worth it as time goes on.
author: Edger Slayer

text: I hate how Spotify gives you 4 ads. Like, I just thought it was 2 ads? And don't even get me started that you can't rewind on the free version, then they give you random songs that aren't on your playlist, which is stupid even SoundCloud, and YouTube Music is better. The only thing I like is that the music can play in the background if you're off the app.
author: Nobody

text: Sure it's good and all when it's not spamming you every time you swap tabs about how you failed to make a payment and upgrade it. Or if you spend to much time (about a minute) on the app and it tells you to update your payment because they need money money money. The app feels more stingy and Greedy than it ever has. One of the many reasons I'm gonna keep my payment off and most likely uninstall soon if they don't fix it.
author: Big Bad Wolf

text: The app keeps glitching out. After about 2-3 days it will randomly get stuck on a song, and play ONLY that song on repeat. Want to enjoy your Playlist? No, one song. Want to look up a specific song and then have it play similar music? Nope, just that song. The only fix I have found is to uninstall and reinstall, sometimes more than once. Update: it now cannot go more than a day without getting stuck on a song. Why am I paying for what is effectively a CD with one track?
author: Ian Wright

text: Please urgently fix the shuffle. Many of us have tried everything so that a 30 hour long Playlist doesn't play the same 20 songs yet the app still does so. It is very annoying and frustrating wanting to play ALL of your songs and only hearing the same 5 to the point where you can't stand listening to them again. It ruins the experience of listening to music. Please change this. The app would be great if this wasn't such a big issue.
author: Gil Alvarez

text: hard to want to keep paying a subscription fee for an app that works maybe 1 out of 8 times I try to use it. which is weird because this used to be a genuinely reliable way for me to listen to music on the go. I'm one more minor inconvenience away from cancelling my subscription and account just to go back to physical media. at least the artists would get paid for their work without a cut going to a useless 3rd party that can't seem to keep seamless functionality going.
author: Michael Corley

text: at this point, I might as well use yt music because a lot of the stuff that I feel like should be part of the free version isn't like, we only get six skips per hour.And I agree, we hardly even get like any options of our own playlist, like we make the playlist.We don't want other people barging in on our own playlist. i really hope that eventually it gets better.Because it's really frustrating right now sometimes.
author: Emma Wiens

text: I love Spotify but you need to change the fact we should be able to have unlimited skips, instead of having to pay for premium. For the free version to have limited skips, and have to get 5 ads in a row right after two songs. You have to make people pay for premium just to get no ads, shuffle, choosing to play any songs in order, and to listen without wifi even though most people already can. So what's the point of making people pay for premium just to get unlimited skips, and better audio?!
author: toga humiko

text: Paying for this app to act a mess on a daily basis. Freezes all the time, I delete artists and tracks from playing, it states it won't refer them again for 30 days and the next song is the same artist and the same song. WILD! volume doesn't work when I connect to my Amazon devices and also freezes and disconnects on its own. I've contacted support 4 times and all that I was told to do, didn't work. The app developers knew they had worldwide issues at one point and they lied to me. App Removed.
author: Agustin Rivera

text: Offers audiobooks but limits your listening time, even with paid subscriptions. Offers to give a "top off" of 10 hrs for $12.99. AI DJ is just extra noise when playing the made for you play lists. New widget for the app takes up more space 4x2 now. doesn't work or show pictures of play lists unless connected to high data areas. 4x1 option was better and preferred for my setup.
author: James Cusumano

text: App will not open, "something went wrong" even after a fresh install. Customer service can't be contacted because when I try to login the site just buffers and there no other way to contact support. It is not my data connection because this happens on my phone with and with out wifi connection and my desk top as well. I will be disputing this month's premium charge as fraud. Make your customer service actually reachable. Especially when the error is on Spotify's end.
author: Joshua Brimhall

text: It lets you watch podcasts, depending on how your parents or you set it up. You can watch podcasts, if you make a passcode, if you don't make a passcode, then you can't really watch podcast. There's all sorts of podcasts, but um i'm eleven, and i'm doing this a five out of five, because cause as a kid, we like to listen to music. And I only use this one because panera or something whatever that other app is called.It is horrible.You can't really even skip the songs but on this one, everything.
author: Olivia Nelson

text: it's been great for the most part. After this last update I can't get it to play more than one song without stopping at the .01 mark of the song at the end. I have to open the app and press play every time. Not an Internet issue. Even my downloaded songs do it. Really makes this app unbearable when you don't have a hand free to continuously press play at the end of every song.
author: Brandon S.

text: ads every 2 songs is ridiculous. Improved for a while and now it's back to ads every 2 songs EDIT: I'm disabled on a fixed income. I can't afford to pay for Spotify. I have no discretionary income. The issue isn't having to listen to ads. It's that ads have increased and taken over the platform. 1-2 minutes of ads after 2 songs is way too much.
author: nunya nunyagmail

text: I've been a premium subscriber for years but over the last few weeks the app has gotten painfully slow and unusable. It will play a podcast for 15 seconds and then take 2 minutes to load the next 15 seconds. I cleared cache, deleted and reinstalled the app... nothing. All other apps and downloads work fine. I'm paying a premium price for a terrible experience. Cancelled and uninstalled.
author: Dave Cronce

text: The app is pretty good, there's a lot of options, whether it's songs, audiobooks(either bought or with premium), or even videos. I think it has pretty much all songs that people world search for(my personal opinion). The only big issue is the free version of the app. The amount of ads that show, the fact that you can't download songs for offline access, you can't put other songs in the queue, you can't repeat songs, you can't restart songs, you can't go back to the song before, and much more.
author: Raegan McSpadden

text: I just super hate that everything is behind a paywall. you can only skip a certain amount in an hour, you can't drag to a spot in the song to see if it's what you're looking for, you cant create a Playlist of your own without it shuffling the songs and then including its own. Basically, you lose any customization if you don't pay. and for those reasons, I'm out.
author: T. D.

text: Good: Has a lot of music on it and the automatic radio station stuff works pretty well. Meh: The UI has slowly, but consistently, gotten worse over time. It's still usable, but it's also getting buggier and buggier (something to do with how they're having AI write all their code? It does feel sloppy). Bad: Every time you open it, you get pop-ups asking you about stuff you'll never want. Close one and another appears immediately afterwards. In short: Plays music, but doesn't seemingly want to.
author: Henry Tregillus

text: The playback speed button stopped working. It doesn't change the speed for podcasts at all, nothing happens when I make a different selection besides 1x. I tried force stopping, clearing the app's cache, and the "login 2 times" trick and still nothing. Now I'll have to re-install, but that also means re-downloading my offline songs and podcasts. I like having podcasts and music on one platform but I see why people HATE Spotify for podcasts now. I'm on premium.
author: Britney S

text: I just got a new device because my last one has been having issues. same brand, same style device, same Spotify account aswell. On my previous device I was able to skip without having to worry about ANY limits, and I could pick the song I wanted to listen to but now? six skips per hour, can't pick the song I want to listen to. I've been getting recommended songs that are not on my playlist and I can't fix it without installing the app. Just admit it's only about the money.
author: Savannah SLAYZ

text: I really like spotify. I have found so many great songs and music. I also appreciate the podcasts and have used the books once. My only concern was when I downloaded some songs in a Playlist, it wouldn't play. I closed the app and restarted to no avail. Typically this not a problem though. As in any app there can be glitches.
author: Susan M

text: fairly new user to Spotify, coming from YouTube music, so it's a bit different in format. So far, so good. I do have premium and I know that is better because you don't have Ads to deal with, therefore my review is based off having the better version. I never messed with Spotify without it. Something to keep in mind when reading this review. Could be loaded with Ads, I wouldn't know, is all. Honestly though, anything that has music involved is amazing to me. Happy listening, friends!!!
author: TeEe

text: Spotify was always my favorite app. I had to give up premium temporarily for the first time in years. First of all, while I understand ads for the free services, there are far too many between too few songs. The music stops playing when you close and reopen the app and they take away your ability to choose songs. They are weaponizing the unpaid experience so that you pay for it. It's absolutely disgusting. I can't stand it.
author: Lindsay Spates

text: Used to be an amazing music app. Best one I had. Then like most other companies, they got money hungry. They expect you to listen to FIVE ads in a row. Who in their right minds downloads a music app to listen to more ads than music. It's actually out of control. 2nd problem is when you have your OWN playlist specifically crafted to your taste, they want to throw in "suggested" songs that are not even the same genre and you only have six skips so it's too bad if you don't like it right Spotify?
author: Leanne

text: constant skipping. I am so sick of feeling like a 90s kid running with a discman with how often music skips. its been an issue for a long time and they don't seem to care. update: tried to reinstall. still happens. now my issue is that homepage is so full of stuff I dont care about, I cant even find a way to find new music. I used to love the recommendations based on what I listened to, now Spotify is trying to do everything and none of it they are doing well. maybe YouTube music is better.
author: Krystal Dawn

text: I honestly can't remember how long I've had this app. maybe like 7 years now? it seems like the last few years though the price has increased. I love my music but in this economy I can only pay so much for music before I bust out my iPod and radio. if prices keep increasing I'll have to cancel. also for the price, why did the small widget have to go? I don't want no huge widget on my screen, the thing literally takes half the screen. I might as well be in the app. also some songs just disappear.
author: MaeMae

text: great selection, bad everything else. artists see essentially no royalties. its all become very AI based, every update completely changes the UI, and theyre hiking prices AGAIN in 2/2026. its not what it used to be. I've used it for YEARS and I'm absolutely sick of the way things are now. I tried going back to free but the ADS! I work in a warehouse and having a whole session of ads after 2 songs is not gonna cut it for me! i hate that I pay! and the 30 min no ads is a lie! new widget sucks too!
author: Shawnee S

text: hi, I just want to write and say how much I love Spotify and I am amazed how much Spotify has evolved in the 12 years I've been a subscriber. Releasing The TOP 100 Songs the other week was incredibly profound and I spent a lot of time thinking about where I was at in my life when I was listening to songs on repeat. I hope someone reads this and knows where Im coming from. this app is amazing. the lyrics! omg, what an amazing feature. and linking events?! amazing. thanks
author: Deanna Howard

text: Very good with premium, but terrible with the free version. Practically useless if you don't pay them every month. With premium, however, it is about the best music app available. The free version just contains a TON of advertisements and only allows you 6 skips an hour, whilst also recommending you songs, so if you get a random song you dislike, you can't skip it. The rating fluctuates depending on whether or not you have premium. I understand the 6 skips, but the recommended songs? Too far.
author: Avalee Black

text: I pay for this app. The paid version for music and podcasts is great. I am leaving a review because I am really ticked off about the audio books. First time listening and it cuts me off almost at the end saying I've used up all my time for the month. I paid for it and I can't listen to the end of ONE book. Way to make someone mad, let them listen to 90% and cut them off at the climax. Thats not how you get someone to give you more money.
author: Valerie

text: Decent app, but I wish they'd create a thumb-down feature (like Pandora). We shouldn't have to navigate multiple screens just to tell the app we never want to hear a song again. Also not a fan of now being forced to use a larger widget. If I wanted to use the larger widget, I would've. but the smaller widget gives almost everything I need (play/pause, skip, etc).
author: Jennifer Marie

text: Audio books extremely limited in play time and no option to purchase -- but you can pay $1.30/hr to finish your book. I'll cancel my subscription at the end of the current billing period due to that limitation because I'm not willing to spend 6 months waiting in between billing cycles to finish my current book. Good music selection and lots of podcasts.
author: Kai Nazgul

text: Spotify has become the soundtrack to my life. Whether I'm cooking, road tripping, cleaning, writing, or having a full-blown existential crisis in the shower, Spotify always seems to know exactly what song I need. The playlists are fantastic, discovering new music is effortless, and being able to switch from Thomas Bergersen to Falling in Reverse to 80s rock without judgment is a beautiful thing. Worth every penny.
author: Nancy Amos

text: I used to love this app but lately the bugs are so bad. I can't listen to music without it glitching. I can't add music to my favorites or any playlist without adding it a few times or it doesn't add it at all. I constantly have to force stop it. I pay for no ads and yet I still get ads when I play podcasts. I'm honestly thinking of taking my business elsewhere.
author: Sarah

text: Spotify overall is in my opinion better then most of the other music streaming services, but there are two things I cannot stand about Spotify. First thing is the shuffle is terrible beyond all reason, I feel like I hear the same songs every single day and have not found a way to fix this. Second is even with lossless now, the audio quality still lacks to others.
author: Spike Hutton

text: I really like Spotify, truly, but the amount of ads is super unnecessary especially when you can tell it's only like that because they want to push you to pay for premium. Also removing decent features and making them premium pisses me off really bad. Oh, and now I can't listen to my songs fade into each other because the song ends and it's silent before it plays the next song. No there isn't a needed update, Spotify is just like that. Always need money for anything good
author: Jay Dawg

text: the latest version is actually insanely terrible. I can't search for a song, can't view an artist page, can't even skip songs without the app crashing. I've tried uninstalling and reinstalling, and that doesn't work, so idk what else to do. I pay for premium too so that's definitely going to change unless a fix happens pretty darn quick.
author: Olivia Shirley

text: Always making improvements huh? Like REMOVING the icon next to tracks that lets me know that they are in a playlist!? I can't remember which of my liked songs are in a playlist when I'm going through them to see if I want to put the ones that aren't in a playlist into one of my playlists! So now I would have to look at every one of them individually to find out. I'm tired of paying for Premium only to keep haveing a worse experience. If that isn't fixed y'all will be losing A LOT of customers.
author: Zero Cool

text: Spotify is great! You can find songs for any mood you are in and all of your favorite songs! I personally love it except for this 2 things. 1st is that skips are limited and you cant go back, and that you cant fast forward or rewind songs! The 2nd is that when I want to listen to all of my songs on 1 of my playlists, it has too little songs so it played songs that arent on the playlist! Im pretty sure that there is a way to turn that off and if so, could someone tell me? Overall Spotify is great
author: Deborah Mermelstein

text: June 2026 update: how many times do I have to hide a "Mixed" compilation album track from my release radar before your algorithm realizes I only want full songs? Second, must of the "Mixed" tracks are from compilation albums and not real new releases, in most cases the songs have been out for years. as a DJ this makes my life so difficult. Spotify just keeps disappointing, I wish I could give it negative stars!
author: Alex Alexiev

text: I've been using this app for almost 10 years and it's become unusable. it's been incredibly laggy recently and keeps crashing and giving me the prompt "This app has a bug please clear the cache." I did that and it keeps happening now prompting me with "this app has a bug please wait for developers to provide an update" it only effects the DJ and audio books frequently. like songs and other Playlist make it crash less frequently.
author: Shiftee

text: The ads are getting way too constant, like after every single song and even during one. But overall, the app is absolutely amazing and I can always find the music I want. There's almost really no limitations other than that, ESPECIALLY with the fact there's not really a limit to how many songs I have in a playlist 🫂 and I don't have premium. Again, ads are CONSTANT, and never ending. premium is so expensive for no reason and no actual improvement has been made
author: Carmilla Williams (Cam)

text: Fine for years and now suddenly has a bug that is causing my phone to force stop the entire app. I've cleared the cache, data, even fully reinstalled the app. It's been unusable for days now. I dunno if it's because of the lack of human workers up there or what but this is frankly insane. Edit: The 'clean reinstall' was no help, and used a lot of the steps I had already tried; aside from shutting my phone off. Edit: FINALLY worked after 10+ tries,,, Developers PLEASE fix this.
author: kasstick

text: You essentially can't do a thing without premium. You can't even add a song to your queue without premium anymore, and it plays whatever it likes. Which is usually not accurate in the types of songs you listen to, despite saying so, leaving you to listen to random un-enjoyable songs cut up by ads, and maybe have a chance of getting a song you actually have on your playlist, and like, to play.
author: Brooklyn Stroud

text: I hate the update that took away my toggle video option. I like to listen to podcasts while I work. so I switch it to audio only so it doesn't kill my battery. but when something comes up that the podcasters and their guests want to show everyone, I like to be able to switch over and take a quick look and then switch back to audio and continue working. So now I have to leave video on perpetually and kill my battery. Ruined my love for this app.
author: phillip huynh

text: The app is good, but pretty much only with a ridiculously overpriced subscription. The free version is genuinely terrible, and one of the things that really annoys me is that you can't even control the order of your songs. I'll queue up a playlist, and it will add a bunch of random songs for no reason. And every time I try to play a song I actually enjoy, the app throws everything else out of order. Honestly might go back to yt music.
author: Colin

text: The free version of Spotify is garbage. It won't let you play songs in order. If I want to listen to an album, it doesn't play it in order and will even go out of its way to play songs that aren't even on the album or by the same artist. And the ads are the worst. Overrated app. I would honestly suggest YouTube Music if you want a good music app. It gives you the option to shuffle or play in order, you can actually skip the ads, and it's overall just more user-friendly.
author: Conor Necessary

text: April 2026 Update: The newest version is TERRIBLE. After over a decade of being a premium subscriber, I'm prepared to delete my account. If this is the direction Spotify is heading, I don't want it. Setting controls don't work (Spotify will skip the start of all songs, even if you turn off gapless transitions.) And every song is now surrounded by AI buttons/options. The simplicity of selecting a title is gone. 2016: I don't go a day without using it. I loved it even before I went premium!
author: Jacelyn Sturgill

text: Been using Spotify for a decade or more. They somehow manage to make the UI worse with each update. Rather than fixing the problems, they just add more features nobody wants. I can't open my queue anymore, or add songs to it from playlists. I have to take multiple steps to add a song to a playlist, because Spotify apparently wants me to put every song in my "liked songs" like a psychopath. Listen to your users Spotify, you're only relevant as long as you're better than the alternatives.
author: Elias Clawson

text: It's good, but here are my issues. The ads. If you have a song on repeat once, you get an ad. I can't even listen to 30 minutes of music. Paying is overpriced anyway. The other thing is that I can't search up a certain song without it becoming a cover, or if it's what I actually wanted it's unavailable after a day and it turns into a cover song. Sometimes I don't want to go on YouTube for it. And the fact if you don't have premium, on mobile your stuff is automatically on shuffle. Hate that.
author: Mai Sakurajima

text: As a long time premium subscriber I will be canceling before my next renewal. Price keeps increasing. But they add audiobooks. Cool. I begin listening to those but then you only get a certain number of listening hours per month. No big, I'll add the audiobook+ add-on. Half way through a book I get a notification that I am out of hours for the month 🤣 but never fear you can add a top-up for $12.99. Nope. Canceling my plan. Entirely. I will switch to literally any other streaming service.
author: Logan Robinson

text: We've gone from 5 stars, to 2 stars, to 1 star. Been a premium member for well over 10 years. The app constantly puts me offline no matter if I'm on wifi or 5G highspeeds. Deletes all my downloads. Randomly stops playing music and will just start playing something else. The queue doesn't work half the time. Auto generated playlists are random stuff I don't listen to, and my "on repeat" is sometimes stuff I don't listen to. It's too expensive for it to be so bad.
author: Nero Griggs
