text: trying to use Spotify on my smart TV. I get through a half dozen songs at most then a commercial break. usually it buffers like crazy through the ads, but it plays the music fine, so whatever. Now, tonight, it breaks to a commercial (5 ads) and keeps getting stuck on one of the ads! it doesn't play it, it just sits there, doing nothing! I've been on ads 3 out of 5 (45 secs left of the break) for at least 15 minutes now. What's the deal?
author: Dawn Olvera

text: Too much visual clutter on the homepage. When I open the app, I just want to see my main playlists and easily scroll through my library. Instead I encounter pop-ups for music, podcasts, events, and announcements I don't really care for. An AI bar I don't want to use. Suggestions I don't want. Audiobooks when I never use that feature. Save all the extra stuff for the web version please or separate it into a "Recommendations" tab that uses the vital space the "Create" tab occupies. It's too much!
author: MJ Limbag

text: over the last few years this service has become worse and worse. every few months they raise the price, and find ways to insert adds back into the app EVEN THOUGH I'M PAYING FOR NO ADDS. I can't listen to any of my podcasts anymore without first getting bombarded by a list of auto populated adds that don't even support the artists. wanna skip the adds? too bad, if you try to it'll just throw you into 3 more, each and every time, until you listen to all of them. cant wait to never pay 20$ again
author: Brady

text: I'd pick Spotify over any other music app instantly, honestly. There are a lot of ads, though. It's very easy to navigate, and they have so many songs on there, that if you somehow can't find one on another app, it could possibly be on Spotify! I also listen to it during school, and it genuinely helps me get through the day. There are limited skips and it will choose recommended songs. However, I only listen to my liked songs, so I don't run into that problem.
author: Chessa Edgar

text: Spotify is the best app for Podcasts. Overall, it's great. I use the free version. I don't mind the few ads. But the issue with the free version is the queue. I prefer to listen to the Podcasts episodes in chronological order. Since the recent update, the episodes no longer play in chronological order and it will replay episodes I just listened too. And the queue doesn't allow you to remove, clear, or rearrange items. Please fix this!
author: Bethany

text: IDk what has happened lately with this app, but the basic functionality has gone down the drain. I have been a paying premium member since Google music died. The widget doesn't work, the car play app is spotty at best. The music that I have downloaded to my phone studders and skips. this app become a bloated nightmare. I have to clear the app cache as much as once per week to get a somewhat stable experience. The ensh**ification of everything is getting old. Do better.
author: Konnor Buxton

text: I love Spotify and have been using it for more than half of my life and it's great. Every song I could want to listen to, Spotify has it. I'm glad I can see the queue, add stuff to the queue, pick songs, etc. There are some downs which are the often ridiculous amount of advertisements. One time I had to listen to 7 ads consecutively and as of lately it's been me listening to 5, typically 4, or more ads in a row. Otherwise, Spotify is great. I get why there are more ads but it's too many in a row
author: Noah

text: I have the paid version which is such a huge improvement over the free. I like having access offline and one click to the music videos. However for the last several months I have been unable to fix the transitions between songs. No matter what I have done- alter the settings, reinstall the app, recreate the playlist, the majority of the songs still overlap.
author: Lee Ann Prillaman

text: very slow loading times on mobile. Only after multiple cache wipes and reinstalls did it start to work again. This wouldn't have happened if they actually fixed the app. I'm already looking for different alternatives because of these persistent problems. Also, they LIMIT the number of songs you can skip or directly listen to, which isn't a thing for the desktop version of the app. Overall, the only reason why I still use it is because of all my songs, that's it. Use something else like Tidal
author: Cameron Proctor

text: i hate the ads from when I had the free version. the prices for subscriptions are too expensive. the app keeps glitching for me, it moves slow, gets stuck on a song sometimes, and pauses randomly (no, it's not my internet, because the app on my laptop and iPad is fine). There hasn't even been an update required. The app is starting to be a nuisance.
author: Jamesha “Jamie” Hawkins

text: For starters, the only reason I don't want to give it one star is because it has music. You can't listen to YOUR playlist in the order YOU put it in, you can only listen to it on shuffle, which can ruin the experience. The second thing is that you can't reorder the playlists, even if it's yours. The last thing is the ads. They are WAY too long and frequent. They always interrupt when the music transition is actually good. Oh one more thing, you can't go back in a playlist, nor forward.
author: Meyer Steele

text: Free version sure is bad now, can't even loop a song anymore and can't even choose multiple songs until tomorrow, two simple features that are now behind a paywall, jesus christ people are so greedy and I can't even pay for premium just for those two features again. I want to be able to choose a song myself, not for a pop-up just to tell me to choose again for tomorrow. Plus, way too many ads. I still like the app, but the changes are never good with the free version. 😑
author: Tyzion Dixon (Sir Shadxw)

text: Overall I love the app. For a while now the app has been really delayed. For example, to add a song to another playlist I have to wait 15 seconds or more for the drop-down to appear, tap the plus sign to add the song to the playlist and wait another 15-20 seconds before exiting the drop-down. Otherwise, the app won't recognize the action and the song just won't be added. I've had a similar issue when skipping/pausing songs.
author: Nicholas Hill

text: The paid version is great and I love it. However, with the free version, it feels like they lock more and more features behind the pay wall every day. I've been forced to use the free version for financial reasons and just yesterday I was able to at least add songs to my queue, but now you can't even do that. TLDR: paid is great, free is lots of ads and very little control
author: Alexandra Skye

text: Updated: The app has better fluid transitions to songs via DJ, however the music selection is not as wide compared to Pandora. HOWEVER, the fact I can skip songs and find a majority of my music is great. Also, the limit of audio listing is a bit of a pain, and i wish I had more to continue for listening to my audio books. But other than that, im fairly happy with my Premium account (which I get with my job) Still have this and and Pandora account, but use this one more. 😁
author: Katie Strohmeyer

text: crashing constantly! ads ads and more trashy ads. "Enjoy the next 30 minutes of add free listening"... 1 or 2 songs later 2 minutes of ads. I listen to music for 8-10 hours a day and have never received that 30 minutes. Customer appreciation obviously isn't a priority. Just feels like they're trying to force me to pay for premium. And now the app crashes frequently. Still waiting on that 30 minutes...
author: Grant Wire

text: Spotify gets a 3 but its falling fast. In the last few months it has not been working properly. My app is up to date and my internet connection is great. I have even uninstalled and reinstalled Spotify. My other apps work great, just not Spotify. One of two things happens. It either takes a few minutes to load up, or it simply doesnt and I try again later in the day. Very annoying when you pay for an app and it doesnt work. Spotify Ive had you since 2014, but currently looking for other options.
author: Dan Dan

text: I've had Spotify for years, both free and Premium. I've never been a fan of ads, but I dealt with them until I finally purchased Premium. (Back then, it was random ads but sometimes it'd be 30 minutes of uninterrupted music). As for premium, unlimited music, tailored weekly playlists based on your music, an option to download to listen while offline and the inclusion of audiobooks makes this the app I will never leave.
author: Amy Overacker

text: Idk how or why Spotify has become so goddamn slow. It takes forever to search and load anything. It won't let me add songs to my playlists half the time. Constant audio issues as well. THIS is the biggest music streaming platform out there. How is this happening? I also miss the "what's new" section as well. I went from being on top of new music to being in the dark half the time. Fix it, and get rid of the abundance of AI gimmick playlists plaguing my home screen.
author: Jared

text: I would give more star but I have a past with the app. I remember what it used to be and loved it then. This app makes it almost impossible to enjoy your music because of the ads. If I dont want to pay way to much to get premium then I have to sit through 4 minute long ads while listening to a playlist. I cant repeat my song when listening on here because it plays even more ads then if I just listened to a playlist. I use music as a creative so focus, to create and to feel things. I cant do that
author: Eithnee van Tuil

text: Enshittification shows itself strongly here. Albums now repeat, without any way to disable it. Which defeats the purpose of the artist's choices and reduces the impact of a good album. Shuffle, regardless of which setting you use, will pick favorites of the week, and if you have a playlist with 300 songs, you might only get to hear 50 without manually selecting a different one. They also made it so you can't block artists from suggestions anymore. all around awful service.
author: Melanie McGrath

text: God this app is so annoying. I can't choose what I want to listen to, my podcasts won't download, and I can't even play what I want without it beginning from a random song in the album. I quite literally have to skip through until I find what I originally wanted to play. That wouldn't have been much of a problem if there weren't a limited number of skips. Spotify, please fix this app.
author: Naz ʕ·ᴥ·ʔ

text: Ever since the update I have been experiencing nonstop issues with Spotify. Both on mobile and on Chromebook. The app's front page doesn't load, playlist images do not load. Clicking on a playlist doesn't show any of the songs and is instead empty, and when I am somehow able to load the songs it doesn't play any of them. It either doesn't play them at all or skips through every song in rapid succession. Spotify on Playstation is working for now, but we'll see how long that lasts when it updates.
author: Altero “Luis” Rivera

text: Had to uninstall and reinstall to fix a blank screen. If I check the play queue on a free account, the app now straight up lies about what's ahead and never actually returns to the original album or playlist. Otherwise: I can easily add playlists and access all the recommendations associated with my account, and Premium's offline option works well to save data. And I can rearrange the order of songs within a playlist! Unfortunately, no folder arrangement support. No ICE ads, please.
author: Hana Sundet

text: having issues being able to add new podcast episodes to the queue. I already cleared the cache, getting ready to uninstall and reinstall. I wish there was a way to shuffle the queue...for example I want different types of holiday music, so I add multiple playlists to the queue, but they only play in the order that I added them. It would be great to have shuffle as an option. I still love this app!
author: Heather Navarro

text: No matter if I switch to new playlists or switch to a podcast, whenever I go back to music I will get the same songs in the same queue order. Nothing I read online has helped. I spend so much time skipping songs I heard hours ago because the queue has reset. This has gone on for months. I will not recommend Spotify until this is fixed. Even the response tells me to do things I have tried. Your customer service team cannot help at all
author: Cody Burk

text: They have started to lock basic music player features behind a paywall. this review will stay until it is changed back. In 2 days they initiated it then reversed it within 24 hours. Then initiated it again but this time there was a nice new discount on their subscriptions. after reading comments this is the second time they've done this within a year. ads I can handle it's part of a free service. but if this happens again I will never use Spotify again. many other options out there.
author: Jesse Mollica

text: I'm not sure if this is a problem on my end, or if it's something in the app, but it's suddenly started to refuse playing music for me, either not loading in the playlists, or skipping over songs one after another... I had those app for about six years I think, and this hasn't happened to me before. If possible, I'd like to hear a response as to what happened and whether it's something on my end to try fixing...
author: Abigail Cipolla

text: ap has done nothing but crash for the last week, if it even loads in the first place. in the rare instance I do get it to load, I choose a song from my library, and it starts playing an entirely different song that's not even on my library. signing out and re logging in doesn't work, deleting AP and redownloading doesn't work, don't understand how they can raise prices but lower the quality of service on the ap.
author: Nick Garland

text: I like this app because I can put songs in one place when I remember the song, but the ads, and the pay to use, like ARRANGING YOUR SONGS, That is the most pay to use thing i have ever seen, don't even get me started on price and having 80% of the app locked behind a subscription wall. but otherwise, it's a good app if you don't mind the downs. "update: this app is just a paperweight if you don't have Spotify plus whatever this pay to use app has, and they have zero shame for that." don't get
author: Stephen Jones

text: Spotify always tells me I am offline, and I have to clear the app from my recently used apps 1-3 times. Then it will say I am back online. When I click the buttons to choose what genre I want to listen to, it rarely stays on that genre and lately, isnt even working at all. I again have to reload the app 1-5 times just to get it to work. It is becoming more frustrating lately than it was before.
author: kai poitra

text: Even with paid subscription, I'm getting ads! Can't play podcast video. It's perfectly fine to watch a 20-30 minute promotional video while driving, but impossible to watch podcast videos. Another proof of just how greedy their decisions are, it has nothing to do with safety. Playback is randomly switching between phone and tablet when I press stop, play, on headset. Both devices have the shared control set to OFF.
author: Attila iD

text: Honestly getting really sick of not being able to use the app without being connected to WiFi. I have enough signal to stream video in HD on another app, but you mean to tell me that's not enough signal to load one audio only podcast? It's a daily issue, and it's obviously on your end. Glad you guys keep upping the price of premium while consistently making the app work. Really makes me feel like my money is being well spent.
author: David

text: More expensive but not better. I liked the way it used to work. If you're playing a playlist and then pause it. It resets the playlist or something. I keep listening to the same songs because it doesn't know how to keep your spot on a playlist anymore. Edit to change to 1 star. I loved this app. I recommended it to everyone. Now the app is trash, trash, garbage, trash. Plays random playlists. randomly shuts off. I just transferred my playlists to YT, because f this. SPOTIFY is not Real.
author: Breezi Hendrix

text: Spotify is cool since you're able to listen to music and stuff. Got lot's of artists, has some nice features, etc. But the app lags so much when loading and also when trying to play a song from a playlist. Whenever I want go listen to one of my playlist, it waits a dozen seconds before starting the playlist. And when I am listening to a playlist, whenever I click on a song to listen to, it lags it by another dozen seconds. Hell, it sometimes doesn't even play it. Fix this bruh it's annoying.
author: Mr.Stichable

text: 5/2/26 update: When it works, it's fine, but it has a lot of issues- cutting out of podcasts randomly, jumping to entirely new ones in the middle of one playing, and now it's just playing random podcasts instead of the one I'm selecting. It plays ads, only on my phone, despite having a premium account. Someone at spotify commented to try reinstalling- I did, obviously. I'm not going to keep wasting time trying to figure out why your app sucks, that's not my job. Looking for a new service.
author: casey babb

text: As if the most recent update, casting to Google Home speakers no longer works. I can still use it with Bluetooth ear buds and headphones. I can still use it in my car. I can still cast to my LG TV or my Roku. But casting to my speakers — either directly or by audio mirroring my phone to my speakers — no longer works. All my other music or audio apps still work (wife's experience is the same). So, it's the app, not our phones.
author: Thomas Jones

text: WAY too money hungry, pressures you into getting premium such as using unskipable ads and always using pop-ups. It LIMITS the amount of songs I can play per day and it has the AUDACITY to play songs that aren't even part of your Playlist. These "Recomended" songs are always a coin toss, with one side being heavier and more likely to be on top- The Bad Songs. Pair this with the amount of songs you can play and you're listening to a song straight from a garbage land fill for who knows how long.🫩
author: Miguel Perez Bonilla

text: The app is good at first but it limits so much stuff if you dont pay for premium. None of the ads are skippable, and some of them last 2 minutes long. At one point, it literally said that I have to pay to choose what song I want to listen to, which is absurd because that's the whole point of a music app. This app might as well just say "If you don't pay, tou can't listen."
author: Ridley Bolick

text: It's possibly one of the greeidiest music companies out there. I joined when the app had minimal advertising, and you were actually able to listen to music. Now, this company has 30 seconds of ads every 2 songs (about 1/5 of my time listening), and constantly locks features behind premium. This has slowly gotten out of hand to the point where it will lock rewinding songs behind premium. Not to mention the terrible UI, trying to advertise podcasts when I only listen to music, and AI songs. 2/5
author: Kyson Rice

text: Severe decline in quality over the past year. Been a user for the past 7 years? The Ai slop is incredibly annoying and not having the option to turn it off is frustrating. Besides that it's just a endless loop of the exact same songs. I try to search new things and it just plays the same songs over and over. I used to find new music I liked often now I'm lucky to hear anything but 1 genre. It used to be a music app now it just feels like another doomscrolling app.
author: Justin Haefemeyer

text: I love Spotify 💕 it has all my music in one place & such a wide variety to be able to look for new music. I have premium & I honestly think it's worth it. That hulu/spotify college student deal is killer (I don't have it but I wish I did. I'd lose all my music & I don't wanna have to go through switching it to a new account a 3rd time) edit: ive been getting a bug where I'll listen to music, close the app, & when I start up again, the music is at the beginning of my first session
author: Em M

text: I used to love this app, the music and podcasts were great in the free version then I started paying and the ads are just killing me now. How is that even though we are paying we are getting those annoying ads in the middle of the podcast! I'd rather pay for the YouTube premium add and avoid them than continue paying for ads in the middle of my podcast or at the end of every other song. This is not a premium service anymore.
author: Leidy Ruiz

text: It's the best music app on the market for the value it gives you. The premium is 11.99 but it gives you access to every song you could ever imagine. and if you can't pay the free version isn't bad but has its downsides (as it should for a free version). And the price is market price so it's very reasonable if you can't pay $12 a month for music then you don't really need the music.(no hate just Spotify isn't that pricey)It's the best subscription I have and I have thousands of hours on Spotify.
author: Josh Fischer

text: 2 minutes of ads, just to listen to 3-6 minutes of music (depending on the length of the song, could be 1-3 songs), the most small features now require premium, and now, for some reason, I can no longer import the music I have downloaded on my phone. Being able to listen to my downloaded music like that was the main reason I had this app, but when I try to import my music now it says "This content is no longer available." You guys have absolutely butchered your app.
author: Noah Wingheart

text: Pretty great, i love music quality. I love that i can turn my phone off or leave the app and music will keep playing. The equalizer is a cool option. The only thing I hate is how they limit skips and you can't rewind where you are in a song (on non-premiun account), as well as after like 30 minutes you can't choose which song you want to listen to (also non-premium).
author: Danny Il.

text: Spotify is a classic for a reason. I like the variety of music, but I wish I did not have to pay for Premium to escape the dreaded ads. I know that's how this company pays for itself, though, so :/ I guess it makes sense... otherwise reliable and fun to use (And in my opinion, there are so many pros that they easily outweigh the cons)! EDIT: bringing down to three stars because you shouldn't have to pay just to fast forward or skip songs. New video feature pushes ads twice as often.
author: Evelyn M

text: absolutely the worst! just opened the app and said something went wrong. also, I clicked on a song in my likes, and it stopped, said nothing playing. WILL NEVER BE USING THIS SERVICE AGAIN! I wish 0 stars was possible! every time I click try again, it would pop up the same message not even a second later: "something went wrong, try again." DO NOT DOWNLOAD, WHAT A WASTE OF STORAGE!
author: Jaakub Johnson

text: * Ok ok I'll come back to premium next month, stop asking me! Love Spotify. First had it without ads, and it was near perfect. But the price is kind of high (13/mo sheesh) Now I have the free version and it's still pretty good. I can listen to all my playlists, though I can't choose the order of the songs or replay them. They don't always let you choose a song but often you can. The ads are frequent but shorter in duration than regular radio ads. overall, great source of music!
author: Comrade Rotifer

text: The price can be steep, but the features that come with that price make up for it. I don't know if any other music media app that allows you to cross-fade music tracks. With the addition of audio books and podcasts too, there will always be something to listen to. I personally feel that Spotify has a much larger artist library as well. having automatic downloads active means that you will always have something to listen to even if you lose signal. I very much enjoy using my Spotify.
author: Logan Littlefield

text: It's an okay app.You can make individual playlists it's just that not only do you have long ads, there is a limit to how many songs you can play or else it shuffles you can't even play the songs out of order or skip without having Spotify premium. If you're unable to play the songs without paying then what's the point. I can play the song on YouTube free of charge. I would say save your money unless you're rich.
author: amadea yankah

text: "30 minutes of ad free music" after one 1:30 long song I get 4 ads. False advertising sucks. Like, the mobile version doesn't let you even pick the order of the songs it's all shuffled all the time unless you have premium. Mobile is the only version that has that by the way. It SUCKS. Fix your app. Edit: The app somehow got worse, you need to pay to REPLAY YOUR ALBUM. I AM SO DONE WITH SPOTIFY. YouTube Music it is. Screw you spotify. I'm done.
author: Castle crasher YT

text: I had almost all Music apps but never really used them more then I do with Spotify. I love everything about it. I also love the mixes it makes for work outs or to clean or road trips. it keeps me going and motivated plus I hear songs from singers I never heard before that makes me want to go see them in concerts !! I love Spotify. if your looking for a music app/podcast this app is for you!!
author: Rosalee Meehleib

text: Forced ai integration ruined this app. You can like a song and it simply won't like. when you hit options for a song such as to add to a Playlist, not all the options will be there. Your home screen won't be music most of the time now. Now its random videos / podcasts you've never heard of on topics that they're trying to push. Then there's the false shuffle issue they've had for years and it seems its gotten far worse. Waste of money and time go to SoundCloud.
author: Christian Whitney

text: I absolutely love this music app! I have been using Spotify since 2014 and I have always had the best experience while using it. I also love that Spotify offers podcasts on here as well. Having premium and being able to make my own playlists or podcasts is amazing for when I want to worship God, workout, listen at work, or go on road trips. I also love that Spotify will help you find artists similar to who you like and listen to, along with Spotify making you customized playlists for you too!
author: Miranda Hughes

text: will not update my followed podcasts properly. skips to the 1st episode (already listened to) of a podcast when you finish an ep instead of going to the next. and constantly "tries to play" notif when app is closed. edit: I did reinstall before even reviewing and it fixed nothing. my podcasts show up fine on my laptop the app just wont work.
author: Evan

text: This app once was a 5 star music gem... At least with a paid account. Now after the almost useless additions of music videos, AI playlists and excess song info etc. it crashes randomly all the time, often takes several minutes to load, sometimes has issues connecting to devices. Annoying when it stops playing outta nowhere then takes 3 min to reload what you were jamming... Still probably the best collection of ava music. Trying to do more than what it was best at... playing music
author: Joe Wilson

text: The music selection is great and that's pretty much it. I'm absolutely sick to death of podcasts and audiobooks. I just want music and Spotify has turned it's focus to other things, including garbage AI. The discover feed is pretty poor now, doesn't really find any more artists that you'd like that are similar but I see nothing but full screen ads for things I don't care about, and I pay you ad free Spotify. I'm definitely at the point where I will be looking at other apps that fit my needs bett
author: Brian

text: I stopped paying for premium and I was shocked by the underhanded tactics they pull on basic members in order to make their experience unbearable, until they shill out for premium. The amount of ads are ridiculous, and now I can't even listen to an album, either in-order or shuffled, without other songs in the mix?? And now, after a recent update, I can't even click on what songs I want playing from my playlists. This is scummy, but somehow they still can't afford to pay their artists properly.
author: feral catgirl

text: free version is horrendous, with excessive ads and total limitations. Forgetting users who try free version later become premium; if you limit everything, people will leave. Whoever is making decisions in app is only leading it to failure. Instead of limiting or having massive audio ads, why not place image ads or offer large rewards for watching ads? Right current version doesn't work; the songs don't load.
author: Tristan Rosseau

text: As a paying customer I do not want to pay for AI slop. I want real music, written by real musicians, real pod casts read by real people, videos created by the band, etc. I do not want to have to play detective every time an unfamiliar song comes on. "Is it AI or does it just suck?" please provide a way to block this content. The badge helps, but it doesn't keep slop from being suggested or played.
author: Christa Genger

text: I used to get mad at the UI changes that would be made every so often but now the app is near unusable. My everyday play list has hours of music with hundreds of songs on it. It used to be that shuffle would remember the session and not play any of the songs it had already played or that I had skipped but now no matter when I start or stop the playlist, shuffle wants to play the same 30 songs. I know Spotify is really proud of their new vibe coded stuff but now the app sucks.
author: Jas Smith

text: Fix your widget! 90% of the way I interact with your application is via the widget. The old widget was fine then you announced the new one and it is not good. It constantly says I'm offline and "tap to refresh" but tapping does nothing so I have to search for the app and launch it from there. I'm not offline. I work from home I'm always online. even if I was offline I have several downloaded playlists. The other widget never cared if I was "offline". Just fix it or let me use the old widget.
author: Finneus McClain

text: App stopped working after updating on June 15th 2026. Keeps telling me "something went wrong" and will not fix itself upon refresh. Tried restarting phone. Turned off & on. App still will not work. Internet works, obv, bc i can still review. App has been weird for a while, but I was letting it slide bc I could still listen to music. No point in paying for a music app with no music to play. Seriously considering ending Premium & closing account.
author: Deku Lily

text: Its been amazing!! Best with premium. Without premium? Use Youtube music! My only issue, which is deducting three stars, is that it ALWAYS glitches and kicks me out of the app! Listening to music, I change a song, then about halfway through the song, the app closes. The song restarts upon opening the app and pressing unpause. This is thoroughly annoying! It makes no sense considering my Spotify is up to date.
author: Hazel

text: Sometimes it resets my podcasts back to the beginning, annoying af. Updated: subscription expired and now prices have gone up even more. on top of that, the ads are ridiculously loud. I'll be in the middle of a workout and suddenly my neighbors can hear the ads coming out of my speakers.
author: Young09ethan

text: Used the old widget for years with absolutely no problem. i was forced to use the new widget and it's hot garbage and never works. The service is nice, but I'm pretty annoyed that the old reliable widget was replaced with one that looks EXACTLY A THE SAME but rarely works. I'm guessing a good amount of it was vibe coded based in its performance.
author: Derek

text: the ads suck, like really suck. and the app itself sucks sometimes because ur Playlist is on shuffle(I personally don't like that, I like the order that I have it in for a Playlist make) and you only get six skips per hour, sometimes within 2 hours. I love Spotify, I really do, but lately it's been making me mad with all this stuff.
author: Alexander Paul

text: This app is GENUINELY unusable without premium on mobile. You can't do anything without having to pay and it sucks. It pretty much forces you into getting premium and it doesn't even guarantee that it'll run smoothly. Some stuff wont load sometimes and it is NOT just my device or internet because I've tried with other devices/internet. Other than that the app is okay only with premium.
author: moon

text: This app has gone downhill. If I'm being honest, it's probably 3 stars, but having used the app for years, the app is not what it used to be. It's still my go-to app for music, but it's getting slower. What used to be reliable is now infuriating. And before anyone says it's my internet, no, it's not. Even after trying everything, the app is slow to load and respond. Offline mode isn't even usable. I know I'm hating on Spotify. I just wish they would focus on performance rather than new features.
author: Brayden McAllister

text: Love this app has all the songs I love and plus I get to like new songs to when it recommend music!! Definitely the best :3 ❤🧡💛💚💙💜 Edit: Okay so sadly this has changed my app keeps crashing like bad I've cleared the cache and restarted the app but nothing it's like broken. I love my music and during work I can't pull out my phone a fix it. hope this issue gets fixed.
author: Jenny Escabar

text: please make this more like the desktop version. we need to be able to see all the music, and play albums with the songs actually in order. also why don't you guys offer CDs and records etc? I mean really, screw it and become your own distributor. also it sux when i am browsing through music and it will randomly start playing the first song i heard when I opened the app. other than that, pretty dope! how about a mode with less clicks to play music? bigger play button? disappointed @ lack of reply
author: Ziphren Kamikaze

text: Disappointed with platform as far as app goes the ads are exceptionally loud, reminiscent of when FCC intervened with television ads. Also doesn't play the songs selected from groups I follow! So now I rarely use the app, instead installed another app that allows me to not only upload my music, but also provides the option of selecting music to download from other selections!
author: Sandi T

text: sucks! the new update sucks! it makes it so you can only skip so many songs which means you only get so many randomly choose a different song, which i use a lot to skip ai songs. and it makes it so you can choose from a playlist one song and not one randomly so many times to! i mean i knew it sucked but not this badly. Just use youtube, way more user friendly.
author: Willow Beauchamp

text: Ai DJ sucks, the algorithm only seems to go by what you tend to listen to instead of musical theory of any sort. Huge changes from Die Prinzen to Green Day, when I wanted to listen to Die Prinzen and similar German pop rock bands. Other than that I would reccomend the premium version if you listen to a lot of music, the regular algorithms seem to do quite well when you want to listen to a specific artist or genre.
author: L Squared

text: got rid of the 1x3 widget for no reason. I don't want to use half my screen for the 2x high widget. Honestly makes the whole experience so much worse. Edit: despite Spotify's response it still sucks. can't go forward or back. It still gets stuck on can't update, and changes to some random playlist when I reach the end of my podcasts. It has made my Spotify experience so much worse.
author: Rich Jones

text: over 10 years as a premium subscriber and have been getting random pop-up ads in the past 2 months. tried support and the end resolve being told that is what happens... what part of NO ADS is misunderstood? this is from music, not podcast listening... got passed around 4 times... canceled my subscription no resolution, no escalation of problem I don't recommend this app
author: Dreamcatcher Swistak

text: Honestly I've been all over the board with music apps. I even had Pandora or whatever it was for three years straight, then I came across Spotify (I had it but on my TV) which lasted two months before I started getting annoyed! I switched to SoundCloud which then I got hacked but couldn't sign back into SoundCloud so I came back to Spotify. I hate Spotify because of the ads. Me and auto books are like besties CANT LISTEN TO THEM BECAUSE I don't even have premium! Other than that it's good 👍
author: Nicole M

text: the only good thing about this app is the premium with no ads, unlimited skips and repeats, and doing wtv you want with your playlists every time I want to play a game, my music stops and Spotify decides to restart my entire song, it's just repetitive and I thought it was a 1 time thing, but it's an every day thing, it happens 2-3 times a day if I'm lucky Edit: it's happening at least 6 times a day now and 4 if I'm lucky, which I never am, pls fix your app
author: Cayden Spicer

text: Super SLOW interface!! The app is OK, the music is good. But, grief, it takes ages to load things. Try and add a song to additional playlists—you'll wait at least 15-30 seconds for that to open. Truly awful user experience. UPDATE: Reinstalled, as recommended, and it's still super slow. Every app action is sluggish and delayed. Like a song? Wait 15 seconds or more till the UI registers that. Also, sometimes it now won't pause (button changes, music keeps playing) and has to be force stopped.
author: Leona Dorothy

text: Over a year later and this is still broken... Why can I no longer click download and it queue the download while not on wifi or download as I listen to the music? It didn't work for one album then did on another and not on a third, seems completely random. Also I don't need to popup Every time about "download using cellular", man you guys love popups and adverts even on premium.
author: Bobby Hill

text: edit 6/24/26: for a while now, only sometimes do songs add to playlists when I tell them to. i prefer the previous version of how it looked when I went to add a song to a playlist. I'd also like if I could have certain songs excluded from my Spotify Wrapped. Every year, mine is just songs I go to sleep to. 😭 shuffle feature could also be better. it just plays the same songs even if I have others in the playlist that I'm listening to.
author: HarleyCAO 13

text: its awesome! I love music and spotify is my go to. but one frequent problem is that every time I try to add a song to a playlist it doesnt show the pop-up that lets you know it was added so I have to check it like 3 times and sometimes add it like 3 times for it to actually be on the playlist. its super annoying-
author: Sophia Herald

text: This app is trash, you can pay for premium for no ads but they still allow creators to put their ads in. Also consumers shouldn't have to pay for ANOTHER subscription to listen to audio books if they are already pay for premium. AND THE STUPID APP GLITCHES SO MUCH! Searching for a new platform that doesn't try to screw their customers at every turn.
author: Megan Segars

text: This app is increasingly trash. Loading is often painfully slow. The new widget is completely dysfunctional. And the Blend algorithm seems to think that if I listen to a song one time, I automatically love it. Also, I wish I could set a specific playlist to skip explicit songs for my kid, instead of having to set that for my whole account every time.
author: Curtis Evans

text: I've been using spotify for nearly 10 years and I'm not sure what happened with this past update but it crashes constantly. I've uninstalled it, cleared all the caches on all of my apps, restarted a bazillion times, and it still is not working. Not having music is worse than not having a phone.
author: Meagan Crown Esche

text: Overall positive experience. There are a gazillion songs available and the lossless audio quality is excellent. I have a paid plan and will gladly pay for high quality audio. I do find a couple of things frustrating: 1. Some songs disappear from my "liked" list even though they're still available. If they're removed for copyright purposes, I would prefer a notification so I can search for a similar replacement. 2. I'd like to flag songs to never play again, not just 30 days.
author: Philip Sharpe

text: I could only listen to two or three songs before I get 3-5 ads. I would also constantly get the same ads over and over again, even when I do "I get this ad constantly" when it asks. Honestly, there should only be one to two ads after about three songs, and not 4-5 ads instead. I would also get different amounts of ads after listening to a few songs. But either way, the music works well. As a mobile user, I didn't see a clear queue button when I tried to clear my queue, please fix all these.
author: Gloria Taylor

text: The new update is broken. It initially wouldn't fully complete the update, so I uninstalled and reinstalled as suggested and now I'm just getting "Something went wrong. Check your connection." My connection is fine. I've uninstalled multiple times, restarted device, clear cache, etc and nothing works. There's no point in paying for premium if I cant even open the app 🤷‍♀️
author: Amanda T

text: the last week or so audio doesn't play when I click on a song and when I go to switch to a new song and click on a new available sound it will just repeat the previous song again. this happens immediately after I just renewed my bill cycle. so it's really fishy and I have reported feedback but I haven't heard anything else. currently I have a 4 minute long techno song playing that is complete silence and for some reason I cannot get it to play. in the meantime I will use YouTube music.
author: CyberFr33k

text: looking through some reviews having the same issue. why do I have to reinstall in the first step. why do an update and mess things up. I was thinking of getting premium, again but those customers are having issues, too. Pandora, maybe? But I don't like their premium because it changes my music lists. iTunes, I guess and find my old ipod.
author: G G

text: I'm a long time Spotify premium user. Spotify used to be great for music and podcasts, efficient and easy to use. but no longer. Spotify is starting to get messy and commercial. It reminds me of how Myspace & Facebook used to be useful in their early days, but turned into bulky, spammy, corporate, garbage. I am looking for an alternative platform that isn't Apple, I recommend others do the same.
author: Chalk Maze

text: The way this app somehow gets worse and worse with every update is almost impressive. It's gone from slow, due to the huge amount of new, unnecessary "features," to damn near unusable. I've had to uninstall and reinstall the app 4 times in the last 7 days just to use it. If they spent half as much time optimizing as they do adding stuff that nobody asked for, we might actually have a good, stable app. But I'm only a user, not an investor, so my input doesn't matter. 🤷‍♂️
author: Atlas Prime

text: Since the latest update, it's been frustrating to use. The currently playing widget/bar(?) thing inside the app and outside the app has stopped working after an ad plays, so I can't see what song I'm listening to. Restarting the app doesn't fix it. Restarting my phone doesn't fix it. The only temporary fix I've found is changing playlists - but as soon as another ad plays it's broken again. It displays all the ad info correctly, then breaks as soon as it goes back to music.
author: Samantha Bahr

text: Been using Spotify for over a decade now. The prices have been increasing but nothing new except... Spotify wrapped which has been a thing for a while so it's not even new. The DJ is weird. My weekly discovery is sub par now. We still have to pay extra for audio books. We get free audio hours but only for the premium holder for the family package. A wild notion, honestly. It makes the premium look like it's not worth as much as it is now. Worth half as much at this point imo.
author: Niko Horton

text: listen, I would say that the ads aren't annoying because they aren't constant like some other apps, it's the limitations that I hate. only long screen devices like tablets can get access to many things like repeat,shuffle,etc but for phones it limited and we can't even play a entire playlist. no I don't want recommendations I want my playlist also apparently there's a limit on what you can listen too (for your choice) this segregation all over again. DO BETTER SPOTIFY !
author: Claire

text: The new widget absolutely sucks, completely takes me out of my listening history to the most recent episode and when you tap it it doesn't always open the app. I believe that this app is completely worth the monthly payment that's the only real set back however you get what you payed for and so much more, especially if you listen to music a lot! I use this app on almost a daily basis and it is by far my favorite music app! I can find anything and everything not just music! Its an amazing app!
author: Alexis Davis

text: Where do I even start? Ads at the top of the home page every time you go back to it, the "not interested" button on video ads doesn't work, a "recent ads" tab when you're scrolling the menu to get to the lyrics page for some reason, static ads over the album cover on the now playing menu that respawn every time said menu is reopened, having your ability to play a specific song limited unless you pay for the subscription that increases in price every month. This platform is a complete joke.
author: KittyL0v3r00

text: I love Spotify! it's just i can't go back a song,rewind, or go forward apparently? And a little much adds its just to much for the adds for Spotify. Please change that, that would be helpful. But the rest, spotify let me listen to all the music I wanted and gave me subjugation, I have lots of new songs on my Playlist because of the subjugation. Also it does not cost a dime... if your used to adds. I recommend this app just that there's probably going to be 5 adds wasting 3 minutes of your life.
author: Emily

text: Used to be great, not so much anymore. I'll start with saying I do create with AI but I'm very transparent about that's what it is. I have my playlists and up until recently loved Spotify DJ to discover new music. I'm listening today though and it's stuck in an AI generated song loop. Before if I got bored of a genre I could skip a few songs and it would change the genre. Now it just keeps spitting out, and I hate this term but it fits here, AI slop. I should be able to skip the AI songs
author: Tracey Smith

text: I havr been using the free version for a while now and i am it sucks a lot. We are litterally listening to more adds than we are listening to music. I always get interupted with adds every 1 song i listen too! Also, the songs in my playlist don't play from bottom to top. Instead it just plays in a random order. You guys couldn't get any more greedy. At this point, I feel like switching to Youtube is better because you can actyally skip adds.
author: Abigail Gan

text: This app is completely unusable if you don't pay them 12 dollars a month. It takes away basic functions of a music app and locks it behind a paywall. There are more ads than necessary. One time I got 5 minutes of straight ads just for another ad to play after one song. 90% of the time ads play after every two songs.
author: Alexander Sullivan

text: The service spotify provides isn't bad, but the efforts of the company to constantly update it have only made it worse. I pay for this app to listen to what I want, not to listen to paid for suggestions and generated playlists. There's no random shuffle because the app needs to feed you music from labels that pay, so obscure music gets shafted for whatever spotify thinks you want when you listen to long playlists. It'd be nice to listen without algorithms choosing for me, I never asked for that.
author: stale marshmallow

text: I can't afford insurance, but I can afford this. I love having premium on here. it's definitely worth it EXCEPT recently!!! I been having issues with these updates. 1st I used to pay less, which a little irritating with that much increase, but eventually shrugged it off because I love my spotify (normally). AND now as soon as I start jamming out to my Playlist it now kicks me out. At first I thought it was just me & only 1-2 time thing...but no I'm not the only 1 & it happened too many times!!!!
author: Brigit Dawson

text: It's ok. I would give it 5 stars based on customer service and the ability to be able to talk to a real person, but music selection in comparison to you tube and the fact that it's better than Pandora but not by much for a person in my age group and race it's not the best. this is the update. they still don't sync music well yet and for a single person they never offer discounts. to me they don't value long term single customers like they do groups or students
author: Anthony D

text: Getting worse and worse every day. Same issues on my phone, iPad, and PC for over a month. The shuffle feature refuses to work for my downloaded playlists. The queue never shows up, and when it does and I remove songs from my queue, they show back up in my queue back to back. If I start a playlist by picking a song, it'll repeat that one song after every three songs that play. If none of these occur, the shuffle still plays songs from the same artist and album back to back on large playlists.
author: Maggie Beaumont

text: great to listen with, songs are free and you can access it through Instagram and describe what you're listening to, an you also can get a widget for the app and play music. The only issues are that you have to purchase a subscription in order to listen without wifi and there are a lot of adds. But otherwise, its a pretty good app for music
author: Ainsley

text: I keep getting pushed to try the new widget, so I did. It freezes, takes forever to load, and many times won't launch the app, forcing me to go into my app drawer, find it, and launch from there. C'mon guys. This isn't rocket surgery. I've reverted to the old widget, which better not go away before you get the new one to work!
author: Scott Follett, Jr.

text: you can't listen to the songs you want to, you cant skip songs you don't want to listen to, you have to deal with ads, unless you buy premium. It also likes to skip songs randomly. The app is trash and it only gets worse over time for those who can't afford to pay for premium, actively removing features or forcing you to use their garbage new features like smart shuffle. I am not going to pay for premium out of principle at this point.
author: Patrick Stevens

text: The new update has caused the app to freeze and crash, it acts like it's off line when it clearly isn't. I have had nothing but loading issues since the update. I have tried uninstalling and reinstalling only to have it working worse each time. Starting to think it is not worth the money we are paying monthly for an app that doesnt function properly. 6-6-26 app has issues auto changing playlists. trying to hard to be Prime. miss the old Spotify before all the Ai BS stuff, the videos and books.
author: Mis Adam

text: 2026 review update: I've been a dedicated, paid user of Spotify since apprx 2015. I've always loved the control I have on what plays and when. Lately, gapless playback and autofade controls have a mind of their own. I hate when one song cuts off the ending of another one. I've turned it all off, double checked the settings on all my connected devices. I've even uninstalled/ reinstalled the app. The extreme crossfade continues; considering switching to Amazon music.
author: mendy h

text: most music is available, though music will sometimes be removed by artist for whatever reason. If music collection gets too large, Spotify can't handle it and will get VERY SLOW, to the point it can take entire minutes to boot up, and refuse to perform certain actions due to time-outs. but for most people it should be fine. I just have to many playlists.
author: Hi Technique

text: I wish there was a way to edit the custom sorting of playlists on the mobile app. At the moment, I have to use the desktop version to organize my playlists the way I want them and any new playlists I make on mobile are always shot up to the top by default, which can be a bit annoying. Otherwise, great app. I use it daily for all of my music needs. 👍
author: Emily Poe

text: Very annoying experience every day. Whenever my phone connects to any bluetooth device Spotify will automatically start playing music. It doesn't matter if it's in the car, earbuds, or portable speaker, it's still automatically launches. I've turned off autoplay in Android Auto, I disabled Android auto, I unchecked anything related to autoplay in Spotify itself, but it's still doing it. You guys are well aware Im not the only one complaining about it.
author: Slavik

text: This is a great little app, even the free version. Don't see the ads because I use an ad blocker that's not an app but changes anyone can do to their WI-FI settings. One of the things I don't like in the free version is you can't listen to music tracks or albums in a specific row, it's all random. After all it's not the Editor's Choice with 1B+ down loads for a reason. I highly recommend it. Update : with the last app update I can no longer cast to my tv, tv does not show in the connect list.
author: William Carroll

text: I've used Spotify for years but suddenly you can't hear it despite it saying it was still playing on my earphones. I immediately tried other apps and they work fine. I restarted my phone but no change. I've reinstalled it and it still doesn't play anywhere, on the phone speakers or on earphones, despite it showing as playing. The app is now useless!??!
author: Stephen McCoull

text: Spotify has gotten greedy. The music is fine, but every other song so far I've experienced having to wait through five adds. this is every 6 to 8 minutes depending on the length of the songs but the adds themselves are longer then that! There used to be add breaks from Spotify but it seems they've completely abandoned that and overloaded their listeners with tons of adds to either pressure you to buy premium or to generate more money than they already have. it's gone absolutely down hill now.
author: Jacqueline V

text: The podcast tab is completely unreliable. I am receiving the error "Whoops something went wrong" about 50% of the time now, very infuriating. Researching online tells me that this is a very long-running problem - with absolutely no solution offered. And why am I getting overwhelmed with advertisements when I'm paying for a subscription?
author: Steve Ryan

text: I've had Spotify for years and think it's great but my god the algorithm is garbage and so is the shuffle. you will constantly get the same songs playing over and over again. Whenever they update the app it gets worse. There is no reason that should happen when my library has over 5000 songs. Same thing if you pick artist and song radio with music in the same genre. It constantly plays the same songs even under a different artist or song within that genre. Please fix. It is very annoying.
author: Taylor Behnke

text: Pretty good music, nice podcasts, overall a good app. However, there's 1 major issue: for well over a year Spotify has been crashing randomly on my end for no reason. It's really frustrating because it restarts the song too and I've tried everything to fix it. It crashes all the time and no fix works. I've talked to customer support and they've done nothing. I refuse to use another music app, so PLEASE FIX IT!!! 1 star because Spotify doesn't listen to my complaints about the issues. Do better.
author: Moth

text: Ok everyone knows Spotify is great so I'll just mention what could be better: 1. My daily mixes are too similar to each other, and there's too much music style diversity inside each mix. Would be better to have more narrow-scoped mixes. 2. The app performance is mediocre, specially taking into account how basic it is. It's unreal that a music player can get slow in a modern-day device with gigabytes of RAM and a processor that a decade ago would be for computers and not phones.
author: Santiago Serrano

text: absolutely no complaints whatsoever. But I do have a suggestion on a function you guys could add if you could ever update. Can you guys make it so that way you can more easily control the songs on your playlist? Like. set specific parts that you want to play instead of having to skip through a song to get to the part you like. Or, having to skip the first 20 seconds because the song has a long start up. You get what I mean?
author: Hampter Hampton Hamilton

text: It seems like you wanna burn my skips sometimes. I have over a thousand songs on my playlist and I somehow manage to get these rows of songs that I have to be in a certain mood for to want to hear. I also don't understand the point of putting the little radio button on the mini player RIGHT NEXT TO THE SKIP BUTTON!! IT'S CRAZY MAKING!!! ABSOLUTELY CRAZY MAKING!!!
author: Robbie Bryant

text: I've really enjoyed using Spotify. I'm 55 so I'm not always the best with the newest stuff but they make the directions pretty idiot proof! They don't always have the customizations I want (and of course I can't think of one at the moment) but I do like the ease of use and that they've always had any album or song or artist that I r looked for! A solid 4 stars.,
author: Dawn C

text: On the home screen tab of the app, there is already a grid of 8 things that you've recently played. Which is helpful sometimes but they get replaced by other playlists. I wish they were recommendations based on the time of day or on recently played or the type of music you've recently been listening to or your birthday. I wish we could also pin a song, playlist, album, podcast if we would rather have that take up a space, rather than the suggestions.
author: Ryan Bonner

text: The layout is not consistent/intuitive with the PC version. I don't know how to find my daily mixes. seems like they're scattered across the app randomly. headings and sections seem to change every day. the lyrics panel minimizing on every new song is frustrating. I'd like to not need to interact with my phone on each song so I can see the lyrics. when in Android Auto, the audio skips and stutters, and the only fix is to unplug and re-plug-in my phone, and that only sometimes works.
author: Nolan Gustafson

text: I wish that there was a "disliked songs" playlist instead of the "snooze" system they have now. Why wait 30 days to dislike a song when you could just have it catalogued on a permanent playlist? You are already keeping track of the songs. I also don't like have a song downloaded multiple times because of where I liked them from where from different places on Spotify.
author: Zachary Secules

text: Don't know whose idea it was to remove the what's new tab, y'all made it so complicated for no reason. Extremely disappointed I don't have access to the free audiobooks just because I'm not the account manager. Bring back the hearts for saved songs!! Y'all are always changing things y'all shouldn't be, worried about the wrong things always. Don't like how liked songs is now considered a "playlist" either.
author: Liset Hernandez

text: I tried adding a song to a collaborative playlist maybe 10 times now? still not popping up in the playlist. it's not just an isolated incident, either. update or not, if the app isn't working as it should, I see no reason it should garner a positive rating. and that's before the AI enslopification stuff
author: Anthony Facca

text: Not bad, but could better. I love how convientant Spotify is, but I dislike how hard it is to find new, unknown music. Like, when I click the "Radio" option, I still get alot of songs I already have saved in that mix, and when I search for new music and scroll down the list, songs just start repeating... I want to view ALL music on Spotify, not just what Spotify decides to show me based on whatever factors. I feel like they only show me songs that are popular are mainstream...
author: Airjohn Neziri

text: I can't in good conscience give it 5 stars even if I am paying for premium, if the free users suffer so much from having only a skeleton of usable features surrounded by more ads than song, another in the mobile "free app" hellscape. On top of that, they just disabled their small widget, forcing users to only be able to use the 4x2 one, which takes up too much space on my designated widget area. can't wait to finally swap to my new digitized physical library soon, and give Spotify the boot.
author: Garik

text: Just like other reviewers say, too many ads. Yes, I know I can pay for "ad free" but I don't want to and app should play more songs than ads or balance it out. Listening to 2 songs and than have 4 advertisements isn't good. Also, when I play an artist, I want to hear songs from that artist. I started the TLC Playlist and the first 3 songs were Michael Jackson and than 2 Outkast songs back to back but NO TLC!
author: Rose DiFonso

text: after they added ai to the app, the quality has fallen so much that I am no longer willing to use it. the algorithm based playlists have started only focusing on one genre of music even though I listen to many. premium is too expensive. the free version is too spammy and has literally no liberty. and my app started pestering me when I turned off premium. offers of the next month being heavily discounted and popping up playlists that beg me to stay.
author: Nunya Business

text: Gosh, the ads are OUT OF HAND! Gosh, it's every three songs, a new ad comes up. I can't listen to my music without hearing, "Buy this." "Get that" like, no I don't want to pay 13 dollars for a premium package! Lower the darn prices. I would consider it if it was like 7 bucks, but THIRTEEN?! You're insane. And for what? Some music?! I don't know. This is just stupid.
author: A Walker

text: What was once a great music app has been turned to absolute garbage by letting AI take over. Spotify wrapped is super inaccurate, my "on repeat" playlist features songs I have listened to one time and doesn't even show the ones I literally have ON REPEAT. Shuffle shuts off the second I switch bluetooth devices. And as of late it's constantly stopping. I use Spotify daily and now it's suddenly "you're offline" with full service. Sometimes it lasts a few minutes, sometimes it's several hours.
author: S White

text: Horrible irrelevant convoluted GUI. In the playlist/queue you can only scroll forward, you can't scroll up, can't see songs already played or go back to replay them without pushing the back button a million times. When I search for a song, Spotify gives me a list showing the same song over and over and over again. Would also be very nice to see original release year and album title of songs both in the now playing screen and when searching. Can't delete history!
author: J Dit

text: The features for free vs premium are extremely inconsistent and constantly change without notice. I started using the app a lot more once i could pick and choose songs earlier this year, but now that feature is missing again for the free tier? The only thing keeping me on this app is the fact i haven't seen one with the same collection of songs, and the fact that youtube music got rid of their related songs tab. Still less ads than regular radio most times
author: Kimberly Howland

text: I absolutely love this app! it works in every aspect of my music needs and it's easy to navigate. I listen to music almost every waking hour, if I'm awake music is playing from this app. The audio quality is amazing and I freaking love the premium benefits that it offers!! the music library is mind blowing, I guess in a nutshell, I STRONGLY RECOMMEND this service! I've tried other music streaming platforms and paid for their "premium" service and I'll never go back!!
author: Greg Stroud

text: app used to be REALLY good, but now it has all sorts of issues. rarely plays when I tell it to through headphones or earbuds, doesn't show the correct name for the audio that the app is playing, sometimes doesn't even let me go to my DOWNLOADED playlists when I don't have service. if I didn't already have so much set up through this account I 100% would not be paying for it.
author: G T

text: Used to love it but now, I can't even get into the app and listen to music after this stupid update. Even my playlists won't work when I go to at least see what songs I loved to write them down and it acts as if my playlists are empty. This started when I went to put music on and on its own, started skipping songs repeatedly when all I did was press play on my playlist. Its not the wifi, I tried uninstalling and reinstalling, I reset my phone, reset the app, even tried making a new account.
author: Jayden Allison

text: I'd write a 0 star review if it was at all possible. I've had premium for almost the last decade now on and off. it started out as a good deal allowing me to do some extra. Initially Spotify free didn't even have ads. that was it's draw from Pandora in the first place. Then the premium was to remove ads and was a good price. The free also still had single ads you could watch for uninterrupted listening afterwards. Now the free experience is absolutely painful and almost unbearable to use.
author: Japhrey Veglahn

text: It hardly ever works on mobile for me anymore, let alone with my wifi. It will sometimes not even load anything in and just be a black screen like it is tonight. I don't know if this is a my device thing, or if other people are experiencing too, but it is straight buns. Also, premium is really only worth it to pay for if you're getting the student rate, in my opinion.
author: Amelia

text: this app is absolutely terrible, though they give you a temporary time to have a bit freedom which is just picking music only. I get 4 ads and once I'm done I switch to a new song and get another stupid ad. wanna fast forward music to the part u like, wanna skip 3-5 unskippable ads that are 15-30 seconds each, wanna choose which song to listen from ur playlist, wanna be able to repeat ur music automatically, wanna have ur songs play in order, well too bad u can't, u gotta be a premium person.
author: Tenzin Herico (O5-1)

text: Sucks! used to be able to turn off shuffling now you have to pay to turn it off and control your playlist! When I play an OST for a movie or game I want to listen TO THAT SOUNDTRACK ONLY! Not get random songs that are "similar". I only reinstalled recently because I can't afford YT music and it's features like listening with the screen off! Do better damn it I'd rather fork out the cash and pay for you tube music again damn the expense!
author: Starboy6201

text: Just keeps getting worse. I can't even listen to a Playlist anymore. Spotify randomly play unrelated songs, I have to go back and pick a song in the list, turns out that's limited now, and then it keeps doing that until I run out. There's no way to turn that feature off, and now I don't even want to use Spotify for music anymore.
author: SampledPlains72

text: Algorithm is repetitive, I have over 3,000 songs and they play the same 20 songs no matter if you switch it up. Drake just dropped an album, basically forcing me to listen to his music that's stored in my library, the last 3 weeks. They promote, individual listening experience but they really just promote and push whatever is popular.
author: Sauce Gallagher

text: If my phone is set to battery saving mode, Spotify will take its sweet time connecting to the internet. They keep raising the prices and the quality just keeps getting worse. Ai generated slop floods every genre, Spotify happily recomends this tripe. Even if you have premium, Spotify shoves ads down your throat when listening to podcasts. They also boast a long list of audio books "included in premium" but only give you a set amount of hours a month to listen.
author: Ian McBones

text: terrible since the last update.... I've been a premium user for years, and it's the most used app on my phone. I never had so many issues with the app, but it's almost unusable at the moment. Very long lags, offline message when my WiFi connection is indeed strong, it crashes so many times that I give up waiting. For £13 a month, it's not currently worth paying for, so PLEASE FIX IT 🙏 (Samsung Galaxy S21 Ultra)
author: Brigitta Banyai

text: I love having a music streaming platform with everything in one place, but the last few months the app hasn't been running properly despite my many efforts to repair on my end. Changing between playlists or pages in app, it takes forever to load. Says I'm offline when I am right beside our working wifi. It's become very frustrating to the point where I am considering another platform.
author: Chantelle Tuck

text: Too many constant and bad changes. Randomly makes the "add songs to playlist" feature look really terrible and user unfriendly, also randomly some accounts won't be able to choose songs or add things to the queue when others can (all free accounts). And some accounts get 4-5 ads in between every single song when some only get one ad after a few songs. Why would I pay for an app that I can't expect consistency from?
author: Skye Stotts

text: the app is great and all, but the ads. They say you won't get an ad for 30 minutes but it's like 15 minutes instead. One cool thing is you can make a queue for your songs BUT you can't organize it and if you accidentally press on a song in your Playlist, IT DELETES YOUR WHOLE QUEUE LINE oh AND you have a limited amount of times you can make a queue for a day; so if you accidentally get your queue deleted, you'll probably have to wait another DAY to make a queue
author: Gen Flair

text: At first, I enjoyed it before this current update. But now, it's getting greedy and unenjoyable. We have to pay for Spotify premium for basic interactions like choosing songs to play, I thought WE are the ones choosing the songs?? why is it suddenly limited? And to skip songs? It's just absurd to me and alot of listeners. And the ads are increasing, It's disturbing our listening experience. I suggest remove the limitations and decrease the ads.
author: Jefferson

text: I adore the app, but at this rate going to YouTube is better lately as I can at least skip ads, no premium needed. Not 1 ad, not 2 but now 3 back to back ads?? Couldn't get a little more greedy now could you! Make it easier for the people who can't get premium. We are listening to more ads than actual music! And the ads suck just by the way, so uninteresting. Still love the app, but please just remove at least one damn ad, or at least allow us to skip it. Don't get greedy now, you are making $$$
author: Storm Hanger

text: if I worked at Spotify, I'd just revel in the fact that it was a money factory. instead they're forcing AI half-baked into it, forcing videos into the podcasts with seemingly no way to have them not being videos as the default, and generally diluting the efficacy of the product with each new update. I mean it is so much harder to find my music on the homepage now than it was 18 months ago. I'm about one more downgrade away from switching entirely.
author: Charlotte B.

text: I use Spotify on the daily but I have been unimpressed with a few things like the spike in prices and that fact prices are advertised as one thing but are actually another thing, oh and on top of that there are occasionally Ads that are not part of the podcast, why are there ads in something that I pay to not have them (occasionally keeps crashing on my phone)
author: Trickster- -Fox

text: why is it every time I'm trying to add a song to a playlist it won't add no matter how many times I click and then finally it adds after the 30th try? is it starting to get really annoying and I would like to be able to add songs to my playlist love songs I hear that I like that I think would fit my playlist but I can't because I'm always constantly having to click the add button over and over again just to get no results. please fix this it's so annoying
author: Matthew Roberts

text: Decent app for pods as I'm not an iOS user. All these years later, I still miss Google Pods terribly. I subscribe to multiple pods, but they're not all listed in my podcast menu... only eight of the pods are listed. Perhaps they display properly in the paid version. However, I'm not inclined to pay for an app to listen to pods.
author: Drew Iturbe

text: I've been a dedicated Spotify user for more or less 5 years now. I went from a free package, to student premium, then to regular premium, and now I have duo premium. I have seen it change and adjust and come into its own, and it gets better every time! It keeps usability at the forefront of every update and I love all the bits and gadgets that they add but I am not pressured to use any of them!
author: Azariah Bates

text: ads have gotten better for me, only one or two THANK GOD , but they're still not "thirty minutes no interruptions!" I love how many songs there are and I don't really mind the new songs being mixed on , but maybe a genre system would be nice, like being able to select only certain kinds of genres to be mixed into one playlist so it's accurate, because I keep getting almost rock kind of songs in a playlist with Miku and cave town-
author: Cat Turner

text: Wish I could rate it lower. I've been using the free version for a while, seeing if it got better and worth the paid version. Nope. Now it plays podcasts in the WRONG order, from newest to oldest instead of oldest to newest which would skip to the next podcast. Now it will stay on the same and replay episodes even if they are marked as played. Uninstall and refresh, clear cache etc does nothing. Time to find another app.
author: C D (Stunt)

text: I'm fed up with the captions. I've spent hours trying to turn off these annoying captions but nothing works. I want to see the artist cover and not these distracting badly synced AI captions. Searching for solutions I've noticed that this seems to be a problem for years. Isn't it time for us as customers to decide whether or not we want the captions?
author: Kerstin Peters

text: Poor UI. Ads pop up on premium (disguised as something I might like). Ads on podcasts. Often unable to access downloaded library when offline. No option to disable sync between devices. Buggy, especially when listening to podcasts. I tried sync between devices and that's buggy. Downloaded huge video files without my permission and had to find settings to change that. Randomly changes settings like when I unplug my earphones, now music keeps playing but silently?! Pays musicians a pittance.
author: Alex Harford

text: This app keeps crashing despite clearing the cache, it is still crashing. The advertising is annoying louder than the music or podcast. This is really beat the purpose of being a background music. Jolting to say the least. I'm better off listening to my own music or using other players. 2026 update: totally giving on on this app, hangs all the time, will not even open, uninstalling.
author: Wenny Sumarni

text: Recent 'improvements' include the addition of ambient sounds to certain tracks, all of which sound like they were produced by a tone death masochist. Spotify, don't overcomplicate stuff. Sure, the GenAI features are cool (excluding DJ and it's fetish for recommending songs that are like baking a cake) but don't try and change the core product. Don't even get me started on Spotify wrapped these days.
author: Josh Milligan

text: So they finally gave the option to disable smart shuffle, I can rate Spotify more than 1 star now. Always loved Spotify, and just hated that smart shuffle. It would be amazing if y'all could add a way to let us know when a song is removed from a playlist. I just recently went through and noticed multiple songs that weren't there anymore. Assumably due to them being removed off Spotify. If there was something that showed us what songs got removed, we could easily look for a replacement.
author: CM

text: Would get 5 but some quirks. there seems to be no way to move songs around in liked songs in an order you would like manually unless you load them into a queue or make a playlist. i download songs and sometimes the flow is annoying but you cant change this. Also, so many tracks become unavailable in my lists or some albums by bands are not available presumably because Spotify decided they were botted so they don't have to pay them. edit * its 2026 and I think I'm done with Spotify since 2007
author: GARETH HALL

text: The app has been great but recently with new updates it's become cumbersome to deal with. I like the Spotify DJ, but the app somehow turns off the DJ and switches automatically to Daily Mix 2 which I don't like because I have to keep monitoring my phone now to make sure DJ is up and playing music when I don't skip songs often. I didn't use daily mix In the past because it's too short and I need something to keep going while I'm at work. Edit: this fix they suggested in their reply doesn't work
author: Walter Beaver

text: So most of the technical issues have been dealt with and this app seems to be running pretty flawlessly these days. I would recommend this to most people but especially anyone into Deathcore as it seems to have the best selection of current and past artist within that specific genre. The only reason I'm not giving 5 Stars is I hear the Artists don't make what they should. Who really knows though. Enjoy.
author: Caymen Sanderson

text: It's a really good app, featuring all kinds of music, from old to recent and every genre. I really like it. But the thing is when one hasn't subscribed to the premium features, he's deprived of many choices. That's understandable, but then part of the restrictions is that there's a limit to how many times you can choose a song from your library in a day. Another thing, the app tends to play or switch to songs that are not in my catalog while I'm playing a song stored there. it's infuriating.
author: Muhammad-Sheriff Omeiza Abdulmajeed

text: When things go wrong on the app, like ads appearing when you're paying for Premium, it's very difficult to contact anyone to resolve the issue which is unacceptable when you pay £11 a month. This happened to me recently, and when i have more time, I will be considering finding an alternative, especially if I can find a way to import my playlists.
author: J PCKP

text: The U.I can be better. The search button should be at the top. Next, the automatic shuffle. I think the tempo and most dominant sound in a playing song is how the next song is determined. But it's not as efficient as YouTube music. Oh! I really love how that I can enjoy podcasts without ads and also download episodes to listen offline, no matter what plan I'm on. Spotify really did great on that one. I also love the captions for podcasts feature.
author: Chukwudi Favour

text: Constant popups for premium, can’t skip without long ads, can’t play albums in order, and they have even stopped you from playing your “liked” songs. I mean, how could you stop us from playing our favorite song. So what's the point of this app then? It's better to listen to the radio, at least they'll play the latest songs. The free version is complete trash now. I’m planning to switch. Let’s see what we can find.
author: Priya Ghosh
