text: Recently, it feels like the premium subscription is offering less value for its cost. One thing I recently discovered was that there is a limit on audiobook listening hours, and they will try to get you to pay extra to continue listening (this is with the premium subscription). I don't remember seeing this when I signed up, so if audiobooks are part of the reason that you want to sign up, I would recommend looking at other options. Also, it's hard to find user-created playlists now.
author: De la Rey Myburgh

text: First off, I love Spotify. Second, I play one playlist, and an ad says enjoy the next 30 minutes of uninterrupted listening. Then, halfway through listening to my song, I decided I wanted to swap playlist. As I do so,I get another ad? Also, I love having the free skips, but I feel like maybe have 10 skips instead of 6? I also wish I could download one of my playlists, but I dont want to pay for the Spotify Premium every month. Overall, I love Spotify and will keep using it.
author: Amelia

text: Managing the Queue is hit and miss. No direct menu option for it. Can add a few titles only to find, later, that the queue has been wiped. Can't change the order of play by dragging and dropping. EDIT: A year later and queue Mgt is still a mess; ye, can now change order easier, but it still seems to appear and disappear at will. Downgraded to 2 stars just to signal continued disappointment.
author: Nigel Ingledew

text: It keeps repeating the same songs in my playlist over and over again even if my playlist is 27 hours long. There's many songs for it to play, but it just plays the same songs. It's ruining my listening experience. Plus, I can barely even listen to songs without being asked to buy premium. Such frequent ads.. it's like this isn't a free listening app anymore.
author: Anika

text: This isn't a critique of the content. The app and company are dissapointing to me. I've paid for an add free experience but for some reason they think it's okay to pepper podcasts with adds. It makes me feel betrayed. I'll be jumping ship the moment I find a better option. Re: the reply I just got... I'm not sure what you misunderstood about my post. I paid for an add free experience. You've included adds in some of your content. I'll be shopping for a truely add free experience.
author: Chuck D

text: If we can add a playlist from the queue, add a "remove playlist from queue" option next. It's so annoying; I accidentally added a playlist from my queue, and whenever I try to listen to another song, that queue immediately plays. Also, can you all add something that will display the genre of the song? It will be very much appreciated.
author: Dhaniel Lyne

text: Overall I'm happy but the issue of autoplay for podcast. If you listen to one it then goes to your autoplay list & there's no way to get it off. While driving, an episode of a podcast I like & follow finishes then starts an episode of a podcast that I may have listened to once but did not like. I've combed through the settings & there's + ways to like/add a podcast to your list but none to remove it from your autoplay. Fix it!!
author: Tasha McNee

text: Bro!!!!! Too many ads! It's really annoying. So many ads! They are coming after just 2-3 songs and that's too 3-4 or 4-5 ads! This is really annoying and if this continues I'll delete Spotify and I will tell my friends also to delete Spotify! I appreciate Spotify in quality but this is too much! It's our choice to take a premium or not. Why are you forcing us by making those many ads!? Really a bad service bruhh 😮‍💨😒. I hate Spotify nowadays.
author: Nayana Jyothi

text: listening to music - great! However I hate the fact that spotify are pushing their own ads inbetween podcasts. As I've already paid premium, you're now making me pay again. And no, this isn't ads included in the creators podcast, they are played separately before and after the podcast. No point paying for premium if you mostly listen to podcasts, purely because Spotify feel like they haven't made enough money out of you. Oh and trying to charge 8.99 for extra audio book hours is just plain greed
author: Tom H

text: It's been going on very well, everything is perfect, but my account is signed up using my phone number, WITHOUT any emails, so when I go to the official site to log in through the phone number to add an email, it doesn't work, and keeps saying "Check your phone number." Petition to actually make us able to log in using phone numbers, Spotify😞...
author: Zeina Mostafa

text: I can guarantee that I'm not the only one to mention this, but please let us play music from the watch speaker, there is literally no reason not to. It would just make it so good to randomly vibe to music without a phone or when you're playing sports etc. Having to play music from the YouTube app is so annoying as it just randomly powers off. Other than that, great app no complaints 👍
author: Tim Tekavec

text: To say that this app is greedy would be an understatement. You can't even choose the songs you want to listen to, ads are everywhere and it will continue to shove it into your face. You can't skip, you can't make a playlist where it's not on shuffle, and it's genuinely the epitome of a prison if it was an app and if it arrested you for not being on premium. I would rate this 0 star if I could.
author: Kinzy xoxo

text: Too many stupid ads, and there's also some sort of bug that Spotify says you'll get 30 minutes of no disturbance from ads, but the next song I put I need to watch an ad and it doesn't stop here! on my phone, I can't replay the same song I was listening to! maybe I misunderstood some lyrics, I can't go back then. There's also another problem still with my phone where I can't see the songs I add on a playlist, and whenever I want to add a single song, the app shows me an entire playlist. 1 star.
author: Gift Destiny (Bona)

text: Great way to listen to music and affordable pricing for premium subscription. Edit: Everything in this app is good and conveniently adjustable to one's needs, except the damned "smart shuffle". The radio is more than enough to discover new songs. When I want to shuffle/unshuffle my own playlists I could not give half a damn about discovering new songs. Please give us the option to turn it off or on in settings, the same way you can toggle all the other features you provide.
author: Ruben “DJ Rubicon” Strooh

text: For the last few months I haven't been able to play liked songs for a majority of the time. I can play them by searching for them or playing from a specific playlist but not being able to listen to the liked songs playlist is very frustrating and defeats the whole purpose of the app. Occasionally it will work for a few days then stop again. I've deleted and reinstalled the app and it doesn't fix anything.
author: Dylixia

text: used to be better. you have to pay for basic features now. you can't make a playlist without it adding unwanted extra songs. I hate it so much. when I want to play a song, it starts playing suggested songs instead of the thing I want it to play. awful. still decent app, though at this rate, youtube is better.
author: evie

text: Don't waste time with the free version, fork out for premium. Free version is awful, no way of sugar coating that. Do not recommend the unpaid version at all. Premium however is great! There's a huge library of albums, playlists, podcasts; it's particularly helpful being able to download songs for offline play. The Discovery Weekly playlist can be hit or miss but is greatly appreciated all the same! Some performance issues, it must be said. And we could really do without the AI junk
author: L.J. Turner

text: Even their free version is abysmal. Yes I know they're trying to force us to buy the premium version. Byt omg. I tried to play one song. It offered me a selection instead and wouldn't let me skip along to the ONE song I wanted to add to my likes songs. I've used up all my skips for the month now. I'm going back to Amazon music for another try over there because Spotify makes me so angry
author: Marion Jones

text: super buggy app, constantly switches audio sources (computer, car, phone, etc.). no control over the default "home" screen/cannot specify library as home screen. app feels like you are being marketed to, as they are constantly attempting to shove whatever drivel they can down your throat. will be switching to another provider as soon as I can.
author: Keith Nixon

text: not sure how it's possible in today's technological age but every week this app just gets worse and worse and less user-friendly. opening up the app itself now takes anywhere from 10 seconds to over one full minute to get the main screen to pop up, and after testing it a couple times, hitting the search button gets you a blank screen for 2 plus minutes before the actual search bar pops up giving you access to look for something. a reinstall does nothing to fix this.
author: Sarah Birkeland

text: Not sure what's going on lately, but the app keeps crashing and freezing. I uninstalled it and reinstalled it and now can't even log in. I'll have to cancel my subscription at this rate. Getting fed up with not being able to use an app that I pay for. The error message tries to blame my Internet, but it's working fine.
author: Jo

text: Spotify is great - had premium now for years, recently though despite having offline access, the app refuses to load my downloaded library or allow me to play anything but a few select songs that I can find only by tapping on every single one I have saved. Keeps doing it after multiple reloads with the most updated version. It's almost like offline access breaks the mobile app's ability to do anything.
author: Darcey Quinn

text: I am not paying for premium, Im making that known. If I click on a playlist it'll play a song or two then just start playing random music! Its so frustrating, because all it does it play different music and completely forgets what you were playing before!!!! I am so mad because Im just trying to listen to a specific playlist. :(
author: Gabbi Cravens

text: The app will regularly try to fetch data about my downloaded albums from the internet even when I'm completely disconnected or have an inconsistent connection. The only way around this is to turn on airplane mode or put Spotify on offline mode, neither of which is something I'm willing to do every time want to listen to music on the subway. Apple Music is worse in every single other way but I will have to go back because working downloads are absolutely essential in a paid service like this
author: Dylan Bulfin

text: I'm not sure what caused this glitch to be happening constantly, but whenever I try to play something and then skip to the song I want to hear, it stops playing everything entirely and it is getting very annoying. I'm tired of not being able to listen to anything after I skip something. Please fix this or else I am uninstalling and switching to another music provider. I am SEVERELY disappointed.
author: Brianna Mullen

text: I'd rate it higher as it does have a lot of good features but there's one thing it's lacking compared to all other platforms. A dislike button. I don't enjoy rap or other genres, and yet I can't dislike them to remove them from automated playlists or even their new "DJ" feature. I like other platforms as I can hit play and let a random radio start off a song, then hit dislike or like to tune it. Spotify however doesn't allow for that and almost forces making playlists.
author: Chloe Timmins

text: Unfortunately, Spotify keeps trying to dig its own grave. Songs are absolutely limited, and you can't even choose what playlist you want to use despite that. Unnecessary ads. Spotify used to be good, for both paying users and free users. They keep trying to pressure you into buying their premium by placing basic functions behind a pay wall. You're better off using Youtube music than this sorry excuse of an app.
author: Space Geek

text: Glad that they seem to have fixed the algorithm but really pissed that the queue is different now. Like one of the the main reasons I used premium was so that I could use the queue to preemptively skip songs without having to interrupt my listening session and now you can't do that for some reason?? I just don't get it
author: Toby

text: Crashes constantly, even with no apps running in the background. Happens for almost every song. I tried to fix with all suggestions Spotify made, cache cleared, no downloads. Nothing. It has been unstable for years. It can't be the phone, as I just got a new S23, and this is still happening. I will be cancelling my premium. Awful user experience. Sick of this. If I could give it 0 stars, then I would, because the app is borderline unusable for its primary function!
author: Ed

text: Very convenient for listening to both music and podcasts, but how come you don’t get the option to listen to songs unshuffled? Whereas if you were using tablet or desktop/laptop you get to pick whichever song you so desire and even play them on repeat, have the option to shuffle your playlist or have it play in order of your arrangement. I know it’s mostly got to do phones having the ability to be carried literally anywhere but c'mon.
author: Lyven

text: The free version has more cons than pros. You can get to listen to a whopping 4 ads before listening to a song,podcast, or whatever you listen to. I am not sure if this is only me, but the smartshuffle feature had gone out of control and started playing songs not on my Playlist no matter how much I skip.
author: Meme Mania

text: I find it pointless to have "shuffle" and "play in order" to be behind premium. It should be basic interaction that should've been free. I understand the premium for downloading music, ad free, and listening offline; but having those two behind a paywall just sucks. Like, what do you mean I have to pay for something that's free on any other app? :(
author: Diana Zhane Conte

text: Adds videos and reels to a music app that doesn't pay enough to the artists, threatens a country to pay them money and then plays victim, offers a product on constant downgrades for an expensive price + has an algorithm that doesn't work properly and recommends you stuff that's the opposite to what you play, creating misleading AI-based playlists that aren't anywhere near being good. A shame how people prefer this over better options that show love both to music and fans. Now lyrics are premium.
author: unforseen consequenses

text: I allowed my Premium subscription to lapse because I was unhappy that despite paying for Premium my New Episodes feed was full of paywalled content that I couldn't play or remove from the playlist. Now the app refuses to play ANY music at all. No playlists, no albums, no songs. I regret ever paying for Spotify, because if I had never subscribed to Premium I would still be able to play music on it, even as a free user.
author: Andrew Squires

text: I wish they'd introduce a feature similar to Apple's Smart Playlists, so I don't have to manually add songs to playlists. I am begging for a light mode option for the UI :( The dark mode is iconic and part of Spotify's branding, but it makes the app more difficult to use because I have a hard time reading the content. I would very much like an option for light mode!!!
author: Ela Kaimo

text: it's a decent app when it works, but it never wants to work properly. Currently the app crashes every time you try to search up a song. Often times the player will just stop working or show music is being played, but lacking audio through anything. Integration to smart watches also crashes or freezes and will shift priority back and forth often
author: Curtis Smith

text: App is good but glitchy. It randomly freezes my music and I usually have to unpause it a few times to get it playing again. Really annoying so I thought my Internet was the problem so I got the individual premium plan to listen ad free and offline, so I downloaded a few playlists turn Spotify to offline and to get no interruptions I turn on airplane mode. It still randomly freezes my songs, I don't understand why no Internet to interfere, good storage, new phone, app updated. I'm not sure why.
author: Danny Martinez

text: Why is no one talking about the downloads that sometimes disappear when we try to transfer from Internal memory to SD card? I just lost 5.6gb of downloads for absolutely no reason, I'm highly pissed off rn, the time and data costs. It takes to download them is... And I'm seeing everywhere that there's nothing I can do about it. Spotify has done it again my storage location was changed from my SD card to my internal memory for no reason, I have lost my downloads again, this time w/o evn ding antn
author: timi seriki

text: Spotify supports ICE by advertising pro ICE ads. That is completely unacceptable. The app itself is good. Great library of music. The new shuffle system sucks though. Its a shame because I want to give a much, much higher review because I have very much loved my experience on Spotify for the past few years, but I cannot ever recommend an app that supports riping families apart and bringing terror to people. Shame on you Spotify.
author: Left Handers Rule

text: It's fine, until you finish a pod & it starts playing a random pod that you've never listened to or don't like. There is no way to remove certain podcasts from playing, pls add a not interested button for podcasts! Also paying for premium & only getting 15hrs of audiobook play is a laugh. Barely 1 book in most cases, barely 1 week of listening to/from work on my commute, then oh no, have to wait another 3 weeks to finish it. Forget listening while you work. The time top up is a rip off.
author: Stacey Micallef

text: Amazing app on all aspects. Huge collection of data, songs and artists of various styles of music and in different languages. Originality and richness of playlists based on your favorite artists and taste of music, sound quality and many many other good options. I actually tried different apps like Anghami and that was a very bad experience, believe me Spotify is far better in every way, even what you get with the free version is enjoyable and might be sufficient for many.
author: Tony Montana

text: My queue will glitch with multiple of the same song, I'm talking 5-7 of the same in a row. It's been doing this for well over a week. I also wish there was a way to hide recently played collections from the homescreen. Other than that it does what I need it to, but hate that the price keeps going up. Also BRING BACK THE HEART FOR LIKES PLEASE
author: Loreena

text: Generally all good but when listening via a smart speaker or android auto the app does not stay up to date with what's being listened to unless you open the app. So for example if I spend an hour listening but don't open the app up to sync then it thinks I'm still on a song I listened to an hour ago. This is especially frustrating especially when using DJ or a playlist as it will just cycle through all the songs you previously listened to as it doesn't know you already listened to them.
author: Christopher Terry

text: I really love spotify, its my only music app i use, but... the only problem are the ads, i get way to many often after 1 song. after 1 song i get up to 3/4 ads almost everytime, i wish it was only 1. and i really don't want to buy premium only because it cost money, like every app, and i prefer to not pay on apps.
author: McKenzie Herren

text: Lee been having fun with this appListening to my own music but one day The menus of Albums or artists profiles changed For some reason when I go into my playes I have to press a green play button and I can't even listen to the songs I wanted to hear! And plus I can only Watch 6 song The playlist gives me meaning I can't even listen to my own song I want to hear I'm so mad
author: Mikhai Jackson

text: I downloaded the app for both the music and podcasts. it has been just under 5 years. Stop trying to fix what's not broken. I completely understand the need for ads but do they have to jump in and overlap stuff you are listening to like an old 1990s radio DJ talking over a song? For podcasts, why did you get rid of the new episodes list? Do you even understand the problem it creates for people who listen in their vehicle? Time to search elsewhere...
author: Martin Wood

text: Spotify is an amazing music streaming app!🎵🎶 It's got an insane library pop song, playlist , and podcasts that cater to every note and test. The discover weekly and Release Rader playlist are always on point, introduction me to new artists and tracks I love. The UI is sleek and user- friend, making I want to listen to . Overall, Spotify is a must-have app four music lovers! ☺️🫶
author: Mayank Sarkar

text: The only paid subscription application I have continued to perpetually maintain. It's versatile, and can be used in a multitude of ways. I use it across multiple devices with pretty seamless functionalities. For instance, the echo dot which is placed in my bathroom. "Alexa play Spotify" is a nice option when already in the shower. A Galaxy Tab mounted on my Dashboard with access to the internet via hotspot. Ghetto fancy fam.
author: jaymes harmon

text: I absolutely love Spotify, there's not a whole ton of ads for me and I can still listen for free. I like the Spotify wrapped feature at the end of the year and I love the fact you can see your top songs and artists. Also the mixes are just amazing that they make including all my favorite songs and a few new and suggested ones!
author: Paylan Almond

text: It has had such potential for so long... But falls short on so many things! This review box doesn't have enough characters & I am over trying to convey this to someone important who wants to listen (tried beta vs, tried online suggestions, etc). Was switching to SoundCloud then the audiobooks were added! Still think more audiobook hours should be available on premium - 15hrs = 1.25 books/month & unfair some podcasts added to audiobook category & therefore included in this 15hr allowance.
author: Clare Hooper

text: Since the last update all my followed podcast settings have changed from only showing unplayed episodes to all episodes. You now push through requests for extra payments into my new episodes playlist despite the fact I already pay for premium. I can't even edit my new episodes playlist to remove them, not a premium service anymore, time to look elsewhere.
author: Glenn Abbott

text: I've used Spotify constantly for years now with premium but I hate how expensive it's gotten and that some features aren't in premium (uninterrupted podcasts, limited audio book time) which is a little annoying. Another one is when I'm using offline mode and every now and then I'll get a "log back in to confirm you're a user/still using offline" and making my music unplayable. Annoying but okay
author: Cam

text: Generally a good app, but in the past few weeks I've grown increasingly frustrated how the "more" menu in Your Episodes has changed so the Add to Queue option is "below the fold." Share, Remove from playlist, & Download already have their own buttons, so why do they appear above the fold? Please give Add to Queue its own button outside the "more" menu and/or put the options with external buttons at the bottom of the "more" menu. And add a feedback option in the app. Samsung Galaxy s24 Ultra
author: Christina Christian

text: This app used to be great. Then they got greedy. I have a paid account, yet my homepage is constantly inundated with advertisements for trashy podcasts that have nothing to do with anything I ever have or would listen to. Like so many other companies these days, they have begun shoving their stupid AI in our faces, to the point where they rearranged a perfectly good UI to try to push their playlist creator. I -never- use that, and now it's in the way. I always keep the app on offline mode now.
author: Lily Elrick

text: It's okay, I guess but like when there's an advertisement I press the dislike button and it says it improves my ad experience but it doesn't. It just puts more ads. There's also this glitch, where if I stop playing it it continues playing. I really, REAAALLLYYY hope you fix this spotify edit: Please show how many skips I have left so i can skip the ACTUAL bad songs
author: master vein

text: extremely ticked off that you removed the ability to remove songs queue in a PAID premium subscription. for this much money I should be able to remove songs from queue. fix it. not only that but shuffle doesn't work like it used to. I get the same songs all the time and artists in a row. that's boring. the whole purpose of shuffle is to shuffle, and get a variety of your playlist. you messed that up too. fix it.
author: Kathy

text: Music is OK, but podcasts are buggy and crawlingly slow (eg 20 minutes to open new episodes, then another 20 to open 'My episodes'). Hopeless. So much so, as to make the app virtually unusable. Samsung Galaxy s21 fe 5g all up to date, plenty of space, FAST. IT professional!
author: Rob Cairney

text: Update - Really enjoying Spotify, seems more stable, audio books are a big plus. *Feature request* in android auto you cannot see the full title of the song playing. Can the title be made to scroll/ticker tape. I have the title showing on the main screen and also in the driver's console but both are static and only show the first few words.
author: Mark Ogden

text: I have always loved spotify, but I must say the most recent update just ruined the home screen. There used to be tons of suggested playlists, "made for you", etc. The only thing that remains are the six most recently listened to playlists. The rest has been replaced by autoplaying snippets of random songs or playlists that i have literally no interest in, and there is no way to turn this off. Please give us the old home screen back, i am absolutely disgusted by this update.
author: schlauhonk

text: I have paid for Individual Premium since 2022 and have never had a problem out of Spotify. They've always worked great. I've recently started listening to their audiobooks though, and to my suprise you only get 15 hours a month of listening time, and since I work 8 hours a day 5 days a week, I can easily use 30 to 40 hours a week, and up to 120 or so hours a month of listening time. Due to this limited amount of hours I will be changing to a 4 star rating. I love the music side, but not this.
author: Xavier Ridenour

text: Pretty good app. Play lists & expansive music library. Offline playlists are great Only grips : - for duo/ family accounts you have to enter your shared address EXACTLY (eg rd not road) as the original family - they don't post small pay artists fairly / proportionally for streams. - You can't seem exclude a search word from results - sometimes Spotify playlists/radio songs aren't in keeping with the suggested artists / genre
author: Tonya Gr

text: Overall a great & useful app that not only connects to Discord, but can be used as an alarm too! MY ONLY ISSUES ARE.. If you go to local files, it'll gave a 70/30% chance of closing. Also, with local files, if you rename the file, it'll stay there. That's pretty annoying, but besides that, everything is coolio!
author: Sketch Book (Latte SimpTM)

text: I have premium just to use it offline... but it takes forever to load my libraries when I have no service even when I got a bunch downloaded. I found the audio books but that is completely pointless since you only get 15 hours of audio book time.. most books are over 40+ hours and to buy 15 hours more time it cost about $15. so each book would end up costing 30 bucks or more PLUS premium membership. totally useless feature.. waaaay cheaper to just buy the audio book.. or an audible credit.
author: dustin frech

text: as a customer who pays for Premium all I would like is a seamless experience with no issues, but the app is constantly having issues, sometimes I have to close the app multiple times to get it to even load, and when connecting to my car sometimes it refuses to show the correct name of the song playing and just stays frozen on the same name, I have to force close and cancel the app to solve the issue. disappointed for paying for Premium service...
author: T Dog

text: Still love the app, but I have to say that the UI change to the queue is so intensely frustrating to me. I enjoy that it shows the album covers, but please for the love of all that is holy, separate the things I added to the queue myself from the "next in playlist" section again. The way it looks now is super messy to me and really messes with the way I use it.
author: Fizz

text: NOT WORTH IT. Whatever you do, don't get premium. The second you go back to regular, ads are quadrupled. Without premium, the ads are still bad. Every once in awhile, you'll get an option "wAtCh tHiS aD aNd yOuLl gEt 30 mInUtEs aD-fReE". It's an outright lie lol. They'll play 1 minute of music and give you 4 more ads back to back. And even if you make a playlist of 4k songs, no matter what, it'll ALWAYS play your top 40 most listened, and you'll never hear those other 3,960 songs EVER AGAIN.
author: Elizabeth Blake

text: Spotify has ruined the app with recent updates. I'm a premium subscriber and I want to be able to download playlists to my galaxy watch. Yet, playlists will only ever partially download and previously downloaded songs sometimes disappear. Interface is worse than ever. Not a premium experience - please fix app or I am going to a competitor.
author: Richard L

text: Good selection but needs a better UI like long press to select multiple songs/media. It is such a a pain that you can only move one song at a time in a playlist. When playing from an album or playlist, we need a button to start it over instead of playing from where it was left off. Swipe left to delete. The whole queue should be cleared with one press instead of clearing it twice. I don't know why the Spotify developers don't add these basic features!!
author: Tanzina Afroz

text: been using Spotify for years, never had a subscription, didn't need one as the Xbox Spotify basically gives you premium features (unlimited skips / can play any song) but with ads. had some hiccups here n there, but nothing logging out or uninstall n reinstall couldn't fix. however as of recently my liked songs playlist from Spotify isn't showing up at all unless I search for it. and it will not appear on the Xbox side bar either; it only has past songs or playlist on it. it's super frustrating.
author: RandomWolf

text: I was really enjoying this app for the 2 weeks that I had it, but the app quality just got terrible. I try to play a song from my playlist but it doesn't play the full song, only a preview of it. My playlists are also on auto shuffle but it doesn't even shuffle songs that are in my playlist. What's the point of the playlist then?! And the only way to turn off the feature is to buy premium. Never using this app again.
author: Javae Bond

text: Mostly decent except for the annoying Smart Shuffle feature that messes up custom songs I've uploaded from Spotify Windows App to download onto Spotify Android App for listening, rendering them unavailable and necessitating a re-download from the Windows App to the Android App every time. Would only give 5 stars if there is a way to disable Smart Shuffle completely when cycle toggling between regular Shuffle & normal playback order, and/or at least ask with a pop-up which shuffle to use.
author: Valonia Nightingale

text: If it weren't for the ads, I would consider putting it at five stars. when you want to play a song, ad. When you want to skip, ad. The "30-minute break from ads" is a lie. You get upmost 10 or 5 min until you get another ad or you skip. Spotify, fix your ad problem.
author: Afinegg

text: Loved Spotify for yearssss. But now, premium isn't even premium. Don't waste your money on premium bc you'll still get ads, specifically right before listening to podcasts. To make it worse, this year, they just increased the payment plan cost. Now they put in ads too. Paid for nothing. Not worth it. Please change it, Spotify!
author: Jessenia Funk

text: I love the app but have been running into issues where it is automatically turning on smart recommendations when I don't want that feature turned on. Also noticing that every time I connect it to my smart home device (ie Google Home) it resets the playlist I'm actively listening to, which is frustrating cause I'm just trying to change the device it's playing from - I'm not asking for the whole playlist to reset. If I wanted it to do that, then I'd do that myself.
author: Conor Gallagher

text: Love this app sm, and the little loop videos it plays while you have it open, it gives such a good mood. But one thing I hate is when it says you get to listen ad free for 30 mins, then plays an ad after that song. At least tell me you're gonna play 7 more ads instead of saying I get 30 mins free.
author: emmm

text: awesome selection of songs. It just sucks when you have 100 hrs worth of songs, but the same songs will play during the day when you put your list on shuffle, which is what you need to do unless you want the exact same songs in the exact same order each time. Basically, for example, you get to hear the same 200 songs all the time when you have 15,000 songs in your playlist.
author: Julie “Jules” Davoll

text: 🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟 Best music app ever! 🎶💚 Spotify is my favourite app for listening to music anytime, anywhere! It has millions of songs, playlists, podcasts, and moods — whether I’m studying, chilling, dancing, or feeling low, there’s always something perfect to play 🎧🎵💃. The sound quality is amazing, and I love how it recommends songs based on my mood and taste 😍✨. The UI is clean, easy to use, and the dark theme looks so cool 🌙🖤. Yes, ads come in the free version, but it's still worth it.
author: kanwarjit singh sandhu

text: it works but that's about the best you can say. It's missing a lot of features of other apps and the ones it does have barely work as intended. For me the absolute worst part is how slow it is. It takes forever to load and find my play lists. Was this coded by HS student??
author: Jordy O

text: I love all the different playlists on here,, they let me explore different genres and often I find music I haven't listened to in years! I also like that if I want to just listen to an artist, or an album, I can do that too! I haven't been at a loss for good music since I started using this app!
author: Sara Rupp

text: I cannot add a 6th member to my family plan. it keeps saying the address doesn't match. ...... I confirmed my own address on my own account.... then typed it in 20 different times. still can't add my daughters account to mine. it's irking me so bad. I've never had an issue with this before. as much as I've spent on Spotify over 5 years and with how much they charge, this platform should be flawless.
author: Desi March

text: Good variety of music options, particularly smaller niche bands, but I'm having issues with sound quality, especially with downloads. Muffled/static sound and inconsistent quality, which shouldn't happen with downloads. Can't find any support option on the app either.
author: Drew MacArthur

text: Always my go to for music but for some reason today it isn't working properly. Normally you can search your liked songs for a specific artist and it would queue the next song on your liked list of said specific person, but now it's acting like shuffle is on when it isn't, and playing random songs all over the place.
author: Official MadDog TM

text: The devs increased the monthly Premium subscription for a second time in two years and I'm not sure it's even worth paying for what I get in return. Such a shame, because the app is overall well made and makes for an enjoyable listening experience, especially since many of the bugs were fixed in the past year. I've been a Premium user since 2020, but now I'm considering changing streaming services because of the price of a mere music app.
author: Emma

text: I love this app. listen to podcasts every day. until today. it's doing nothing but cache. and causing high battery drain the second I open it. even after clearing cach. please fix devs. of Spotify!!! so i can resume listening. It's been great for many years till now. completely unusable now. after it's fixed, I'll update my rating.
author: Brian Eyman

text: A very large audio library providing good automated daily risk and recommendations. I have tested it in hip hop and it is the same as tidal, but it is better in podcasts is those are not existing in tidal, and apart from hip hop probably it's a bit better as title started with hip hop. However this case podcasts and maybe more with regards to other music genres. Spotify is a very good music App!
author: Vladislav Stamboliev

text: It's a great music service and I have zero complaints besides some glitches, I just really hope the music artists are benefiting the way they should off of these streams. I'm sure everyone here who has this app loves music just as much as I do and it would be saddening to see these things crashing down in the future. Thank you🤍
author: S1eepy_Naoi

text: I absolutely LIVE off of music. It is my comfort in any circumstance, so Spotify is amazing for me. However, I recently got Spotify Premium to get the better experience and it is indeed awesome. but I have one issue. I wish I could have the Spotify Duo Premium plan to give to a friend who doesn't have Premium. The fact that they have to live in the same country, with the same adress as me is a hindrance. We live in different countries. I wish I could have the possibility to have them on Duo.
author: Veronica

text: I have been a user of Spotify for many years and I thank God for this platform. Its vast collection of Christian songs, ranging from timeless hymns to contemporary Christian music, is truly impressive. The sound quality is really good. I pray that Spotify will continue to make wholesome music available so that more people, especially the younger generations, will come to know the Saviour. God bless!
author: Jerry Lim

text: I keep having this issue where the music stops playing. I can't get it to play again. I uninstalled the app and now I can't even log back in. What the hell am I paying for?
author: Jeremy Uh Oh

text: No widgets are working. I have to open the app to pause at any time. Very frustrating. If it's not one thing, it's another with this app.
author: Kayleigh Rhodes

text: I rate Spotify. Been using it for many years. I pay for it so I can't speak to the unpaid side of things, but if you like music (and podcasts) it's easy to use, very accessible and no real hidden costs once you sign up. Pretty much has everything you need, and good Ai integrations for finding similar music, you searching for new music. I use it on a daily.
author: Bronagh Balemi

text: When I first started using the app it was great, I loved being able to listen to music whenever. The ads back then were fine, 30seconds of advertisement every 30 minutes - that's a fair deal for a free user. Now I get 1.5 mins of ads every 10 minutes. That is way too much. I feel like, instead of making it more attractive to buy the premium version, spotify is just trying to make the free version completely unusable. I really hope they don't continue to make it even worse
author: Mika

text: Price is great for the content. functionality still leaves a lot to be desired. I want a playlist full of the podcasts I listen to and can't. can only add episodes, which makes no sense since I can add an album to a playlist. New widget crashes and is worse than just not having one. It's driving me bonkers. Shouldn't force something on users until it works correctly.
author: Nici Revers

text: Spotify as a service itself is good, however, the Android app is an embarrassment. Even on a decent phone, it constantly freezes, takes ages to load and sometimes crashes. I often have to restart the app simply because a playlist is loading infinitely. Also, sometimes it just randomly starts playing songs when I'm not using the app. Earlier, I couldn't get it to play my songs, and about an hour later, to my surprise, music starts playing out of my phone without me doing anything! Appalling.
author: Owen Steele

text: buggy mess edit: loses your current track so you have to go find it again, sometimes loses the bookmark for the track as well. might not matter a huge deal for music but it's a massive pain in the @$$ when listening to multi-hour podcasts. I've had downloaded files get removed (which I of course don't notice until I'm without internet, you know, the reason I downloaded instead of streamed in the first place.) There's a lot of smaller inconveniences too but these are horrible.
author: Bo Hernö

text: Just updated June 2016 and have lost my play list and saved albums. have reinstalled etc all show but won't play Total rubbish. I downgraded due to the ever increasing price. The free version is dreadful. It says you get 30 mins of music between ads. This is total nonsense. I am lucky to get 3 songs, so 10 mins. There are adverts, suggested songs, and requests to do a survey. I know they need to make money, but this is a joke.
author: Chris Bayliss

text: i have premium standard and i still get pop ups for platinum and it's VERY annoying. i thought you weren't supposed to get any more pop ups after buying but i guess not. moreover, everytime i open it, it keeps showing "you're offline" (my network is fine) and i always have to close and open it multiple times for it to actually start working. it's very difficult to upload local files as well and i thought i should have a better experience considering the amount i'm paying.
author: Pragati Rajput

text: I really wanted to like this app, but it's so slow, it's barely usable. The user interface is so frustrating, it's almost like a Monty Python skit about cyber nightmares. Simple button pushes seem to require negotiations with Sweden, over a dialup line. Podcasts I've subscribed to often just disappear from the list. And there are more and more ads, now even inserted into the middle of podcasts. I've edited this review several times, each time with fewer stars, as the app just keeps getting worse
author: D Hamilton

text: As a Spotify premium user, I don't have much to complain about. But, the one thing that made me rate this 2-stars (hopefully, y'all need to take such feedback very seriously) is the "shuffle" algorithm. It is VERY DISAPPOINTING! I left Spotify for almost 2 years and recently decided to go back to it and this made me remember why I left the app. Having 206 songs in a playlist and Spotify "shuffle-play" decides, that playing the same 23 songs is a good thing ??😤 Y'ALL NEED TO DO BETTER!
author: zamokuhle mjokovana

text: Don't use the "lossless" feature if you don't have unlimited data! Completely wiped out my total data of 25GB....my average monthly usage was 3-5GB before I tried "lossless". I usually listen OFFLINE and have been doing so as normal, but I hadn't realised that due to the nature of "lossless" it's constantly using data in the background to find the best version of the song you want to listen to. Probably a great feature if you are fully aware of the data implications. it's really rubbish if not.
author: Jinny Seals

text: The sheer greed of this app on every update never fails to surprise me. THIS IS A MUSIC APP. We shouldn't be paying this much just to listen to music (and even if I do have the money I wouldn't pay like you're forcing us to). You might as well gatekeep your songs altogether. 1. There's a limit to how much songs you could listen to. Like... 15 times or less maybe? Just diabolical. 2. at least 3 ads every 3 songs. The only good and unique thing in this app is the Wrapped. That's all. I'm done.
author: Azmina Marie Alvarez

text: Too many ads for the free version, like sometimes 3 , 30 sec ads after 2 songs, limited number of skips, can't play in order of playlist, also limited number of times where you can choose your song, I mean just let me hear in peace, I was already compromising with the insane number of ads and unable to play in order. They are just forcing you to buy premium if you just want to listen to a song you like properly. Very disappointing.
author: Shreyom Ghosh

text: why is the 3 dots next to the download button gone in albums. it's a lot harder to put songs into a playlist one by one especially when there is more than 2k songs, it starts to get laggy and sometimes it doesn't add the songs into the playlist first try, with the 'add to playlist' button it was 100% easier. I don't know if this is just a bug on my end or if it was an intended update? other than that, haven't had any other issues.
author: Grayson Fournier

text: Been happily using Spotify Premium for many good years. I recently have had to contend with advertisements and banners popping up, which is fine with any other software or app, but when you specifically pay JUST to NOT have adverts it can be quite frustrating. Edit: The advertisements are inbetween podcasts and are independent of the host's own advertising, it shows up as "Advertisement 1 of 1" right after a podcast and is not part of that podcast. I have confirmed my plan is a paid plan.
author: Tim Smith

text: it's a great app but for the free to use people it's like jail, What do you mean that we have limited access to choose music? after that we can't hear our favourite songs after a long time, and yes we can't forget about the '6 skips per hour' we don't have any free will to skip the bad songs? and a lot of ads here, I can understand about this but every time I click a new song? at this point I would rather not listen to music from Spotify.
author: ARNAV SINGH

text: can't even open it on my phone sometimes. it's not internet issues as I can still use other apps - like I'm doing right now. I get a black screen and nothing else. even after multiple reloads. all these apps are now buggy AF. then when it actually opens, the audio cuts out. there's no point paying premium if you can't actually use the thing you're paying for.
author: m n

text: This app is amazing! I have the free version and the ads aren't that bad. Sometimes I use my mom's acc because she has the premium and it's a lot better. If you can pay for it you should because it's a better experience but if you can't or don't want to it's still amazing with the free version. It has so much good music and I'm obsessed with the way it gives you a countdown till an artist drops the album or single they announced! I definitely recommend it!!! 😊
author: Kamila C. Velez Martinez

text: Getting worse and worse. Every couple of months it starts behaving like smart mode or something is on. I got all the switched off, including autoplay is off too, but still it keeps coming back. I have tried calling Spotify support but they clearly don't know anything themselves. Not better than a standard bot, plus ends with expected result: uninstall and reinstall the app. Thanks Sherlock, that I figured out myself, but it shouldn't be necessary. Disappointing! Time for a change I guess...
author: Peter S

text: After many years of using this app loyally, even as an Apple user, I decided a month ago that I was going to cancel it as I had been dwindling down my usage more and more, maybe using it for an hour a week so switched free version. I must say the amount of ads that you listen to is obscene. I get it. the world's lost the plot, people are greedy and they want more than they deserve. You can't even listen 2 your playlists song after song they shuffle the songs. Scandalous. I do NOT recommend.
author: Elizabeth C

text: Spotify sucks now, for some reason, there has been a bug going on even though I have good internet. I can't play songs and it keeps on saying "Something went wrong, please try again", And when I tried playing a song, it keeps on changing. It pisses me off so much, I'll come back to Spotify once it's fixed. I'd appreciate if you guys fix the bug.
author: Freasiah Aamira

text: Dear Spotify, Is there an update or what? I have been trying to open the app for the past three days and it keeps reading something went wrong, try again. My device is connected to the internet and I'm certain it's not a connection problem from my end because I can open other apps on my phone. I love working with music and with this, it tends to make me a bit slow. Kindly fix the issue as soon as possible. Thank you.
author: Godsloveama David

text: I've been using Spotify for about 15 years and I've never been closer to ditching it entirely. It's painfully slow, overburdened with AI, trying to do too many things until it fails at the basics. As a bare minimum for a media player, I expect to hit Play/Pause and the music plays/pauses. Spotify consistently fails at that, or takes 5-10 seconds to respond. Its cache size is huge and yet it takes forever to load the same playlist I use every day. Way too much bloat.
author: Tom

text: Spotify has a huge music library and a clean app, but using it without Premium can be frustrating. Constant ads interrupt the listening experience, you can't always pick the exact song on mobile, skips are limited, and offline downloads aren't available. It's good for discovering music if you don't mind the restrictions, but the free version feels more like a demo than a full music app. If I were rating Spotify based only on the free experience, I'd give it 2 out of 5.
author: Jana elnumery

text: The app keeps closing when I try to open some playlists or the search bar. just closes on its own even when I'm at home with good Wifi. The only thing that makes it work again for a short time is uninstalling and installing again. I do not pay for the app to malfunction like that. Not to mention songs puasing for no reasons at all, needing me to take out my phone and press play sometimes two times in a row.
author: Rachel Amber

text: was very agaisnt Spotify for a long time, because I didn't like the idea of renting music that I already owned. But, I needed to free up space on my phone, so i finay gave in. I spent 20 minutes adding artists only to discover that I couldn't just have shuffle all- a feature that literally all music apps have had since they first existed. Why would I pay to spend hours adding songs one at a time to a Playlist, when I can open my phone's music apps and hit play, instead for free?
author: Patrick Stewart

text: Constant updates that generally just make things worse and more annoying. Spotify playlists are 90% music that is already in my own playlists. So what exactly is the benefit there. New widget is probably the worse thing they've put out in years. Zero functionality. Most of the time it doesn't even load or else it continuously directs me straight to some random podcast episode even though I'm actively listening to music. oh yeah and of course the price has gone up several times in a year.
author: Conor F

text: This app has become incredibly irritating to use. The constant barrage of ads ruins the entire experience. Just when I think I can enjoy a song, another ad appears. On top of that, after about six song selections, the app stops letting me choose the songs I actually want to play. What makes it even worse is that I experienced ads playing between songs even while I had Premium. The excessive ads and song-selection restrictions make this one of the most frustrating apps I've used.
author: lokesh eshwar

text: Been using this app ever since then. However, my Spotify seems to be lagging. I usually check my listening stats but it doesn't show me anymore. It only shows me my stats for 3 seconds and something will appear stating that I should listen to some music and I can check my data later. So, in those 3 seconds I screenshot it so that I can look at it in my gallery, but I'll only get to see my top artist and song, I want to see more than that. PLEASE FIX THIS ISSUE!
author: Chloe Rompe

text: As a free user, I find this app frustrating. I understand that premium users get extra features and that free users cannot download songs, which is fair. However, the constant ads and repeated premium pop-ups make the experience annoying. What bothers me most is that I cannot always listen to my favorite songs the way I want because of the restrictions. It feels like the app pushes subscriptions instead of giving free users a decent music experience. Music should be enjoyable, not frustrating.😤
author: Supriya Biswas

text: I've been paying for premium for years and suddenly getting Spotify ads in the podcasts. I notice it promises ad-free "music" so I assume they are taking advantage of that language and just putting in podcasts instead. This is not ads inside the podcast, like they claim in other comments. It's new ads, between podcasts. Completely cheeky and absurd.
author: Mick

text: WORST music app ever! You think listening to music is an option? You think this free app won't choke you till you buy premium? Hell no! Also, the fact that if you choose one song and can't loop is crazy. And the automatic shuffle?? Jeez! What's next, no listening to music unless I have premium? Its so stupid. There are much better options for music. I only use Spotify to find songs, and that's it. Don't even waste your time.
author: Giulia P

text: I cannot download playlists anymore, I am getting "change your settings to allow downloads using cellular/mobile data while I am in my WiFi and even deactivated mobile data. I am sure it will work soonish again. the amount of such annoying bugs is ever rising since a couple of years. I love this app (I still don't want to use past tense) please get on track. please!!! I am changing from 5 to 1 star hopping that people's votes still have some meaning.
author: Thomas Schupp

text: I enjoy listening to music a lot but i think that there are way too many and too long adds. Therefore they are way too loud, it hurts my ears and i wake up at night due to that every 5 minutes. Music helps me fall asleep but the loudness is so disturbing it also causes me to wake up every time. Im sorry but if the adds could be mild volume and at least less then this app will deserve a better rating! Thank you for reading and i look forwards for an improvement in this app!☺️
author: Asa

text: Don't get me wrong, I love spotify. but you guys sometimes make youtube look like a good option. A few days ago, I had to change my subscription due to some issue, and I had to use free mode for some time. And when I tried to listen to a song, it made me listen to three unskipable ads consecutively. P.s there is a bug both in beta and stable version, where it logs you out and when you log back in, it start some random song you never even listened to. It started after I changed my subscription.
author: Ali Umar

text: Spotify's an app that I literally use everyday. Because I genuinely love listening to music, especially on this app. But lately it's hitting my nerve. Previously it was 1 add after 4 or 5 songs at max (The era everyone loved the most). And then, it became 2 adds back to back after 3 or 4 songs... Pretty reasonable. Which was okay with most of us because it seemed fair enough. But 3 adds back to back after barely completing 1 song is just absurd. Please fix this or else I'll have to switch. 🙏
author: No

text: I love the app, as always. However, in my Xiaomi 17 Pro Max the new widget doesn't appear, I can't find it in the widget list (I do find the old widget) and the notifications that you put in the app to add the widget also didn't work (I even tried to find it in settings, both in Spotify app and cellphone). And now you disabled the old widget. And Google Maps navigation integration is also not working. And offline (bluetooth only) jams would be amazing for car trips.
author: Renato Alves

text: I rated it 1 star just to get their attention, we want a comment system in songs,so we can leave a comment like other social media platforms, who's with me guys?? 👇🏻 and they should improve the jam system, like, we should get to play the same song at the same time on all devices on the jam, and anyone could control what song plays (not queue list) at the base premium version. And a trial at 0 ₹ (could be just for 7 days if you don't want to give 3 months) would be great. otherwise great 👍🏻
author: Jeet Mondal

text: spotify is absolutely good. But, one thing I really hate here is that, there's no play all songs button. I have taken premium for high quality music. But, the stupid play button here, is the headache. First few songs are okay for particular artists, but, after few play the queue trails of to other artists without playing all musics under currently selected music, even after turning off advanced scuffle and autoplay. I hate this.
author: Rajok Chowdhury

text: It's a good app, but it's very greedy for the free version. I think they're trying to force us to subscribe to Spotify Premium. I mean, what do they mean by playing an ad for every song?! It's also boring, and even the subscription process is difficult. I've tried, but it keeps failing and telling me to try again! Even the free month they keep talking about, I can't try it. Why should I pay before trying it if it's free?
author: Maram

text: The number of ads on Spotify's free version is simply too excessive. Sometimes multiple ads play after every single track, which ruins the listening experience. I understand that Premium is optional, but free users should not feel forced to subscribe because of constant interruptions. Please consider providing a better balance between ads and user experience.
author: Absar Haider

text: This is edited from my original rant of the ad problem on Spotify and it's so much worse, now you get an Ad every single time you OPEN THE APP. Basically every song is ad on ad on ad and I don't seem to even get 15+ minute ad breaks anymore. It's as if you can't listen to music without purchasing Premium which is such a let down compared to older Spotify, even a couple years ago it wasn't as much of a major problem. Disappointing.
author: Memo

text: Spotify would be a five star app if the options and decisions for free users were expanded. We can't even pick what we want to listen to from our playlists and we only have a couple skips an hour. I feel Spotify already was doing enough with this until they decided to take away queues for us as well. This is highly disappointing because now if you don't pay a subscription price of $15.99 a month, the app is practically useless. I don't mind the ads, but please at least give queues back.
author: Zoe Fisher (FISHER)

text: You cannot remove podcasts from your library if you listened for a while and you do not like it. You still get recommended it and autoplayed after other podcasts. How can I set that I do not like it and I do not want to hear it. I listen podcasts in car and it is not easy to change it once it got autoplayed. Not good app for podcasts. I am moving to different app. Very bad experience.
author: Milan Jacko

text: I hate bait and switch, which is why I deleted this app. When you say "LISTEN FOR FREE WITH SPOTIFY" but then you clearly put that item behind a pay wall, that is FALSE ADVERTISING. Specifically, I'm talking about the Hysterical podcast. Don't advertise that it's free and then say whoops, actually that one costs money. PODCAST REPUBLIC APP is way better! They are always totally upfront and forthcoming with what you do and don't need to pay for. Should've listened and not wasted time on Spotify!
author: T RF

text: I've been using Spotify for years and have been using the audiobook to help me read more, but only giving us 15 hours a month of audiobook listening time is ridiculous. I listen to audiobooks at work, as there are books I would love to read but don't have time. Even if I pay an extra £9 a month I still don't even have enough hours to last the week. It would cost me £50 extra to fill the silence in work. Also not helpfully when you use it as a dyslexic and can't actually do anything.
author: Hannah Back

text: Spotify provides excellent quality playback and song recommendations. However, it would occasionally stop playing a song if it detects connection interference. And there is no immediate option for equalizing music via the app. If such an option for equalizing music was opted-out to avoid software conflicts varying from mobile to desktop operating systems, then I understand.
author: Vaughn Parkes

text: Vibe coded slop. Offline doesn't work half the time. Which is really annoying because it's 90% of why I have this app. The app keeps resetting privacy settings. I don't want a social media app, your job is just to play music. That's it. When I'm driving and I pull up for fuel I don't want to be ask if I want to start a jam session with some random I've never meet. Especially when I've told the app to turn that stuff off repeatedly.
author: something like that

text: When you say 30 minutes of uninterrupted music at least try to follow up on that. It's astonishing I'm not able to play the songs I want, not able to skip to specific part, not able to skip to next or previous songs. The basic feature for a music player would be to play a song I want to and it's failing to provide that. Premium is supposed to have premium features, Spotify premium unlocks basic features Music player. Came here after my premium ended, free is still very trash.
author: Sangharsh Pipare

text: Spotify has a great music collection, but the free version has become very frustrating. Getting 3–4 long unskippable ads after every 2 or 3 songs completely ruins the listening experience. The ads are too frequent and interrupt the flow of music. I understand ads are needed for a free service, but the current experience feels excessive and needs improvement.
author: HARI PRASAD BASVANA

text: What a bloody bloatware this has become.. Been terribly slow for a while, now it keeps crashing every 10 seconds UNLESS I turn off "Music videos", "Canvas", and "Other videos". Will miss them so much. Customer service (self-identified to be human) is equally terrible, totally unwilling to help beyond repeatedly suggesting to reinstall the app while pretending it would not cause any data loss. Yes, it surely would, maybe have a look at your own docs..
author: Csaba Győrffy

text: Old widget was better.... A good app, I have premium so I don't have to listen to the obnoxious ads anymore thank god. Better than Google Play or iTunes. I wish I could reset my listening preferences though to start from scratch or choose not affect preferences when I choose. Also, an option to toggle "play or add to queue" on tapping a song would be nice because I always accidentally change songs when I'm trying to add queue.
author: T Girl

text: I've been a Spotify user for years and was always happy enough with it until recently. The app freezes and lags for every single action you need to take from searching, to playing, to adding a song to a playlist. Could be two, three or four minutes for it to start playing a song! I now consider it unusable and sadly need to move to an alternative service
author: Emma McClaughlin

text: A great way to listen to music and podcasts, I heavily recommend it. My only issue is the amount of ads. I don't even need dinner when they shove 4 consecutive ads down my throat. They're really frequent and now there's even an ad for when you just open the app. Really awful, but overall Its a nice way to listen to your favourite songs.
author: Iker Cartagena

text: I have premium, and I genuinely enjoy the app, but I am struggling to find songs that have been confirmed released on this platform, maybe its my region, but no information online says that certain songs arent avaliable in my country, and when I tried to find a song on the album it did not show up even though it is posted there on other's devices on the same app, maybe its a device problem but I warn others that if you want to be able to find every singulsr song to not go with this app.
author: Riley Butler

text: Spotify's free experience has become very frustrating. There are too many unskippable ads, sometimes 3–5 in a row, and basic features like song selection, repeat, and proper shuffle are locked behind Premium. Earlier, the free version was limited but still enjoyable. Now it feels like almost everything requires payment. I'm seriously considering switching to another app. If this continues, Spotify may end up driving loyal users away.
author: CHITRA TS

text: recently the app doesn't really work anymore. every time im trying to listen to music after two songs maximum the app just crashes. it starts with freezing the screen then after a bit the music starts to stutter really bad that you can't even make out what song is playing anymore and then the whole thing just crashes closes the app and even my background of my phone is completely black for a few seconds. I'm actually disappointed the premium subscription gets more expensive all the time n now ts
author: Michelle “shelle” B

text: I've been using Spotify Premium for years. other apps are fine, but when I have to play Spotify - there is a problem. looks like it's playing but I can't hear anything and restarting phone is NOT a resolution. Another thing. Spotify forced me to use new widget. Since I have it, is all the time frozen: ,SPOTIFY IS OFFLINE, TAP TO REFRESH ' I did and it's not doing anything. WHAT'S HAPPENING HERE? Why I cannot use old simple widget? I gave 5 Stars before. now- only 1. sorry 😐
author: Aneta Kowal

text: randomly stops working and there's nothing to do about it except either uninstall and reinstall the app or log out. really annoying for long trips especially when you have no data. also super slow and laggy, menus take forever to load even when connected to the internet. premium is really expensive, but the free version sucks.
author: Zara Thorne

text: I used to love Spotify and raved about it to anyone who would listen. Lately the app has been slow and drops signal. I blame it on the fact that the app now contains video. Update: Spotify support reached out and suggested a clean re-install. No improvement. I stand by my previous review. iTunes or Amazon Music looks pretty good right now.
author: Jamir Hicz

text: It's a good app, really! I can listen to whatever I want whenever I want, but you're probably wondering why I didn't give it five stars then. Maybe because of how genuinely stupid it is, you are a multi-million money-making company, and yet the amount of ads is not needed. Its as if there's one every god damn second, and don't get me started on the limited skips and how I can't actually pick what songs I want to half the time, this only makes me want premium less, to be honest.
author: Phoebe Alexander

text: I swear they teamed up with companies like android to make life harder for us, these days phones don't even have a way to play songs in your files! I tried to play a song in my files and Spotify opened??? almost all the apps to play music you already have on your device are ridden with ads and Spotify is HORRIBLE I only use it because there's nothing else. I get using ads to pay artist but not being able to go back a song or move to a certain spot in a song without premium is RIDICULOUS!!
author: Amanda Hilditch

text: Spotify is a very good app for listening music. It offers vast variety of music and podcasts but the only thing is that without premium it is very irritating u get ads after almost every song and those too long ones so if the ad rate could be improved this is 5 start i liked the older Spotify where after a add break u get 30 mins of uninterrupted music without ads but now on the newer version I can't say.....
author: Abhimanyu

text: When I was downloaded this app I loved it, because it was a free app to listen my favorite songs. But nowadays the word 'free' is completely fake, because, at first it started to contain ads but nowadays it doesn't let us to hear our favorite songs after searching some, it recommends us to join premium. How can you deprive to listen our favorite songs by telling us it's free:( But now I'm trying some other apps and those are better than this:)
author: Rajlaxmi Pramanik

text: This app has the audacity to say that I disconnected despite me having good wifi or good internet connection. I can have full bars 5G/Wifi and it still keeps saying "something went wrong, check your connection and try again" when it's already good, so I keep pressing the refresh button and it still keeps giving me the same something went wrong message. And it has been going on and on for a while, I thought it was actually my internet but it kept on doing that despite me having good wifi.
author: Rene Vincenzo Torralba

text: Spotify is great, does what it's supposed to do. However this recent update where it's informing us that the 4x1 widget is no longer going to be available and we have to switch to the bigger 4x2 widget which only adds thumbnails of playlists for quick opening. I'm all for more widgets, but not at the cost of fixing something that is not broken. I prefer having my home screens locked with similar applications stacked for a more organised look. This new widget is not even aesthetically pleasing.
author: THEDON FONZ

text: This has to be the worst music app to ever exist. There are ads every 2 songs, and the ads are more than 1—sometimes 3 or 4 ads just to listen to a song that lasts about 3 minutes. THE AD ITSELF FEELS LIKE IT'S LONGER THAN THE ACTUAL SONG. Also, don't even get me started about the playlist. When you make your own, it will literally switch to a song you don't know that isn't even in your playlist. There's so many disadvantages u might as well try to find a diff app
author: Charlotte Simon

text: I went back to premium. Not because I want to, of course. Paying for the premium leaves a bitter taste in my mouth knowing full well that the free version doesn't even let you use the most basic functions (e.g., choose your songs, rewind back to a certain part of the song, next). I would have gratefully went back to premium for the DJ if it weren't for the app being too greedy. Who would have thought I'd have to pay for the most basic functions that music players are made for.
author: Jin Kisaragi

text: In my opinion, At first I enjoy listening to songs, But as time went on, there was a limit to listening to songs, requiring payment for Spotify premium to choose songs. I thought we were the ones who chose if we wanted to listen to a song, but now we can't listen our fav song because we need to pay. We need to wait 24 hours to choose our favorite song and listen to it, I don't like your new policy, because there are others who can't afford Spotify Premium due to lack of budget.
author: cc celestial

text: Spotify is a good listening app, it has a lot of songs to explore, but the premium subscription is getting out of hand, yes, the ads don't bother me too much but I can't even play my songs in the order I want, and in my opinion, it just makes the app harder and more complicated to use, it would be randomly playing a song that I like then a song I know nothing about. Still, it's a pretty good app especially to people who enjoy listening to music. But the premium is too much.
author: Mikayla Magsico

text: Spotify is great, not gonna lie. Playlists, shuffle, smart shuffle, you can put songs on repeat and there's a bunch of great stuff. But lately it has been having way too many ads to the point that it's annoying as hell. After like 2 or 3 songs there's almost always an ad, I'm not even exaggerating. I've been using spotify since 2023 (I think) and it didn't have this many ads before. And the worst part? There's more than 1 ad in a row. Sometimes it can have 4 ads in a row which is horrible.
author: tell lemaco

text: The free version of the app is, unfortunately, nearly unusable. Ads appear once every sixish songs and it is not possible to pick the music you want to listen to. With limited skips, you really are just at the mercy of whatever the algorithm wants to serve you. I'd recommend picking any other music streaming service if premium isn't accessible to you!
author: meri

text: I have had Spotify for 10 years now. it's something I use everyday and have loved so much over the last year though it has been a very frustrating experience. extremely slow to load anything when I hit play or pause it takes about 5 or 10 seconds for it. actually to work the price keeps going up and the quality keeps getting worse! It's not just my phone. it's all my devices even after clearing the cache, uninstalling and installing again etc. still the same issue s!!!!!
author: julie mantua

text: The free version is terrible and I won't recommend anyone to use the free version. The company is really greedy and puts an ad after every 2 to 3 songs. after a certain number you can't even select a song of your preference. the playlist you created can't even run in order, there is a shuffle system which won't turn off until you buy the premium, therefore you have to manually select the songs. Worst music app.
author: Neha Gupta

text: My experience is getting worse and worse. Since approximately 2 months ago the app started to become slower, taking ages to load, play and search for artists and playlists, also quite often becoming unresponsive, even though there's enough free space available. Nothing is working so far (cleaning cache and data, logging in again, reinstalling). Currently it doesn't even open, all I get is a black screen.
author: Cláudio Ferreira

text: Spotify people, the app has a bug. After an advertisement the app stops giving me the option to move through a song. You know the slide option, where you slide your finger and you can for example be at the beginning of the song or at the end or at the middle of it, depending on your preference. Well, after an ad, you can no longer do that. So, unless this is YET ANOTHER premium feature, please fix it
author: Elina Docheva

text: We non premium listener hear plenty of ads, minutes at most, it's disruptive, repetetive, and annoying. Atleast make the selection of music unlimited it's the least you can do. If i discover other app that offers free music with free selection of music, I will replace this app with no hesitation. And of course you don't care even though you make a lot of money just us listening to the ads you make us listen
author: Venz Lapinid

text: I've enjoyed using Spotify for years, but lately it's been disappointing. The free version is packed with ads and so many limitations that listening to music just isn't fun anymore. And when I finally considered upgrading, I found out the Duo plan was gone. It was the perfect option for couples like us who wanted separate accounts without paying for a Family plan. Feels like Spotify is making things worse instead of better for its users. Bring back the Duo plan and make the free version better.
author: Bhavesh Rajput

text: This app was very good when I first started using it three years back. that time, my rating was 5 stars. And now, it's asking ₹99 premium so I can play any songs of my will. Why? At first, problem in showing lyrics, second thousands of ads in between songs which ruins the whole vibe, and now I can't even play songs from my playlists that I want next? Why? Please change this "Get premium to play any song, any it." thing. It's clearly irritating when you can't play the song you want.
author: Aditi Mohanty

text: I love it when a singular song interrupts my endless streams of ads. What I love even more is when it's a song that I don't like and realise I don't have enough skips to get past it until I get back into the endless stream of ads. In reality, Spotify is a great app with many songs and I would love it if it wasn't just so enshittified (term by Cory Doctorow). Spotify squeeze out everything from its users through making us listen and, recently, watch so many ads. It's a great app just flawed.
author: James Roberts

text: The app worked well until the last update or two. It started glitching and crashing frequently, not to mention the increased number and frequency of ads. In the last few days, the app has completely disappeared from my phone twice except for the icon. When I tap to start the app, the screen goes black, nothing is playing. It appears fine after a reinstall but crashes again. Might be time for me to look for alternatives.
author: Brigita Litte

text: I have been using Spotify for almost five years and have remained loyal to the platform. While ads in the free version were understandable, the recent restrictions on basic features like changing songs have been very disappointing. It feels as though user experience is being sacrificed for profit. As a long-time user, I expected better. These changes have made the platform far less enjoyable, and I am now considering switching to another music service.
author: Vibek Chakraborty

text: Thanks for the update. I can no longer play music. I either get a spinning green circle or I get "that didn't work try a refresh" I can see my playlist but I can't play them. I can't get at my account to contact customer support because guess what? "Something went wrong. Check your connection and try again." Uninstalled is and installed twice with reboot. Logged in with Samsung and logged in with email "magic link" the second time. No success with either. Unable to even log out.
author: Pam Buckshon

text: Spotify is hands-down the best music app out there. Its recommendation algorithm is unmatched—playlists like Discover Weekly and Release Radar introduce me to amazing new artists every single week that fit my taste perfectly. Creating, organizing, and sharing playlists with friends is incredibly intuitive, and the lyrics sync up flawlessly. I couldn't live without my daily soundtrack!
author: Tamia Naidoo

text: App is SO frustrating without Spotify premium. On my phone, I only have 6 skips per hour, which is egregious for an app about listening to music. I get 4 ADDS in a row. You'll be listening to an album and then Spotify will play 'similar songs' that aren't on the album and that you probably would've never picked before. And without premium you can't edit the queue of songs. It's a scummy practice making listening to music on a music app nearly impossible without paying.
author: Karolina Gliniewicz

text: Modern Spotify is a dystopian nightmare. Even though I'm paying an exorbitant amount for Premium, I have to wade through SO MUCH promoted content, ads for upcoming releases I'm not interested in, audiobooks I dont care about, podcasts that aren't even related to what I like. I won't even try to describe the degree of AI slop theyre trying to shove at me. Then finally, after often like 30 seconds of scrolling, I find what I'm looking for. Spotify, you should be ashamed.
author: Tim Sullivan

text: why so greedy? over the time it seems you have become so greedy. there are already plenty of ads in the free version. accepted. now the skips are limited. but you keep adding bad songs to the playlists which I don't wish to listen. and if I have made a playlist for myself what right do you have to put sings in it of your choice? then what's the point of me making a playlist? at this rate I'll have to think about switching.
author: Shubi Mittal

text: Spotify is one of the best music streaming apps available today. It offers millions of songs, podcasts, and playlists for every mood and occasion. The app is easy to use, and its clean interface makes finding music simple and enjoyable.
