I especially like the personalized recommendations, such as Discover Weekly and Daily Mixes, which help me find new songs that match my taste. The sound quality is excellent, and I can listen to music anytime, anywhere.highly recommend it to all music lovers.
author: Md Mosarof

text: its really good, no doubt (unless you dont have premium). but when i make a playlist of a podcast (just one) sometimes it loops, which i want and sometimes it goes to a whole nother podcast, which hasnt really been that big of a deal except now it closes instantly when that happens and when i try to reopen it ive tried restarting, clearing the cache and updating (update wasnt available so i guess it doesnt count) though i figured out one way to fix it quickly choose another podcast or song.
author: Toki Djurhuus

text: I’ve used Spotify Premium for a year and decided to cancel. The main issue is the recommendations. I expected to discover new, niche music, but the algorithm got stuck in a loop, pushing the same popular tracks over and over. It fails outside the mainstream. ​Also, the audio quality is lagging behind. In 2026, Spotify still lacks lossless/FLAC audio. For decent headphones, the sound is just not good enough. Not worth the money.
author: Mikhail Didenko

text: This app crashes with every video ad, which shouldn't even exist in a music app. It also made every feature a Premium feature, as far as I'm concerned, at least on mobile. Too many ads as well, and is even worth boycotting, in my opinion. I use to use it 6 hours daily. Until they revert this, I'm not using it again. 0 stars, if I could. Would no longer recommend.
author: Logan “TILTED”

text: I used to love this app, but lately it crashes nonstop. I have closed the app fully, reinstalled, ensured my phone and the app are updated, and cleared the cache but nothing helps. sad. Giving it 2 starts because when it DOES run reliably it is a great app. but also I couldn't get the student pricing to work (this crashes also). edit: dear spotify team, if you read my review you'll see I have reinstalled the app many times 😂😂 thank you 💖
author: Tee

text: Although, everything is excellent, i'm okay with ads. But, what is your problem, I can't play whatever song I like to play at any time, I can't skip more than 6 times an hour without paying for premium. Seriously, is this way to beg for money. Is this app for listening songs freely or just pay money for listening songs independently. Seriously disappointed Spotify!!
author: Aviral Arora

text: Podcasts are just ridiculous amounts of ads now. I have podcasts that they have ads pre, mid, and after the podcast. Why am I paying premium membership for this? Amazon music does the same and has no need for premium, just the minimum prime membership. The new widget is absolutely annoying. It has to load every time, and you can't open spotify from there until the widget fully loads. Bring back the older widget or, at least, give the option to have the older one.
author: Juan Perez Lopez

text: Why did they remove what's new? No one knows. For me, it was the main reason to use Spotify. After painstakingly following ALL the hundreds of artists I listen to, it was a practical list of releases by date so I could track new albums. I'm back to Wikipedia's list like a caveman. Typical enshittification of big apps, they have way too many customers so it's easy to make changes that don't improve anything. Their bottom line is not affected so they don't have to care. C suit happy as always...
author: Kevin

text: It's a great app to listen to lots of different albums. Unfortunately the search function is often misleading and forces you to be incredibly precise in naming songs. I would like a more streamlined playlist experience. For me I would like it to have classical music listeners in mind too, like organising the tracks by corresponding pieces, and having the 'movements' only be sub-sections, not their own tracks. but I'm really nitpicking the truth is I'm very happy with what there is already.
author: Cyprian Fenrych-Beecroft (sea of bruh)

text: I tried it on the one month free version and the playback did not sound right it sounded flat. The other problem was finding the songs I like, even so that Google listed it as one of the best in the prior search. I decided not to upgrade and when the trial ended I got one or two songs to one full length commercial at an even more reduced sound quality. Management at Spotify give your head a shake that is how loose customers
author: Markus Schmalz

text: Great app and I really enjoy using it. although I've been experiencing problems with the fact that I have to exit my Playlist to make songs load. it takes like 5 tries to add new songs to my Playlists. for some reason songs have been removing themselves from my Playlists(?). And I personally dislike the fact you can only access the search bar when you're at the top of the playlist now. I can't ignore these problems and for that reason I've rated it 2 stars.
author: Asiah Cadeau

text: If you sign up for an account expect the price to jump frequently for almost zero increase in service. Their song lists just play you your favorite songs back. it's like pulling teeth to find new music. If the kids didn't use it for all the Disney songs, and if I wasn't too dang incompetent to figure out how to transfer my playlists to a different app- I'd be out. The kids can't even listen to kids stories which is the whole reason I got roped into the Spotify family. Take my money. Smad face.
author: Marcedes Bossaer

text: I love this app. less ads much more enjoyable. I just love it. we can see abt the artist or see the lyrics if we want to, that's like a plus point. and it has all the music genre I like. kpop, hip hop, pop, phonk , funk and more. and without premium, we can close the app but still listen to the song we played. perfect for multipurpose. as a student myself, I love listening to music while taking notes so spotify is a go to and a must have. try it for yourself I don't think you'll regret. 5 stars
author: Navu

text: The free version has started to become a headache and is even becoming terrible to be brutally honest. After every 2 songs, there are 3 long ads, then there is the constant persuasion to get premium. We can't play songs in our desired order, can't skip more than 6 times per hour, can't choose to play any song. Please do something about this, and remove some of the restrictions users are facing in the free version.
author: Anubhuti Gupta

text: Was good until they started to play ads in podcasts even for premium users in 2025. Then, they increased the price of premium. I refuse to pay more for a worse product. And they still lie in their advertising claiming the premium subscription was ad free. Scammers! In addition, it gets slower by the day. Now, it's almost impossible to load anything in my library, too slow! Update, 19 May 2026: All my playlists are suddenly empty. I guess I'll not even use it for music anymore. Bye bye, Spotify!
author: Jul Jan

text: I find lots on this app that I'm interested in. Due to the adds and lack of replay on demand in the free version I decided to upgrade, which starts with a free trial. If you keep refusing to upgrade the app just starts doing what it wants. This upgrade was great, however, this "free trial" wasn't free at all, I was charged for it! So I cancelled it, had to dig a bit to find out how but I did! It also constantly runs in the background you have to dig to find out how to stop that too.
author: K P

text: Great app overall, but I hope they add a better quick add option to playlists. For example, when I listen to a playlist and it's done, it keeps playing similar music, which is fantastic, but saving those songs to the playlist requires plenty more clicks than I feel is necessary. I also run into a few issues where I have to basically reinstall the app in order to use it, both on mobile and PC. I hope this review is useful and brings insight to the company and users :)
author: Tristan Scharneck

text: Spotify experience is declining. Not clearly marking AI music is annoying, and the sponsored music ads for premium subscribers are what push it over the edge for me. The fact that you have to access this setting from the desktop version of Spotify is obviously intentional to make it less convenient for users to opt out. the deal pop-ups when you open the app are also very annoying. I don't need to be sold more while I'm already paying for your service. it's worth checking out your other options.
author: Stephen Scott

text: Honestly the app itself is fine, but the ads are just way too much and too often. I get to listen to 2-3 songs and get 3-5 ads back to back. And the most annoying part is that how aware they are of that. I understand the free version needs ads, I'm fine with that, but the way it is now is just overkill. It used to be better. Now they punish people that can't afford a subscription. It's disgusting.
author: Joanna K

text: While Spotify has been a great app for me over the years, it has become worse for free users like myself.First of all, the ads. The ads have become way too frequent like every 2-3 songs you get a 3-in-one ad. Another thing is the fact that you can only listen to a "sample" of a song. This is straight up bs. Also the fact that you can't play the album YOU created in the order you like. This just feels like Spotify has given all the features to premium users and left us free users with the scraps.
author: Sharlet Lynn

text: This is a pretty good music player app but there's so much frickin' ads! You have to PAY premium just to hear songs in your order, move your place in the song, and get unlimited skips. I thought that was the bare minimum an app made for playing music should do!? Also, the song order editor in playlists has improved, but can I move bundles of songs to where I want it to be in the playlist and not just top and bottom? Not terrible, but not good. 3/5 stars.
author: Clarizsa Allyn Sy

text: "Mixing" playlists is very restrictive: you don't have a lot of options aside from the preset transitions the app makes when trying to mix two songs together. Also, for some reason, the app doesn't allow you to mix songs at certain durations, which really doesn't help anybody. Edit: Local files appear to be downloaded, but the app refuses to play them when you are not connected to the internet. Also, it is extremely laggy and nearly caused my phone to crash. Please fix these bugs.
author: Yamud, Vince Hemsley P.

text: The app has become extremely slow, bug after bug and the UI is very laggy. Downloaded songs take an eternity to get registered as such, meaning that I'm unable to play them whenever I open any playlist. The search function is extremely slow and, despite a solid connection, won't work the majority of the time. Edit: THEY TOOK 'YOUR FILES' FROM US??? MY MP3s!!!
author: Juli

text: I've used Spotify premium for many years and have always enjoyed the interface and availability of songs. Recently, I noticed on both the app and Spotify web, most tracks will play for the first 10 secs and then stop. If I wait, it might continue to play after a minute or so. I tried clearing data and cache, uninstalling and reinstalling but it actually made it worse. The songs I had on one playlist (not downloaded for offline mode) was playing perfectly fine until I reinstalled.
author: Jeanie Lee

text: I use Spotify all the time and never used to have any problems. Unfortunately now I can't search anything new as it's saying I'm offline even though I'm not. It's taking incredibly long and giving 'Oops Something went wrong' whenever I move between tabs (again my wifi is fine). I thought at first it was due an update but it's not. I don't understand why this has just started happening. It also randomly undownloads songs and I have to re-download them. It is frustrating and unusable at this point
author: Robyn Moore

text: Spotify was great -- but now I suppose they've updated it, and it's become so frustrating. I can't listen to a specific song, because it seems they've all been grouped into playlists -- I have to listen to the entire playlist to hear the song I want, even with the playlists that I've created. And if I want to listen to an album, it only plays one or two songs before switching to "recommended for you". LET ME LISTEN TO MY ALBUM!!!
author: Keith Hanley

text: What the hell is happening to Spotify?! First, I'm getting notifications that I'm offline regularly these days even when I'm using data or connected to Wi-Fi and now tonight I can't use the app at all. Takes forever to load then doesn't. Uninstalled and reinstalled a few times hasn't done the trick. This is turning into a waste of money!!! Do better!
author: Sean Prentice

text: The app keeps on shutting down and crashing, over and over again. If I want to keep the music playing, I have to open the Spotify app and stay on it. Every time I get out of there and open any other app, the music instantly stops and the app shuts down! What's going on? I have been a Premium subscriber for years. I uninstalled and installed the app back, but nothing has changed.
author: William Pavoni

text: Spotify is really a great platform for listening to music, however I happened to experience a problem that has happened repeatedly on more than one definitely. This problem happens when I search for a particular song, it plays another song or other songs, which I found quiet annoying and irritating, please do see this review and improve for our experience. Thank you.
author: PhenyoC Colet

text: Great Music Streaming App. Especially across multiple devices. Free accounts are available with advertising. Which for more budget conscious people. Makes it, the more accessible platform. Out of the others. Yes ads play every 6 songs or so. But if you listened to radio. About the same. You can thumbs up or down an advertisement. If you like or dislike content. Tailor profile, playlists, and artists to suit tastes etcetera. The only downside is that every device platform of the app different.
author: Carley Tanner

text: Earlier, Spotify used to be a really good app. We had the option to either play songs normally or choose shuffle according to our preference. But now, users are just forced to listen to whatever was played earlier, with barely any control left. And the ads? Earlier they were still bearable, but now it feels more like a full-time advertisement app where, out of nowhere, you occasionally get to listen to some music in between. Would’ve given 0 stars if that was possible.
author: Gautam Srivastava

text: It's become unusable with ads. Want to listen to music? Sure, just that you must hear a 70 second unskippable ad first and it won't even be the song you chose. Wan't to look at the cover art? No. Just no, there's an unclosable ad there now. But what if you clicked the song to hear it, specifically? Nope. I mean, just ~10 times every 24 hours. Spotify is not for music anymore. Look somewhere else.
author: R

text: Paying for premium is so expensive already, and you just raised prices again. I would expect more features for a price increase. I would really appreciate more than 15 audiobook listening hours per month. At least 20 if you are raising the price. And I know not every user is taking advantage of listening to audiobooks. I hate getting cut off before I finish a book and having to wait for them to reset. A flawed system. And I'm unhappy with the price I'm paying, so much so that I may cancel
author: Emily Chiasson

text: It would be very cool, if you would make it possible to edit a playlist line up. Simply by holding a song and dragging it to where you want it to be in that line up. Right now, it's grueling with limited options to add that. As of now, it's more based around the artists ease and very little for listener preference. After going to our created Playlist -> Edit -> Check boxes for selected songs -> Move -> only Top/Bottom. There's no way to organize the personal vibe & lose yourself in the music.
author: Sarah OMary

text: It is so easy to use!!! the only problem is choosing your own music for a 1 hour song playlist on phone which is not good BC I can only choose one music and can't make it one hour. I hope they fix that and too many add but hopefully they do smth about it. over all its amazing and I use it every day!! also I want the wrapped playlist back at every end of the year I loved it!.
author: Ruby Star

text: Idk how people are still positive that Spotify is the best music streaming app there is. Unless you wanna spend all your life savings on premium, get ready for some music in between your ads. And if you actually even wanna have a working app, definitely get any other than Spotify, for your own sake. It's the 10th in 2 months that I can't open the app, can't connect to my devices like PC or PlayStation and no matter what I tried it won't work. Please FIX YOUR APP! Thanks.
author: BA23A

text: I've been using Spotify for years now, and it's never disappointed me on sound quality, both streaming with Wi-Fi and cellular data, the end of the year wrap, its all top notch. However there is something about the lyrics that disappoints me, its that some song lyrics are not properly synced and some not at all, and I've been wanting the lyrics to be synced word by word and not by paragraph, sort of like Apple Music. other than that everything about the app is brilliant!!
author: mpshindinge シ

text: I pay for the app. most songs don't have lyrics. The app doesn't work without WiFi in rural areas. Sometimes the app throws a tantrum and won't work at all. I'm getting kinda sick of it TBH. It's glitching is extremely distracting and dangerous when you are driving. It's stressful when you are trying to relax. In short, paying for the app removes adds, but doesn't make the app any better.
author: Leonard Sharp

text: It used to be 5 ads, but I thought it would get better, not worse. Now Spotify add an ads when play some music, so that's 6 ADS. it feels like 3 minute listening too ads than listening an actual music itself, This app just gets greedier every year, and the subscription is pricey. I'd rather use other music apps than this one. I hope it gets better, but I doubt it.
author: Ramadhan Surya Sutanto

text: Price increase... for months I've been dealing with podcasts, audiobooks, videos and disgusting ads when all I wanted was music. just music. and now a price increase for these things that I don't want. they really do take your music hostage... fix this app, make a setting to turn off everything else except music. or at least a setting to start the app in the music tab.
author: Rohail Shah

text: I've been using this app for years, and now it won't let me play anything. I've cleared my cache both in my device settings and in the app settings, uninstalled and reinstalled the app, turned my phone on and off, cleared the data, disabled ad blockers, disable block pop-ups and made sure everything was up to date, my data and wifi work, and it still won't play anything. However a friend can log in on my device and their account will work with no issues. So its not my device, its the app.
author: Victoria Francis

text: Spotify probably has to be the worst music app but I am forced to use it. I will be on WiFi and I will have to reopen the app about five time for "internet issues" I have done this on Enough peoples WiFi to know mine isn't the problem. also this app costs a pretty penny for premium which a lot of people cant afford and the fact you only have six skips an hour and you can't not shuffle a playlist? Spotify can be extremely greedy so if you have the choice, choose something else.
author: Eden Wilson

text: Getting slower and slower, even on a newish phone. Menus take forever to load. Setting the quality to high instead of lossless speeds it up when you're actually playing music, but doesn't help the general sluggishness of the app. Also getting worse for podcasts as they don't automatically update when new episodes come out, so I find myself using podcast addict more for that.
author: Alex Neil

text: I use this app daily to listen to music and podcasts, and while it has a great variety of stuff to listen it, the app itself has been getting clunkier and worse to use with every update. I go offline regularly so that I can listen while I am on the metro (aka underground with no data/internet). For some reason sometimes the audio just isn't available when I am offline and I can't listen to anything, even though I have downloaded the files and it worked earlier in the day. Make it better!!!!
author: Amelia Vadnais

text: Poor experience with this app. Always laggy, always slow. Even if you switched apps for 5 seconds & came back. It behaves like you just reset your phone. Every function takes ages to come back to life. Often it just doesn't recognise that my device is connected to WiFi. I've tried every troubleshooting method available. Clear cache, delete downloads, uninstall & reinstall, etc. Nothing works. It just has poor performance on my device.
author: Zaylan

text: Spotify is my absolute favorite app on my phone. The library is huge, from classic rock to lo-fi beats to the hottest new pop hits. I also love the podcast integration; everything is in one place. Offline downloads are a lifesaver for flights and subway commutes. The audio quality is crisp, the app runs smoothly, and the interface keeps getting better with every update.
author: pakai di mamesa

text: I get that you can't have unlimited skips without premium but you just made me leave the app because I can't put on a single song I choose...and I'm not going to listen to your random choices for the rest of the day. You get x amount of songs then nothing. YouTube is free and doesn't have limits...and with unlimited cell data, I may as well just switch to YouTube...until I find a better music streaming app. Nice knowing ya Spotify but do better. Not impressed at all.
author: Sarah Leopold

text: My Spotify is not working. It keeps saying “Something went wrong.” I already uninstalled it and logged in again, but it’s still the same. I even had to make another account just for it to work, but after some time, it stops working again. Please fix this, Spotify 😭 It’s getting so frustrating because I just want to listen to music peacefully.
author: Alexandra Garcia

text: Update: Crashing is fixed. Queue usage still sucks. You add an album to your Queue, then you add a second album. where does it put the songs for that next album? Why after the next song, of course! So you get one song of the first album, then the whole second album before the rest of the songs from the first. A long time trouble with this app is using it in poor cell coverage, basically useless on long highway drives. Too many menus to just put it into offline mode. This should be automatic.
author: Zandr

text: You have to save the queue!!!! I add a bunch of songs to the queue and suddenly nothing there, zero songs! I'm switching to apple or YouTube music cause this is so f annoying! I pay for the features! Also, add a "next in queue" not at the end, but as the next song, like apple has. Improve, sweetie, otherwise bye. edit: I did as instructed and the same issue remains. I'm canceling, apple music is better when it comes to queues. improve! do better!
author: Juliane Lemes

text: This app has officially become next to useless. Not only is there more ads than there is music, now playlists won't even play. The stupid app plays one bloody song and then stops, forcing me to stop what I am doing and manually make it to play the next song in the playlist. What gives? Are you planning on locking playlists behind a bloody paywall too now? Smarten up, and for anyone wanting to use Spotify: Don't. Just don't. There are better options out there that aren't nearly as money grubbing.
author: Clockwork Couture

text: It's great, honestly. only if you have premium that is. during the peaceful days where I listened to spotify, I only have to listen to 1 ad and the duration was at least an hour to 30 minutes before another ad pops up. nowadays, I have to listen to THREE ads and sometimes when I change to another song once another ad comes. I have to wait until the ad ends for me to change to another song. i know its a way for us to buy premium but some of us would like to enjoy listening to songs peacefully
author: Shamshul Rizal

text: Everything was amazing, I found a lot of my fav songs from this app but as the time passes this app is totally changed. Now, it has many limitations for users - you can't click to choose your song, you can't skip songs as your limit is reached for a day, Ads after every 2 songs of listened and also ads is very annoying specially those rings the bell 🔔, it many times wakes me up while sleeping. 🥱😣 Not enjoying as well as before...
author: Aashish Maurya

text: I’ve been using Spotify, but it’s been frustrating lately. The app keeps saying I’m offline even when I have a strong connection. I often have to refresh it multiple times just to get it working again, which gets annoying fast. On top of that, the ads feel excessive and disruptive. I like the music selection, but the overall experience could be much smoother and more reliable.
author: Maryfran Chyanea Geronimo

text: I can’t say much, but Spotify is a popular music app that’s incredibly stingy, even basic functions like fast-forwarding, rewinding, and similar actions require a paid subscription. Plus, the ads are extremely annoying; you can’t skip them, press the skip button, or change the song while they’re playing. What’s the point of a great app that’s so stingy?
author: The Wind

text: Spotify has been a great app for me, but recently, the ads are too much—there's just too many ads. 3 ads, more like 1 minute and half every after 2 songs, isn't it too much? It was indirectly forcing us to spend money and buy a subscription. I get it, we are grateful that it's still "free" but still, this is a free music app, but lately it has been too much. I also have issues with verifying my sheer id, I can't seem to verify it even on the website. I tried every way
author: SAGUM, COURTNY GRAZEILLE J.

text: Okay I'm sick of this app. Wayyy too many ads compared to music. They fix it for a few months then it's back to a bad ratio. Its 3/4 ads every 2 songs which is just overbearing. Where is the 30 mins ad free listening they used to have? The audio quality sucks now too. Are you seriously locking the way a song literally sounds behind a pay wall? I mays well just use Youtube. Adding songs is a nightmare. It says changes are saved then I look on the playlist and none of the songs have been added.
author: Amy Buckle

text: I will give it 3 because it is usable but the free account is blocking me from listening to what I want. When I want to play from an album it only plays 1 track then switches to other artists and I need to switch back to the artists album, then after a certain amount I get blocked from what I actually wanted to listen to. I see absolutely no point in this whatsoever other than a money grab. Not good behaviour Spotify, also more adds more often, subscription is not affordable for everyone.
author: Adrian Saggin

text: Terrible shuffle system. How hard can it be to write a line of code that says: IF 'song' has played already THEN don't play 'song' again. You'll listen to a playlist, pause it for the night, and it will play in the exact same order the next day despite plenty of unplayed songs in the playlist. App rarely works in places without internet connection despite everything being downloaded. The ads on podcasts are getting a bit insane. Just raise my premium price instead of shoving it down my throat.
author: Niklas Harkonen

text: I don't know what happened but some songs can't be played anymore and every time I open a link to the album of the songs, it says the track on this release is not available. When I click on certain songs to play in my album, it immediately disappears from it after and I can't even search it anymore. Never had this problem before but this is making my experience very poor.
author: Florence Cecillia

text: Good music options (basically every single song ever - you want to listen to it, it's there) but other than that not a good music app. With free version, shuffle does not work, meaning that, even with 100 songs in a playlist, it will only play about 20 of them most of the time with the rest getting played about once ever 3 hours of listening. Adverts are a terrible problem. Want to listen to music? Listen to 3 unskippable adverts for 3 minutes that you've heard 20 times before in between tunes.
author: Tommy M.

text: Terrible slow app that constantly just hangs and freezes. It's getting worse and worse!!! It doesn't justify the premium price. Bugs, freezing when trying to perform simple actions like skipping songs or browsing playlists, shuffle randomly turning off - that's on a recent Pixel Pro. It's awful. And what's up with that huge cache size?? Where's the setting to control it? Might be time to change to AM or YT.
author: Piotr Włodarczyk

text: So, I've been using Spotify for a few years now (free plan), and it gets worse every single year. Before it was acceptable, like ads were one every 10 songs, but now it's 2 every 3 songs. And it's all about Premium! Also, randomly disconnects from Bluetooth earphones and severely crashes multiple times a day. Plus, the AI music has taken over, and it's shite. I've made a Playlist and it just keeps getting random songs in every time I open the app. I'll see you: I'm going to install Apple Music.
author: Bartol Prpić

text: On PC the free version is awsome, yes youget packs of 3 ads every 10 songs but it's still good. On the other hand, the mobile version is so bad, it literally forces you to have premium to chose which songs you want to play. Otherwise it's always on scramble, but that scramble ads the recommended songs to your queue.
author: Stephan Bassmer

text: It was an alright app, then they did some changes a couple days ago. Now it's Horsebleep. Lost all my Playlists cannot access any podcasts and get an error message "Something went wrong, have another go." ..." Try again." I have uninstalled and cleared cache and updated some many times, but the same result error message. Thanks Spotify for improving thai now cannot use. UpdatE- Now working, after 3 days and just then today reverted back to the same problems as before with the new version.
author: HammerHead

text: Leaving this app as too many chronic issues. Refuses to work as apparently 'you're offline' when I'm online and everything else is working! The last few weeks it won't play a queue of downloaded items, each time I have to connect to the Internet and restart the app several times to start the next item, then you can turn off the Internet until the next piece is due (as the item is fully downloaded) and this happens again. Also too many ads on Premium. Shocking lack of respect for paying customers
author: Ziggy Zig

text: Great App and choices to listen too, library is extensive, the only thing that really frustrates me is the number of adverts and the fact that on the free plan you are limited to the number of skips when listening. If you are like me, I never ever listen to a whole album (might be just me) but I skip around all over the place, being forced to have to listen to a whole albmum is more than annoying - but I also understand that you pay for what you get so - suck it up buttercup!
author: Emma Gillespie

text: Just one complaint with the app currently, and that's my main page being invaded by video podcasts about murderers or unsolved crimes. I have to scroll to see my own content and saved playlists etc, and I'd rather not be bombarded with media I don't want to see. I don't mind suggested playlists but a video taking up half my screen when I open the app is starting to get annoying, and no matter how many times I select 'not interested' they keep popping up! Please add a setting to turn off!
author: Dean Oxley

text: The only reason I'm still using Spotify is because there's no way to transfer my 7+ years of data to another service. Please stop pushing for AI features no one asked for and spend you resources to make a good app. And pay your artists a proper amount instead of fractions of pennies/listen. The new widget is completely useless and doesn't work, you should at the very least give us the choice to keep the old widget that was fine and worked well.
author: Nico Ducon

text: Too many ads. The only way you can actually peacefully listen to songs is by buying premium, which is too expensive. please don't download it. SoundCloud or yt music is much better. And also after a point, u can't even choose what songs you want to play. Also, music quality is low. Its all just hype. Worst music app on Earth. Its all a cash grab.
author: Trijal Reddy

text: This app is good for music quality. Before there was not many ads but now there are a plenty number of ads and I'm not able to listen to a song of my choice by clicking on the song too. It's really terrifying. Yeah I understand about the Spotify premium but there should not be much of lack of facilities. Not all can get it. We need the old spotify back with less ads and the old facilities like no Spotify premium on choosing and listening to song of your own choice. Thanks....
author: Komal Krishna

text: Big variety of songs, both genre and artists. Same with podcasts. HOWEVER, it's a need for people that don't have premium Spotify to at least have free skips (even if that means more ads, which by the way are already so many with minimum play of three songs) not to say the ability to listen to their song of choice. Many kids, students and generally adults that may not be able to pay for premium for personal reasons or anything else should be able to enjoy music. Pls fix this ASAP
author: Vasiliki Athanaselou

text: was a great app, now constantly bugging out. Unable to even play a lot of my songs in my liked songs playlist, can't even stay on the selected genre any more. Fix this issue Spotify and I'll consider re-downloading and paying again. And yes I've deleted the app and reinstalled. Edit, the issue has been resolved. Very quick tech help team. Edit again. The issue is ongoing, I've moved to SoundCloud.
author: James Archibald

text: Spotify has declined while the cost has risen. music file sizes are very small compared to things like Netflix or stan and yet the price is the same. why? I don't listen to music enough to justify paying the equivalent of a quarter of my power bill for songs. I'm a Google app developer as well and I will make it my mission to create a music app that does not cost so much. I might even make it free. I'm not in it to fleece people of thier hard earned funds like Spotify.
author: Desmond Lewis

text: I was previously using You Tube Music, but the adverts constantly interrupt and there is no background mode when you're not subscribed; Spotify is much better since the adverts are not as invasive and I can play this in the background on an unsubscribed basis. There are still periodic adverts and you are limited by how many songs you can skip, but that's an acceptable tradeoff.
author: Jordan Follos Chapman

text: Annoying Live notifications on mobile device keeps appearing and cant be close when playing on desktop. Ive blocked everything on my phone, the notifications, restricted background usage, and removed all permissions. and it still keeps running in background. also subscription is too pricy considering its only music app, it has the same price as Netflix.
author: Reyji Ferolino

text: ​Spotify is an amazing, trustworthy app for finding new music and hosting Jams with friends. However, the free version makes life hard. You get hit with constant ads, and after a few skips, it blocks you and forces a random shuffle. These annoying restrictions basically force you to pay £12.99 a month for Premium just to listen to your favorite songs in peace. Despite the high monthly cost, it is still a brilliant app that I highly recommend to everyone. Please like this review!
author: Jay Port

text: edit: that worked thanks! if you can fix how Spotify crashes when I take photos, amazing PLEASE fix how laggy and slow Spotify has become. Recently, any searches take much longer to load, and then eventually play. Liked songs aren't reliably saved in the liked playlist or in personal playlists I make. I have also noticed that songs I have liked, have become 'unliked'. This all significantly negatively impacts my user experience of the app, and whether I want to continue to pay for this service.
author: Nola Gatto

text: (Update: They finally did everything I wanted so added 2 more stars) Everything is good except for the lack of high-fidelity sound. I have Hi-Res headphones which I can't completely utilise with Spotify's 320kbps quality. Amazon Music provides me with 990kbps to 1500kbps of sound quality with 360° reality audio or Dolby Atmos for songs at the same price. Minor details matter to music a lot so might go back to Amazon Music if this is not fixed.
author: HARDIK SUTARIA

text: free version doesn't play any track you actually want to hear and is totally unusable. The desktop version even with track skipping restrictions is much better. This app is totally useless and unusable 🤣, why would I instal a music app to Not be able to play the music I want when I can just use another app that does play what I want?
author: First name Last name

text: I absolutely hate the new widget! It's loading all the time, instead of showing a recently played song, it gets back to some playlist I was listening to previously, but not the latest one. I preferred the smaller widget witch is not avaliable anymore :/ amd I know I can shrink it, but that's not the point. This one just doesn't work right
author: Ulalume

text: Both desktop and mobile App became super slow and buggy. 1. Sometimes it takes up to 10-15 seconds until I can use the app on startup (even with stable internet connection) 2. Readding liked songs (removing and adding again to move it up in the playlist) takes an eternity and is sometimes unresponsive Didn't have these kind problems 1-2 years ago. Instead of shipping ai features rather focus on keeping the app working as expected without performance regression ...
author: Mike Thiede

text: The app has become so laggy it is basically unusable now: playlist taking minutes to load, same thing for songs afterwards, and sometimes it cannot open an album at all. Today I am not even able to enable shuffle in my playlists. I used to like Spotify but I am forced to find another music platform as it just does not work anymore
author: Clément

text: it is my FAV APP .like very very favourite also my most used one. but but the ads oh God just because of that I'm giving this rating it's so annoying i know we can remove those by buying premium but could you please think about some teenagers!!but we can't pay and my parents wouldn't just do it😔 what is my intention is to keep ads but not like too annoying and also but between every song also I mean u have to cuz u can't just remove em 😅but I hope i really really hope that you could lessen
author: Elsa Anna

text: Updated the review based on a fixed resizable widget. Still delivers good choices of music, app in itself works most of the time. At times the widget just stops working or when playing via android auto, suddenly stops / crashes. AI is getting better and playing more of what I am requesting. Maybe would be nice to have an option of not seeing audiobooks / podcasts. Good that it doesnt use up much space for downloaded tunes.
author: em dee

text: As a loyal Spotify user for years, this app has easily become the ultimate soundtrack to my daily routine. The absolute best part is its incredibly smart algorithm features like Discover Weekly always introduce me to amazing new tracks that perfectly match my vibe. I also love Spotify Connect, which lets me easily switch my music from my phone to my home speakers with just one tap.
author: dinaqarmilla

text: Not sure what's happening with their servers, but it's soooo slow!! when selecting a band or podcast, it takes ages to open the songs or episodes... they should have a classic mode, lighter to work... after many years of paying premium, I'm considering uninstalling it and giving YouTube a go.
author: Gabriel Barbosa

text: Confusing and frustrating billing experience. I paid for my subscription on June 17, but was asked to pay again only 8 days later. My account had already reverted to the free version with ads, so it was unclear why I was being charged for a previous billing period and then billed again so soon. While customer support provided an explanation, the billing notifications were misleading and lacked transparency. Spotify should communicate billing issues more clearly to avoid confusing customers.
author: Jeoff Ashley Torres

text: Spotify has officially become a ragebaiting app. As a free user every time I open it, I'm bombarded with Premium ads. I can't properly choose songs and can't listen the way I want and after every few songs there are even more ads waiting for me. And it's really very much annoying 🥀. It's honestly impressive how a music app can make listening to music this annoying. If the goal is to frustrate free users until they buy Premium congratulations. It worked—I deleted the app instead.🥳
author: Ritika Kumari

text: I’ve tried other music streaming apps, but I always come back to Spotify. The curated playlists are unmatched, and it just works seamlessly on all my devices. I can play musics in phone background while scrolling text and messages. they included podcasts when I can listen while doing office work. ​
author: Zain Othman

text: Terrible customer service. I tried to subscribe twice and both attempts returned an error, yet the funds were still placed on hold by my bank. Support repeatedly told me the payments were pending and refused to activate my subscription. My bank confirmed this is the normal status before settlement and provided transaction references, but support kept repeating the same scripted response. Representatives changed constantly, no one investigated the issue, and nothing was resolved. Disappointing!
author: Ellen G

text: Great app and great that it still has a free version. What's disappointing is the hard sell repeatedly asking you the same questions on purchases they want yo to monthly subscribe. The constant harassment is unbearable. The price to subscribe is astonishing and in hard times people don't have that kind of money to flaunt. Do you want to subscribe? And now? And now and now and now. I lead by example... I stream my own music and I don't charge a single penny. I don't expect them to and never ask
author: Becky Bear1

text: I have the same issue with the other reviewer of Spotify just kept closing on me. I then uninstalled and reinstalled twice and it seemed to fix it, even though there were moments that the app kept closing on me again. Then, I tried to open it again hours later, there was only black screen with nothing on it. ny Samsung phone provided an error message that the app has bugs and that I need to get in touch with Spotify to get another update to fix it. so sad😥. please fix it...
author: Victoria L.Z

text: I really love Spotify, I love the update where you can just search up the lyrics of the song when you don't know the song title, it is very useful when you want to lock in with your assignments, projects, etc. I had Spotify since 2017 until today, thank you Spotify for your usefulness and thank you for being with me since 2017!! love yall!!
author: Loren Garcia

text: I put 5 stars cuz Spotify is a great service, beats apple music by a 100miles and running, but since like, the 2 last months I feel like your server are slower. Idk man, it might be the new wifi here but sometimes, on my computer, on my phone or on my tablet I experience latency. Maybe it's because of the wifi or multiple electronics connected simultaneously, I don't know, but I'm curious. So yeah thanks if you read all that!
author: Malik Khamis

text: It's good, you can find almost every single song in this app and the most accessible. The problem is, the ads. And the limited access you have when you don't have premium. It used to be a wonderful app but now it's just a good app. You can't listen to more than 2 songs without having to listen 30 second long ad plays. And you can't pick any songs for more than 10 times until you have to wait another day for it to reset. Same goes with skipping songs too. There's always a limit to everything.
author: Hiroshi Omaru

text: as a very grouchy person this app helps me calm down. i like screamo, personally. Anyways, the app itself I've never had a problem with, it's pretty good. though lately when I try to open the app I just get the Spotify logo and then it goes away and it's just black. I've tried everything I can think of to get it to stop, the only way to open the app nowadays is to open it, go into settings and force stop it while clearing the cache. then I can go back into the Spotify app and it'll load.
author: Efstathios Smulders

text: Everything about song quality is great but the ads literally pop up after 2-3 songs and its irritating tbh, why do you expect money for everything? does streaming the song on this platform not pay your bills? or you just need extra!? a few options being available for premiums is okay but like I can't change a song after 6 changes for 1 hour!? wth? I can't also get to a particular verse i want to listen!? this is way too much interruption! you need to stop taking advantage in the name of premium!
author: Riddhima Srivastava

text: Horrible app. Very buggy, both mobile and desktop. It is laughably badly developed, even though spotify's only product is the software... It is almost insulting. I've used it on powerful PCs and had same issues. There are too many issues to name; the most prelevant ones are: extremely slow and buggy UI, horrible cache management (went to 50gb+ very quick), randomly switching to other device, etc etc. Even the shuffle was broken for many years... A 13 year old could code a better app...
author: First name Surname

text: Fantastic app. I've been using Spotify for many years and wouldn't switch to any other music streaming service. However, recently the app has stopped working properly. Whenever I open Spotify, it gets stuck on the loading screen and eventually tells me to check my internet connection. The problem is that my internet is working perfectly fine on every other app, so the issue seems to be with Spotify itself As a Premium subscriber, this has been quite frustrating. I hope this issue can be resolve.
author: Anison Victor

text: The best music service out there. I didn't like the ads,the limited skip so i went with a year long subscription. After that,all things i hated disappeared. People want everything but don't want to pay. Well it's not how it works.☺️👍 Edit: Since like a month,Spotify is acting up on me. If i add a song to a Playlist,i have to double check to see if it's there. Sometimes,it's up to 3 times or more b4 validating. Also,how some songs i listened once became my top songs or favorites?
author: Actarus

text: OK app but offline support is terrible. When I tap the three dots next to a song it takes several seconds for the menu to open. When I listen to a podcast, minimize Spotify and open it back up it takes several seconds to show how far I'm in the episode. Sometimes the app doesn't open at all without internet connection. It really feels like you're not supposed to use the app offline.
author: Julian

text: very happy they changed the adjustment for the speed to smaller steps possible... but the sleep timer still only these fixed few options to select from :( Spotify wrapped has sooooo much potential but is used so wrong, so little, so unlogical... so sad! having premium... then still having super loud ads in "sleeping podcast" thanks for nothing ... Almost falling asleep then Bämmm..
author: Paulina Hübschmann

text: Spotify is fine but I am stuck with it as I have a family plan that others rely on. Would be great if there was safe playlists excluded from metrics? (for the kids music). new music for me should not include reissues. Spotify also tries to recommend new music that is so far outside of what I generally listen to, I get frustrated by it. needs to deal better with local files from my library when the artist is not on Spotify.
author: Ross Kelly

text: the app is great (if u pay for it), but I've had a few problems recently. the app keeps closing for no reason, coming up with a pop up that says this app has a bug, wait for the developer to sort it out, I don't understand what this means, but it keeps kicking me out when I try to either view an artists profile or view lyrics, please sort this out.
author: Faith Walder

text: If I'm talking for real, then this app is the best music player you can find in the entire play store!!! you can change the EQ as you want or with the default they give you... Sometimes I get interrupted by these ads and couple of times I had 4 to 5 ads in a row... But still I'm rating Spotify 5 stars... Because, you can't blame free cookies...
author: Fluffer Muffins

text: How the mighty have fallen! Seems to get buggier every week and makes it harder and harder to just listen to music, preferring to shove their latest "pivot to x" (podcasts, music video, audiobook, short form video) in your face instead. Writing this because it prompted me to rate it while the core functionality was down.
author: Tom Harris

text: never works idk how to fix it, ive tried uninstalling and reinstalling, clearing the cache, restarting my phone, but regardless, it doesnt work. sometimes music will play but I cant do anything cant skip a song or pause because it will crash. the music keeps playing after the app is closed. then awkwardly stops. when I try to open the app it takes forever if it even opens, idk how to fix it. I pay premium too, normally id use the Web version on my pc but I want to be able to use my phone
author: Natalia Eden-Winn

text: It was a good app, but I have become fed up with how often the ads are, and how anything actually worthwhile is behind a paywall (except for listening to local files). I downloaded 3 full playlists white the free trial was actuve and the one I didn't even fully download was the one that stayed (the two that didn't work was music, the one that stayed was a podcast). The only thing i will use this app for is to listen to the podcast and listen to my downloaded audio.
author: Liam Csermak

text: Spotify used to be a great app, but the constant restrictions on free users are becoming very frustrating. Being forced to listen to several songs before getting a skip option, along with frequent premium prompts, makes the experience annoying. I understand premium features should have benefits, but basic controls like skipping songs shouldn't feel so limited. These restrictions make it difficult to enjoy music and push users away instead of encouraging them to upgrade.Please consider improving
author: Saurabh Ravanang

text: very very very very bad experience. multiple ads, not letting us skip the song, not letting us change the song, not letting us forward the song. basically you can't do anything if you don't cough up money for them. worst experience. never downloading the app ever again. would have rated in minus if that option were available
author: Kesha Thakkar

text: Biggest miss - option to listen to ONLY new music based on likes, not previously favorited/listened tracks. If I like a track/artist/album - it doesn't mean I want to listen to it all forever, it means I want more of similar music + unknown music from the same artists. Also, Spotify alarm feature in Android is a big issue for reco, which is then highly based on the alarm tracks/playlists. Alarm tracks form suggestions in the app, they get to the top of year end review, DJ plays them ..
author: Dmitriy

text: Back in the days Spotify used to have the best algorithm with playing similar music to a persons liking, but lately I'm getting so annoyed with it as it's just so random. most frustrating part is that I have a 3 month old and for the last 3 months I've been only playing lullabies and white noise, but it still somehow manages to shift to random podcasts I've been listening to last year. I can't explain how annoying it is to have to settle down a screaming baby because it got woken up by Spotify.
author: Anja Slavkovska

text: The "Recently updated" podcasts feed doesn't work. I've performed a clean reinstall, which is the only thing the Support page (and Customer Support) advises to do. It only updates after the reinstall and then it stops. This is the main feature for me, so it's intensely frustrating. EDIT: Still the same problem, more than a year later. Spotify clearly dgaf at all.
author: Gabriela Sládková

text: Spotify is one of the best music streaming apps available today. It offers a vast collection of songs, albums, podcasts, and playlists from artists around the world. The app has a simple and user-friendly interface, making it easy to search for music, create playlists, and discover new artists. One of Spotify's strongest features is its personalized recommendations. Playlists such as Discover Weekly and Daily Mix are tailored to users' listening habits, helping them find music they might enjoy.
author: rida rafiq

text: Spotify used to feel smooth and enjoyable, but now it’s frustrating. Ads are too frequent and ruin the mood, especially when you’re getting into a song. It feels more like interruptions than music. Limited skips and restricted controls make it worse, as you can’t freely choose what to play. It starts to feel like you’re being pushed toward Premium. While the music library is still great, the free experience has become too restricted and ad-heavy to enjoy.
author: Akshay Pradeep

text: absolutely terrible. the free version is just so bad you cant event listen to your own songs anymore. whenever i listen to my playlist it never goes in order and this stupid "Listen to any song u want with premium" is so stupid. its a music app. let me listen to what i want, when i want and what i wanna skip when i want to. Stop. being. greedy.
author: Kärol A

text: it always says six skips per HOUR, then says it will give more the next DAY. Like what??? plus why can't I choose what to listen to? like I chose one song off of my massive playlist after listening to another, and it tells me I have to wait until tomorrow. Don't bother with this app, use yt music. at least there you can actually skip and choose what to listen too.
author: Kiera Dallyn

text: It's a decent music app that I use for several hours a day. The problem is that shuffled songs in a playlist of 8000 often repeat in the exact same order several times. There's also an issue where I can't add songs or podcasts to the queue anymore. Sometimes my queue completely disappears. Sometimes Spotify won't let me choose my songs too (it'll say I'm not premium even though my profile says premium)
author: Jendon Anthony

text: was loving the app but for some reason. I pay for a premium family plan, and I keep getting ads that I'm paying not to get. getting tired of it and getting real close to not paying for the service anymore. also whenever I get an ad. it says ads in beta. I didn't sign up for a beta nor pay for me to be in one. even after updating the app, still getting ads. I'm not paying for.
author: Logan

text: Free version: really bad music quality, ad after and ad, to a point where you can't use the app without constant frustration. Paid version: Music gets slightly bit better, but it's still mp3, absolutely not worth the price. Music library is really good and the Spotify created playlists are pretty well done. Overall, free version is horrible and unusable. Paid version gets better, with great music library, but terrible quality, which, for the price, is an absolute shame.
author: Virtual World

text: Hello, In general, Spotify is great, but as of the recent update, the Spotify app has a bug, and shuts down mid song. It has crashed more than 10 times to the point my phone wants to put the app to sleep. I got a notification that the app has a bug. If it can be fixed that would appreciated. Thank you so much.
author: Beloved Elizabeth Adonai

text: the most annoying app ever. utterly disgusting. despite having premium subscription, this app does not work properly at all. so much lagging and a disgusting amount of bugs. it takes forever to load even when the songs are already downloaded and sometimes takes a minute or two to play the song you click on. not to mention how the songs don't even get saved to your playlist properly. some get saved, but some require you to add it multiple times. this app is ridiculously disappointing.

Fix it Edit: Problems still persist. Unable to skip songs with voice commands because it causes deaync now. Zero improvement!!!

text: ​I am beyond frustrated with this app. It is genuinely one of the worst music experiences I’ve ever had. ​If you actually want to listen to a specific song, forget about it. The app forces you into "shuffle mode," meaning it just plays random songs instead of what you actually selected. It completely defeats the purpose of having a music library. ​To make matters worse, the ads are relentless. author: Kushal Srivastav

text: Spotify Premium has become very unstable for me. The app often lags, crashes, and stops playback even while open in the background. Offline downloads are unreliable and sometimes buffer or take long to start, defeating the purpose of offline listening. There are delays when switching playlists or selecting songs. I expected better, and YouTube Music Premium actually performs more reliably in comparison. Overall, very disappointing for a paid service.

text: The free version is unusable, every 3 to 1 songs 2 ads every ad 30 seconds . That is terrible for average user . author: Aditya Patel
